Release build fetches libmpv dev archive from mutable GitHub release URL, no SHA256, then code-signs and ships. ci.yml:74-95 pins SHA256 — mirror that in release.yml, fail on mismatch, include content hash in actions/cache key (URL-keyed cache at 96-99 can serve poisoned content).

title: ensure_init holds inner mutex across main-thread round-trip + mpv init (12s freeze, deadlock window)
description: src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs:318-459. inner.lock() held across run_on_main_thread + rx.recv() + full Mpv::with_initializer (~12s hwdec probe). Blocking inner.lock() on main thread (Focused handler lib.rs:700, CloseRequested lib.rs:727, fullscreen early-sync) stalls Win32 pump for entire init; if it blocks while ensure_init parked in rx.recv(), queued closure never runs -> permanent deadlock. Fix: double-checked init — lock only to check is_some(), release, guard re-entry with AtomicBool, build host+mpv unlocked, re-lock briefly to store Inner.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] HostWindow thread affinity deadlocks main thread after fullscreen
description:
## Root cause (proven by logs 2026-04-19 23:12:30)

HostWindow's HWND is created inside `ensure_init`, which is called from `#[tauri::command] async fn player_load_url`. Async Tauri commands run on a tokio worker thread, so the HWND is thread-affine to that tokio worker.

Win32 SetWindowPos called cross-thread does SendMessage to the owner thread and waits for the owner to dispatch it. Tokio workers don't pump Win32 messages, so the call blocks indefinitely.

During normal drag-resize this is sporadically OK (the worker thread occasionally gets awakened by tokio activity that happens to service the queue), but the fullscreen path in `player_set_fullscreen`'s `run_on_main_thread` closure deadlocks reliably: the main thread goes into SetWindowPos and never returns. Evidence: `set_geometry` logs fire, but the follow-up `sync_geometry returned` log never does.

With the main thread stuck, Tauri's IPC handler stops processing. When React's cleanup invokes `player_unload`, the command queues but never reaches the backend — explaining the 'audio continues 20s, no [player:cmd] unload in log' report.

## Fix
Own the HostWindow on the main thread. `ensure_init` dispatches HostWindow creation via `app.run_on_main_thread` and a one-shot channel to wait for the result. `destroy` dispatches DestroyWindow the same way. SetWindowPos from any thread to a main-thread-owned window works cleanly because main always pumps messages.

## Acceptance
- Toggle fullscreen, click back → audio cuts immediately and `[player:cmd] unload` appears in log
- Drag-resize still works (same behavior as today)
- No 'cursor is loading spinner' regression

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: main window unresponsive on first Player load (regression)
description:
## Symptom
After my 3 commits (252b631, d2349ee, fee7d2e), launching the app and navigating to the Player:
- Video plays (mpv renders, audio audible)
- Tauri main window does not accept focus
- Windows shows the busy/wait cursor (IDC_APPSTARTING / IDC_WAIT) over the app
- Keyboard input goes to the terminal, not the app (Esc cancels shell input)
- Only escape hatch is Ctrl+C on the terminal

Logs confirm:
- FileLoaded → player://ready fires normally
- time-pos progresses (video is decoding)
- No errors or warnings from player subsystems
- No set_fullscreen command ever fires — user can't trigger it

## Suspected causes (in order of likelihood)

1. **252b631 (destroy joins event pump)**: handle.join() blocks the calling thread. If something during the StrictMode double-mount interleaves destroy() with a cross-thread SetWindowPos from the Tauri UI thread targeting our host HWND (owned by a tokio worker), main would SendMessage-block while the worker is blocked on join(). But destroy should be a no-op on first load (Inner=None) so this shouldn't actually fire.
2. **d2349ee (WM_SIZE → async child resize)**: new WndProc logic that runs whenever WM_SIZE hits our host. The SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS flag should make it non-blocking, but re-entrancy or an unforeseen message path could stall something.
3. **fee7d2e (cleanup chain)**: cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. A fire-and-forget Promise chain in useEffect cleanup shouldn't block the JS thread, and the Rust side is independent.

## Next step
Revert all 3 commits, rebuild, retest. If the hang disappears, bisect to find the specific regression.

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. u4c: HostWindow now owned on main thread (f5bc7bc) — fullscreen + un-fullscreen confirmed working. 31n: suspected commits 252b631/d2349ee/fee7d2e were reverted; safer re-application landed in 6f937e1 and HostWindow main-thread fix; user confirmed nav/play/pause work without unresponsive window.

title: [bug] Native player: mpv D3D11 swapchain doesn't resize on fullscreen
description:
## Root cause

The host HWND correctly resizes to display dimensions (3840x2160) during fullscreen — confirmed in commit 8196e41 and the beads memory. `SetWindowPos` in `HostWindow::set_geometry` succeeds. But mpv's internal D3D11 swapchain stays at the pre-fullscreen size.

In `src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs:30-37`, the WndProc is just `DefWindowProcW`. mpv creates a child render surface inside our HWND (confirmed by earlier working fix at commit b261182, which found a child via `GetWindow(hwnd, GW_CHILD)`). When the parent HWND resizes, WM_SIZE fires on the parent, but child windows in Win32 do NOT auto-resize — the parent must explicitly move children. Our WndProc swallows WM_SIZE into DefWindowProcW with no child handling.

Previous fix b261182 added WM_SIZE → MoveWindow on GW_CHILD inside our WndProc. This was reverted in 30a4949 because "SetWindowPos on mpv's child triggered a D3D11 swapchain rebuild that blocked the main thread". The PostMessage variant (`post_resize` in older code) was also removed as dead code in 193904c.

## Fix options (in order of preference)

**Option A — non-blocking child resize via SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + bRepaint=false:**
Re-add WM_SIZE handler in `wnd_proc` that resizes GW_CHILD with `MoveWindow(child, 0, 0, w, h, false /* bRepaint */)` or `SetWindowPos(..., SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS | SWP_NOREDRAW)`. The async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread's queue instead of sending synchronously, avoiding the main-thread block that motivated 30a4949.

**Option B — tell mpv to reconfigure:**
After sync_geometry applies a large jump (post-fullscreen), invoke an mpv command like `mpv.set_property("window-scale", 1.0)` or similar to force it to re-read window size. Lower risk of deadlock but depends on whether libmpv exposes a reconfigure trigger we can call off-thread.

**Option C — subclass + explicit swapchain size:**
Use `SetWindowSubclass` on mpv's child HWND to intercept its events. More invasive; only if A/B fail.

## Test plan
- Enter fullscreen during playback: video fills the entire screen (not just the pre-fullscreen region)
- Exit fullscreen: video returns to the window region
- Enter→exit→enter cycle: each transition correctly resizes (memory note says 2nd play has issues)
- Drag-resize the window: video follows smoothly
- No main-thread freeze during transition

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication

## History
- b261182 added child resize — worked but blocked main thread
- 0e0eda8 tried SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS — still flaky
- 30a4949 removed child resize entirely — current state, video doesn't resize
- 193904c deleted the SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS constant + post_resize as dead code
close_reason: WndProc now handles WM_SIZE and resizes GW_CHILD with SWP_ASYNCWINDOWPOS + NOREDRAW flags. Async flag posts the resize to mpv's thread queue, avoiding the main-thread block that killed the earlier sync attempt. Commit d2349ee.

title: [bug] Native player: drop-order race crashes mpv on destroy
description:
## Root cause

In `src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs`, `Inner` declares fields in order: `mpv: Arc<Mpv>` first, `host: HostWindow` second. Rust drops struct fields in declaration order, so when `destroy()` runs `*guard = None;`:

1. `Arc<Mpv>` drops → refcount 2→1 (event pump thread still holds the other Arc). `mpv_terminate_destroy` is NOT called yet.
2. `HostWindow` drops → `DestroyWindow` runs on the HWND that mpv's render thread is still using.
3. Much later, the event pump's `wait_event(1.0)` returns `Event::Shutdown`, loop breaks, its Arc drops (1→0), `mpv_terminate_destroy` finally runs — but the HWND is already gone.

The window between step 2 and step 3 is the crash window: mpv's D3D11 render thread tries to present to a destroyed HWND. Verified against libmpv2 source at `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-.../libmpv2-4.1.0/src/mpv.rs:465-468` (Drop calls `mpv_terminate_destroy`).

The existing code comment on `Inner.host` (mod.rs:56-61) is aspirational — the field order is correct in intent but drops aren't synchronous because another Arc is live.

## Also explains: audio keeps playing after navigating to dashboard

`player_unload` returns to JS as soon as `destroy()` returns — which is BEFORE mpv has actually terminated. mpv's audio output keeps feeding the device until the event pump processes Shutdown (up to ~1s due to the `wait_event(1.0)` timeout plus whatever mpv takes internally).

## Fix

Store the event pump's `JoinHandle` on `Inner`. In `destroy()`:
1. Send `stop` + `quit` to mpv.
2. Drop/replace the Arc held on the main side first (via `take()` + `drop()`).
3. `join()` the event pump thread — this returns after it drops its Arc.
4. NOW both Arcs are gone → `mpv_terminate_destroy` ran synchronously during the final Arc drop.
5. `HostWindow` drops cleanly.

After this, `player_unload` only returns to JS once mpv is fully dead + HWND destroyed. Audio stops before navigation happens.

## Acceptance
- Navigate to dashboard while playing: audio stops within one frame
- Close app while playing: no crash in logs
- Repeated play→dashboard→play cycles: no zombie mpv state

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Found during thorough app review 2026-04-19. Related to beads memory 'native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed'.
close_reason: Event pump JoinHandle stored in Inner; destroy() takes Inner out, sends stop+quit, joins pump, then drops. Final Arc drop runs mpv_terminate_destroy synchronously before HostWindow::drop. Commit 252b631.

title: linux-test-plan I.1: Dashboard takes 5+ s to fully render when returning from a library view
description:
## Bug — linux-test-plan I.1 (semi-pass)

Navigation between pages is generally fast, but going from a **library view → Home** takes **5+ seconds** for the full dashboard to render. Other routes are fine (I.2/I.3 pass), so this is Dashboard-specific, not the spinner-scoping regression.

## Expected

Dashboard re-entry from a library view paints from cache near-instantly (same class of behavior as J.2's 30s-TTL instant detail render), with hub refresh happening in the background.

## Suggested skills

- react:performance, react:hooks, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Notes

Likely candidates: dashboard hub queries refetch-on-mount without a warm cache, a request waterfall (sections → hubs → per-hub items), or the library view's in-flight/full-section requests competing with dashboard fetches (test plan K.2 covers abort-on-navigate — verify it actually cancels). Reproduce with the network panel open and log timestamps: `[splash] section settled` lines on boot show the deck/movies/shows staging already exists — check what re-runs on re-entry.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05)

- `src/hooks/useDashboard.ts` (33–240): seeds state synchronously from cache (43–59) but **always re-runs all three fetch effects on every mount** (`fetchMovies`/`fetchShows`/`fetchDeck`, 149–185) — `getRecentlyAddedBySection` iterates every movie/show section (limit 30 each) plus `getOnDeck`. Cache keys `dashboard:{uri}:movies|shows|deck`, TTL 60 min (26–31).
- `src/services/api-cache.ts` — custom TTL cache (`Map` + optional localStorage), not react-query. If seeding works, the 5 s should be revalidation competing for the main thread / network, or the seed path not actually hitting (verify `cacheGet` hits on re-entry).
- `src/pages/Dashboard.tsx` (1–42): also mounts `HeroSlideshow` (325–347), `useMediaContextMenu`, `usePlayAction`, `useDetailPrefetch`, `useParentalControls`, `useScrollRestoration` per entry.
- Also check the library view abandons its in-flight page fetches on navigate (`usePaginatedLibrary` abort) so they don't starve the dashboard's requests.


## Required Skills
react:performance,react:hooks,typescript:typescript,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: Library alpha scrubber: letter jump requires many clicks (scrollToIndex clamps to loaded range)
description:
## Bug — alphabet scrubber: letter jump takes many clicks

Clicking a letter on the library alphabet scrubber computes the correct target but the grid does not land on the first row of that letter on the first click. Session logs show ~20 repeats of the same jump while the user hammered 'N':

```
[library:scrubber] alpha jump (firstChar) {"letter":"N","offset":2020}
```

The offset (2020) is stable and presumably correct — the scroll just doesn't take.

## Expected

**One click** on a letter scrolls the grid to the first row whose first item starts with that letter (or the `#` bucket for non-alpha). Works even when the target range hasn't been fetched yet — jump first, fetch the now-visible range after (see the paged-fetching task this depends on).

## Suggested skills

- react:performance, react:hooks, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Notes

Almost certainly the same root cause as the fast-scroll task: scrollToOffset/scrollToIndex into a region the virtualizer doesn't have measured/fetched rows for is a no-op or gets clamped, and repeated clicks only creep forward as items load. Blocked-by dependency on the paged-fetch task is set — fix the geometry model there, then this may reduce to converting the item offset into a row index and calling scrollToIndex once. Add a regression test: jump to an unfetched letter range lands within one row of the bucket start.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05) — root cause confirmed

- `src/pages/LibraryView.tsx` `handleAlphaJump` (276–303): `letterOffsets.get(letter)` → `gridApiRef.current?.scrollToIndex(offset)` (278–286; the repeated log line is 284). Offsets from `useFirstCharacter`/`computeLetterOffsets` (`src/hooks/useFirstCharacter.ts` 40–126, 139–149) are cumulative indices into the FULL sorted section — correct.
- `src/components/VirtualizedLibraryGrid.tsx` `scrollToIndex` handle (169–212): virtualized branch computes `rowIndex = floor(index/cols)` and calls `virtualizer.scrollToIndex` across 3 RAF ticks (190–202) — but the virtualizer `count` spans only LOADED items, so index 2020 clamps to the end of the ~50 loaded; non-virtualized branch `querySelector('[data-grid-index]')` finds nothing → no scroll (205–208).
- Each clamped partial scroll trips the bottom sentinel → +50 items → next click creeps further. That is exactly the observed multi-click behavior.
- After the paged-fetch task fixes geometry (count from `totalSize`), this likely reduces to one `scrollToIndex(floor(offset/cols))` plus triggering the range fetch for the landing viewport.


## Required Skills
react:performance,react:hooks,typescript:typescript,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: Library grid: fast scroll impossible — paged/windowed fetching with fixed totalSize geometry (Plex-style)
description:
## Bug/perf — library grid: fast scrolling is impossible

In a large library view (~2000+ items per the scrubber offsets), scrolling quickly is effectively impossible. Symptom pattern matches fetch-on-scroll blocking: the grid can't move past content that hasn't been fetched yet, so a fast fling stalls.

## Expected — Plex-style paged/windowed browsing

Target behavior mirrors the official Plex app: you can jump to the very bottom of the library **instantly** (scrollbar/scrubber never blocked by fetches); rows render as placeholders and posters/metadata lazy-load a few seconds later for whatever range is visible.

That implies:
- The grid's total height/row count is known up front from the section's `totalSize` (cheap `size=0` request) — scroll geometry must never depend on fetched items.
- Item data fetched in pages (`X-Plex-Container-Start`/`X-Plex-Container-Size`) keyed by visible range, with in-flight de-dup, abort of ranges scrolled past, and a small overscan.
- Unfetched rows render skeleton cards, replaced in place when their page lands (no layout shift).

## Suggested skills

- react:performance (virtualization), react:hooks, typescript:typescript, prexu:prexu (Plex container paging), testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Notes

The prexu-0szx.7 virtualization work (K.3/K.5 pass for playlists/collections) is the foundation — the gap is the fetch strategy for the main section grid. This is the epic's anchor task: the alpha-scrubber fix depends on its range-fetch design.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05)

- `src/hooks/usePaginatedLibrary.ts` — `PAGE_SIZE = 50` (8), `BG_BATCH_SIZE = 200` (10), `LOAD_ALL_CONCURRENCY = 4` (14); single-page path (228–242); `loadAll` background-fill path (48–122, 183–227); `loadMore` (262–293, one request at a time via `loadingRef`).
- `src/pages/LibraryView.tsx` — `shouldLoadAll = isAlphaSortable && hasActiveFilters` (160–161): the DEFAULT view (alpha sort, no filters) is `loadAll=false` → 50 items + IntersectionObserver bottom-sentinel `loadMore` (456–472). Each fling stalls on a 50-item round trip.
- `src/components/VirtualizedLibraryGrid.tsx` — `@tanstack/react-virtual` `useVirtualizer` (27, 151–161); `virtualizeThreshold = 100` (falls back to CSS grid under 100 items); **`count = rows.length` from loaded items, not `totalSize`** (152) — this is the architectural gap: scroll geometry grows with fetches instead of being fixed up front.
- Fix shape: size the virtualizer from the section `totalSize` (known from the container response / a `size=0` probe), fetch pages keyed to the visible range (with overscan, in-flight de-dup, abort on ranges scrolled past), render skeleton cards for unfetched indices, keep `PAGE_SIZE` tuned (50 may be fine per-range). The sparse item store replaces the append-only array.


## Required Skills
react:performance,react:hooks,typescript:typescript,prexu:prexu,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: Linux hardware test-plan follow-ups (2026-07-05 Wayland pass)
description:
# Linux hardware test-plan follow-ups (2026-07-05 Wayland pass)

Source: user ran `docs/linux-on-hardware-test-plan.md` on real hardware (Wayland session, native mpv player active, v0.7.1 dev build).

## Results summary

**Passed:** A1-3, B, C1-3, D1-4, G1-5, H1-3, I2-3, J2-5, K1-6, L1-2.

**Failed / degraded (filed as children of this epic):**
- E.2/E.3 — popout opens near-fullscreen instead of shrinking, and cannot be resized (Wayland)
- I.1 — navigating from a library view to Home takes 5+ seconds to fully render the dashboard
- J.1 — ItemDetail first paints with prefetched data (poster, cast) then visibly refreshes; related items/trailers load after the flash
- Continue Watching card label shows a stale resume timestamp after a playback session (actual resume position is correct); related: dashboard progress bars only update on refresh/revisit (L note)
- Season cards on show detail have no app context menu — right-click shows the WebKit default menu (screenshot confirmed)
- Library grid: fast scrolling is impossible — appears to fetch-on-scroll; needs paged/windowed fetching Plex-style (jump instantly, posters lazy-load)
- Alphabet scrubber: letter jump computes the right offset (logs: `alpha jump (firstChar) letter=N offset=2020` repeated ~20x) but the view does not land there on first click

**Enhancement (K note):** advanced filtering — only show filter values that return results, cross-filter the filter options against already-set filters, year ranges instead of single year.

**Skipped:** F (X11) — permanently descoped, Wayland-only testing policy (prexu-5jxx deferred). M and N deferred to the Downloads page epic (prexu-5vc).

## Team

Team lead: **opus** — orchestrates only, does not implement. Spawn one member per task; each member is labeled with its model below. Escalation on two failures: haiku → sonnet → opus (collect the failed member's summary and respawn with it).

| Member | Model | Tasks | Skills |
|--------|-------|-------|--------|
| library-perf | sonnet | paged/windowed library fetching; alpha scrubber landing | react:performance, react:hooks, typescript:typescript, prexu:prexu, testing:vitest |
| ui-perf | sonnet | Dashboard 5s render; ItemDetail refresh flash | react:performance, react:hooks, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest |
| desktop-shell | sonnet | popout Wayland sizing/resizability | rust:rust, tauri:tauri, tauri:commands |
| ui-features | haiku | season context menu; Continue Watching staleness | react:components, react:hooks, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest |
| filters | sonnet | advanced filtering design + implementation | react:components, typescript:typescript, prexu:prexu, testing:vitest |
| validation | haiku | strictest lint/type/test pass per PR: `npx vitest run`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, `cd src-tauri && cargo check` | testing:vitest, typescript:typescript |

All members must load core skills first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git, /core:documentation, /core:security, /core:mise, /core:nushell. Code without tests is not complete (/core:tdd). All members work items via the claude task-list tool mirrored from bd (`[bd:ID]` subject prefix).

## Workflow

One feature branch for the epic (`feature/linux-test-followups`), one PR per task or coherent task group, squash merge, no attribution anywhere. Validation member runs after each member's change; failures loop back to the owning member.

title: perf(proxy): stream local-file + proxied responses — range path buffers file tail into RAM (OOM-class)
description: TWO buffering defects in src-tauri/src/lib.rs proxy thread. (1) /local/{ratingKey} range path (~:255-280): end = file_size-1 IGNORES the client's requested range end, then vec![0u8; length] + read_exact reads EVERYTHING from seek offset to EOF into one allocation — a seek near the start of a multi-GB remux allocates gigabytes and can OOM; full-file path does read_to_end similarly. (2) Plex-proxy path (:339): resp.bytes() buffers the ENTIRE upstream response before writing — every HLS segment pays full-download latency before the first byte reaches the webview, contradicting the module doc's 'streams the response back'. FIX: honor requested range end; stream both paths with io::copy over a bounded reader (file.take(length) / resp as Read) using a fixed 64-256KB buffer; take Content-Length from computed length / upstream header. Bonus (same file ~:216): per-header-line to_lowercase() allocation -> eq_ignore_ascii_case slice check.
close_reason: Merged in PR #43 (3e51ede): streaming proxy for local-file + proxied responses

title: perf(bundle): hls.js (~542KB, 54% of entry chunk) ships on every launch — make imports type-only + lazy
description: src/services/tauri-loader.ts:18 imports LoadStats from hls.js as a RUNTIME value (used to seed this.stats), and tauri-loader is statically reachable from plex-playback.ts:8 -> player hooks -> entry graph. Since useHlsLoader.ts:43 ALSO dynamic-imports hls.js, Rollup hoists the whole 542KB library into the entry chunk (verified: hls demuxer fingerprints present in dist index-*.js = 998KB, absent from lazy Player chunk). ~500KB of parse/compile on every launch, pure waste on the native-mpv default path. FIX: replace LoadStats with a plain object literal satisfying LoaderStats (or inject the Hls ctor from the dynamic-import caller), convert all hls.js imports to import type, confirm entry drops to ~450KB. Also consider vite build.target for the fixed WebView baseline + explicit manualChunks entries so this can't regress silently (chunkSizeWarningLimit already fires). Verify with a prod build size diff.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #42: LoadStats replaced with a zeroed LoaderStats literal; all hls.js imports type-only. Measured: Player chunk 675KB -> 153KB; hls.js is its own async chunk loaded only when the HTML5 engine mounts; Vite hoist warning gone. (Correction vs the audit text: in the current graph hls.js was hoisted into the lazy Player chunk, not the entry chunk — same defect, cost was per player open on the native path.)

title: Linux: mini-player expand does not restore full player — Rust clears margins but React stays minimized
description: On-hardware axj4.5 testing (2026-07-03 ~22:23): user dragged mini player across corners (worked), then clicked expand — player_exit_minimize ran 3x on the Rust side (margins cleared to 0 each time, mpv back to full surface) but the React UI stayed in minimize mode: AppLayout kept the mini corner hole (screenshot shows slice of fullscreen video through the stale top-right hole). restoreFromMinimize logged 'restoring' each click; neither the .then (setIsMinimized(false)) nor the .catch produced observable effect — no 'exit failed' logged. Static analysis exhausted: context memo deps include isMinimized, IPC effect can't re-enter without logging, wiring direct. Instrumented: restore .then now logs before flipping; Linux exit_minimize now emits host-window-busy/ready (parity with enter + Windows resync_host — real gap regardless of this bug). Next repro will bisect: 'restore IPC done' log present -> render/state issue; absent -> invoke promise never resolves (response lost). Note: mid-incident the tauri watcher relaunched the app (branch switch), so post-incident state was destroyed.
notes:
SECOND REPRO WITH INSTRUMENTATION (22:35): smoking gun captured. Expand click 22:35:34 — Rust command completed fully (clear_margins + NEW busy/ready pair fired; useTransparentWindow logged transition-busy/ready — parity fix works). But 'restore IPC done' did NOT log then: it logged TWICE at 22:36:01, the exact moment the user exited the player (detach removed the 60Hz pump). => The tauri invoke RESPONSES were stuck for 27s and flushed when rendering stopped. Mechanism hypothesis: wry delivers custom-protocol IPC responses via MainContext::default().invoke() from the tokio thread (src-tauri/vendor/wry/src/webkitgtk/web_context.rs:221); while mpv's 60Hz pump + full-surface rendering saturates the GTK main loop / webkit scheme plumbing, response delivery starves; teardown stops the pump and both queued responses flush. Open questions: (a) why only exit_minimize responses observably starve (drag geometry updates at 22:23 seemed fine — but their .then is only .catch-logged, so silent starvation possible); (b) NEW SYMPTOM: after clear_margins with playback running, VIDEO rendered BLACK full-viewport while audio played and SUBTITLES rendered (OSD fine, video layer black) — unexplained, possibly mpv video-margin-ratio 0-restore quirk with nvdec, needs mpv-side verbose logs or targeted repro. Next: controlled repro — enter mini, ONE expand click, no exit, watch log live for whether/when restore IPC done arrives spontaneously; capture screenshot of black state + eu-stack of both processes while stalled.
ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED via live stacks on a held stall (22:40): GTK main thread parked in mpv_render_context_render -> UNTIMED mp_cond_wait(ctx->video_wait) waiting 'while (wait_present_count > ctx->present_count)' (mpv vo_libmpv.c); present_count only advances in the vo thread's flip_page; vo thread + mpv core both idle in timed waits. Circular deadlock: vo waits for the render client to consume its frame, render waits for the PREVIOUS frame's present. Triggered by the video-margin-ratio writes of exit_minimize during playback (enter/drag writes never triggered it in testing; exit did 4/4 times). Main loop frozen => wry MainContext::invoke IPC responses starved (busy/ready EVENTS still delivered — they ride evaluate_script before the block) => invoke promise pending => React never flips. USER-VERIFIED: pressing pause (any core wakeup) broke the cycle and everything flushed. FIX APPLIED: vendored libmpv2 4.1.0 (src-tauri/vendor/libmpv2, [patch.crates-io]) adding RenderContext::render_no_block (MPV_RENDER_PARAM_BLOCK_FOR_TARGET_TIME=0); linux_compositor render handler now uses it — the GTK main thread can never park in that wait, breaking the deadlock structurally. Windows keeps stock blocking render. cargo check passes. AWAITING RETEST: minimize -> expand should restore immediately ('restore IPC done' right after 'restoring'), video should stay live (no black), IPC snappy throughout.
close_reason: Fixed and verified on hardware 2026-07-03: circular render/vo deadlock in mpv's block-for-target-time wait, triggered by exit_minimize's video-margin-ratio writes mid-playback. Fix: vendored libmpv2 4.1.0 adding RenderContext::render_no_block (BLOCK_FOR_TARGET_TIME=0), used by the Linux compositor render handler; plus busy/ready parity on Linux exit_minimize. User verified: expand restores immediately, video stays live; M7 (stop while mini -> replay full-frame) and M8 (fullscreen interplay after minimize) both pass. Remaining visual staging tracked in prexu-hg1j.

title: Linux: stop button — user-observed 'stuck in teardown / never stops' despite logs showing clean exit
description: On-hardware axj4.5 testing (2026-07-03): user clicked stop in PlayerControls and reports the player appears stuck in teardown and never actually stopping. Log evidence contradicts a Rust-side hang: handleExit start -> player_unload -> destroy (render context freed, pump joined 0ms, destroy:bg dropping Inner strong_count=1) -> playerSession.stop -> overlay unmount cleanup logs -> stopped timeline beacon delivered, all within ~1s. Two open leads: (1) lifecycle.rs spawn_teardown_task logs BEFORE dropping Inner and nothing after, so a hang inside mpv_terminate_destroy (e.g. audio/pipewire teardown) is invisible in logs — add a 'destroy:bg Inner dropped' completion log to make this observable; (2) what the user actually sees on screen (frozen last frame? navy? unresponsive UI?) is not yet captured — needs their observation + any log lines after 20:48:17. Blocked on that repro detail.
notes:
ROOT CAUSE FOUND (2026-07-03, live eu-stack on frozen repro, pid 189033/webproc 189078):
NOT a Rust/mpv teardown hang. On the frozen instance: prexu main thread idle in gtk_main_iteration_do/ppoll; teardown thread already exited; no mpv threads left (only an orphaned idle CUDA worker). destroy/mpv_terminate_destroy completed fine.
The freeze is a WebKitWebProcess (webkit2gtk4.1-2.52.3-1.fc43) main-thread LIVELOCK at ~90-100% CPU inside CSS transition retargeting during style resolution — repeated samples all inside: WebCore::updateCSSTransitionsForStyleableAndProperty / Styleable::updateCSSTransitions / KeyframeEffect::apply / KeyframeInterpolation::interpolateKeyframes under Style::TreeResolver::resolveComposedTree <- Document::updateLayout <- Page::updateRendering <- LayerTreeHost::updateRendering. JS/event loop fully starved (no webview logs, no input, last composited frame stays on screen). VmRSS grows ~35MB/s while frozen (2.9GB->3.0GB in 3s) — transition objects accumulating each cycle; will OOM eventually.
Onset correlates with exit path: prepareNavAway removes body.player-transparent (full-tree recalc) then Player overlay unmounts; last JS log (stopped beacon) ~2s after class removal, then livelock. Suspects: (a) transition-retargeting storm from the body class flip cascading var()-dependent styles, (b) dashboard refetch after stopped-beacon mounting infinite-animation shimmer skeletons (.shimmer styles.css:138) + transitions, (c) transition:all sites (SessionCreator.tsx:411, AccountSettingsPanel.tsx:383). No matching known upstream WebKit bug found in a first search.
Next step: diagnostic bisect — global '*{transition:none!important;animation:none!important}' armed during exit (mirroring the reduced-motion block styles.css:375) to confirm trigger class, then narrow to the culprit element. Full stack dumps captured in session scratchpad (frozen-stacks.txt, webproc-stacks.txt).
SECOND REPRO (21:45): kill switch armed->disarmed cleanly, freeze began AT DISARM — the re-enabling class flip retriggered the same storm (5 stack samples: 4x updateCSSTransitionsForStyleableAndProperty/KeyframeEffect::apply, 1x IntersectionObserver checking isRunningAcceleratedTransformRelatedAnimation). CULPRIT IDENTIFIED: PosterCard root combines (1) className=card-enter -> 'animation: cardEnter 0.35s ease-out both' where cardEnter animates TRANSFORM and 'both' forward-fill keeps the finished animation permanently in the effect stack, (2) transition: transform 0.15s ease (PosterCard styles.card), (3) inline hover transform. Hundreds of cards => any document-wide class flip (player-transparent removal, kill-switch disarm) forces createAnimatedElementUpdate + transform-transition retargeting against a filling animation on every card, and webkit2gtk 2.52.3 livelocks (also explains the 20s splash hard-cap at startup: same storm while shimmer skeletons+cards mount, self-resolves when JS isn't starved). FIX APPLIED (working tree): .card-enter fill both->backwards (stagger only needs backwards; 'to' == natural style so visually identical). Diagnostic exit motion-kill switch left in place for the retest; remove it (or keep as belt-and-suspenders with a comment) once the fill-mode fix is confirmed. tsc + 1777 vitest pass. Awaiting on-hardware retest.
ROOT CAUSE #2 (the actual user-visible freeze; CSS storm was a co-occurring second bug): detach_mpv dropped the mpv RenderContext on the main thread WITHOUT gl_area.make_current() — the mpv render API requires the creating GL context to be current for ALL mpv_render_* calls including mpv_render_context_free. The free therefore ran against no context / GDK's own paint context, deleting mpv's GL objects inside GDK's state and corrupting GTK's toplevel compositing: the window stops committing buffers, frozen on last composite, black after resize, while every thread stays healthy/idle (verified: third freeze had prexu main + WebKitWebProcess both idle-polling, JS alive — a Vite forced reload rebooted the app under the frozen glass, invisible to the user; window black at new size after user resize). Evidence: create path makes context current (try_create_render_context line ~828), detach didn't. FIX: make_current (realized-gated, error-logged) before the take/drop in detach_mpv. Also fixed splash hard-cap false warning (App.tsx dismissIfQuorum now clears the timer — it fired+warned on every boot even after quorum dismissal, which earlier misled diagnosis). cargo check + tsc + 1777 vitest pass. Awaiting repro of play->stop on hardware.
close_reason: Fixed and verified on hardware 2026-07-03: two root causes — (1) detach_mpv freed the mpv render context without gl_area.make_current(), corrupting GDK's GL/compositing state (window stopped committing buffers → frozen on last frame, black after resize); (2) card-enter 'both' fill kept finished transform animations permanently active on every poster card, livelocking webkit2gtk 2.52 in CSS transition retargeting on any document-wide class flip. User verified two consecutive play->stop cycles (button + Esc) with no freeze.

title: Linux: reveal-mute gap remains — host-window-ready fires on PlaybackRestart, ~0.5-1s before first GLArea frame
description: On-hardware axj4.5 testing (2026-07-03): ~0.5-1s of navy background with audible audio before video appears on load. Root cause: events.rs emits player://host-window-ready on first PlaybackRestart per file (events.rs ~line 638), which on the Windows HWND path coincides with a composited frame, but on the Linux render-API path mpv composites nothing itself — the GtkGLArea render callback does, and the render context is attached around the same time. So both the body-transparent reveal (useTransparentWindow) and the reveal-mute restore (useNativePlayer) fire before any frame is on screen. Fix direction: on Linux, emit host-window-ready (or defer it) from the compositor's first actual rendered frame after attach (linux_compositor.rs render callback), once per file load — episode handoff soft-stop keeps the render context alive, so 'first frame per load' needs a per-load arm, not per-context.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #36 (main 46de2d3): host-window-ready now emitted from the GLArea render handler on the first rendered frame per load (armed at first PlaybackRestart, disarmed on FileLoaded/detach). On-hardware verify steps recorded in issue comment / axj4.5 test plan.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Fix in PR #36 (fix/linux-first-frame-reveal): events.rs no longer emits host-window-ready on Linux at first PlaybackRestart — it calls linux_compositor::arm_first_frame_ready(app) (one-shot AtomicBool + a queue_render nudge so a paused restore still consumes it). The GLArea render handler emits host-window-ready after the first render_no_block Ok of the load. Disarmed on FileLoaded (stale arm from an aborted load must not fire on a black frame) and on detach_mpv. Windows unchanged. ON-HARDWARE VERIFY (axj4.5 plan): load a movie — expect no audible-audio-over-navy gap; logs should show '[player:events] first PlaybackRestart → arming compositor first-frame reveal' then '[player:linux] first frame rendered after arm → player://host-window-ready' with the reveal coinciding with visible video. Also re-check episode handoff (soft-stop) re-reveals each episode, and test plan A3 (mute preservation, prexu-jphh) is unaffected.

title: Linux: app crashes on launch on Wayland (webkit2gtk DMABUF) — needs WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1
description:
On KDE Plasma Wayland + NVIDIA (nobara-pc, webkit2gtk 2.52.3, gtk3 3.24.52), 'npm run tauri dev' (and by extension the shipped AppImage/rpm) crashes IMMEDIATELY at window/webview creation with:

  Gdk-Message: Error 71 (Protocol error) dispatching to Wayland display.

The process dies before first paint — no window. Setting WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 in the environment fixes it: the app then launches, renders opaque, and runs normally. This is the well-known webkit2gtk DMABUF-on-Wayland breakage (especially with NVIDIA). Discovered verifying prexu-duna.3.

## Impact
A user installing the Linux bundle on a Wayland session (default on modern KDE/GNOME) + many GPUs hits an instant crash on first launch. Phase-1 'HTML5 ships on Linux' is not actually usable on Wayland without this.

## Fix options
- Set WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 from within the app before GTK init (std::env::set_var early in main, non-Windows) — most robust, no user action.
- Or export it in the .desktop/AppRun launcher.
- Consider also WEBKIT_DISABLE_COMPOSITING_MODE=1 as fallback; verify video still renders.

## Acceptance
- Fresh Wayland launch of the bundled app shows the window with no Error 71, no env var set by the user.
- Verified on at least KDE Plasma Wayland; note GNOME Wayland + Intel/AMD behavior.
close_reason: Fixed in #27 (squash-merged to main): set WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 at top of run() on Linux when unset. Verified live on KDE Plasma Wayland + NVIDIA — clean launch with no external env var, Error 71 gone, window renders.

title: Windows CI: pinned libmpv nightly 404s (upstream rotates releases) — host durable mirror
description:
The Windows headless-mpv (ci.yml) and release.yml Windows legs download libmpv from zhongfly/mpv-winbuild pinned nightly tag 2026-05-29-13a3e3ad0e. Upstream deletes old nightlies, so the asset now 404s and BOTH workflows fail on Windows (affects main, not just feature branches). Pinning any nightly is fragile — only ~3 days retained.

## Fix
Host a durable libmpv mirror we control (e.g. a 'libmpv-vendor' GitHub release asset on nwilliams22/prexu, up to 2GB) and point ci.yml + release.yml at it. Stable URL that never disappears. ci.yml needs only runtime libmpv-2.dll (mpv.lib + headers already vendored at src-tauri/ci/mpv/); release.yml needs the dev archive to generate mpv.lib.

## Acceptance
- ci.yml + release.yml fetch libmpv from a source we control that does not rotate
- Windows headless-mpv job + release Windows leg green
- SHA256 still verified
close_reason: Self-hosted libmpv-vendor release on nwilliams22/prexu mirrors the baseline dev archive; ci.yml + release.yml repointed at it with pinned SHA256. Windows headless-mpv job green (fetch from mirror succeeded). Durable — asset is ours, excluded from delete-older-releases.

title: Verify HTML5 playback + codec coverage on Wayland (scopes Phase 2)
description: Launch the app natively on a Wayland session (npm run tauri dev). Drive playback across representative media and record which codecs/containers the webkit2gtk HTML5 path plays vs. fails (H.264/AAC, HEVC 8/10-bit, AC3/E-AC3, etc.), plus any transparent-window artifacts. Output: a documented codec-gap list that defines the must-fix scope for the Phase 2 native player. Acceptance: gap list captured in the issue + referenced from the Phase 2 ADR.
notes:
WAYLAND HTML5 CODEC-GAP VERIFICATION (2026-06-30, KDE Plasma Wayland + NVIDIA, webkit2gtk 2.52.3). Drove real playback in the running app against the Plex library (bad-dong-serv); decision read from app logs ([playback] canDirectPlay / buildTranscodeUrl, [player] URL chosen).

LAUNCH: app does NOT start on Wayland without WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 — instant 'Gdk-Message: Error 71 (Protocol error)' crash. Filed prexu-z5mz (P1). With the env var: launches, window renders OPAQUE (transparent:false fix from #26 confirmed — no Wayland desktop-bleed), Plex auth + playback all work.

DIRECT-PLAY (webkit2gtk <video>, no transcode): ONLY H.264 (High/Main, 8-bit) + AAC stereo. (The 'Burbs: direct play, container mp4 h264/aac; audio AAC stereo 93kbps — played, no audio error; 'quiet' is low-bitrate source, not a bug.)

FORCES PLEX TRANSCODE (native libmpv player would direct-play all of these):
  VIDEO: HEVC 8-bit (main), HEVC 10-bit (main 10), AV1.
  AUDIO: AC3, E-AC3, TrueHD, DTS / DTS-HD MA (dca/dca-ma). Anything past AAC stereo transcodes even when video is H.264.
Verified titles → all canDirectPlay:false → buildTranscodeUrl: The Abyss (HEVC8), 2 Fast 2 Furious (HEVC10 4K), 21 Jump Street (AC3), Air (EAC3), Ant-Man & Wasp (HEVC10+TrueHD, slow transcode start), Apocalypse Now (DTS-HD MA), Bohemian Rhapsody (AV1+Opus). Each logged 'HLS non-fatal error fatal:false' during transcode spin-up; none fatal.

LIBRARY IMPACT (Movies section, 3240 items): video forcing transcode = 560 HEVC (547 are 10-bit Main 10) + 2 AV1 ≈ 17%. Audio forcing transcode (even with H.264 video) = ac3 74 + eac3 68 + truehd 10 + dts/dts-ma 18 ≈ 170 titles. => Phase-2 native player must-cover scope: HEVC 8/10-bit, AV1, AC3/E-AC3/TrueHD/DTS(-HD MA). Feed this list into the Phase-2 render-API ADR.

title: Add tauri.linux.conf.json (deb/rpm libmpv depends, transparent=false)
description: Create src-tauri/tauri.linux.conf.json (deep-merges over tauri.conf.json on Linux, mirroring tauri.windows.conf.json). Set bundle.deb.depends and bundle.rpm.depends to the distro libmpv package; configure appimage as needed. Also set app.windows[0].transparent=false on Linux — transparency only serves the Windows mpv overlay and risks desktop-bleed on Wayland. Acceptance: built .deb declares libmpv dependency; Linux window is opaque.
close_reason: tauri.linux.conf.json now sets app.windows[0].transparent=false (full window object required — RFC7396 merge replaces arrays wholesale; visible:false/title/geometry preserved). Bundle deb/rpm libmpv depends already present. Verified merge + Linux cargo build green.

title: release.yml: install libmpv on the Linux release leg so cargo links
description: The ubuntu-22.04 leg in release.yml installs webkit2gtk/appindicator/rsvg/patchelf/openssl but NOT libmpv, so tauri-action's cargo build fails to link (libmpv2 -> -lmpv). Add libmpv-dev (Debian: libmpv-dev) to the Linux apt-get block. Mirror the existing Windows libmpv provisioning pattern. Acceptance: release.yml Linux leg builds + bundles without a link error.
close_reason: release.yml Linux leg installs libmpv-dev (line 58); cargo links system libmpv. Verified in committed workflow.

title: [epic] Linux build & release pipeline (HTML5 ships)
description:
Phase 1 of Linux support: make the existing HTML5 (<video>+hls.js) playback path build, link, and ship on Linux. The native libmpv player stays Windows-only for now (Phase 2 brings a render-API native player to Linux+macOS — see native-player-cross-platform-direction memory + docs/adr-native-player-cross-platform.md).

## Why
- release.yml has a ubuntu-22.04 matrix leg but installs no libmpv, so cargo can't link (libmpv2 is a hard non-Windows dep) -> NO Linux .deb/.AppImage/.rpm is produced today.
- ci.yml never compiles src-tauri on Linux (prexu-z4g3) -> Linux-only Rust breaks reach main (already happened, PR #15).
- No tauri.linux.conf.json -> bundles don't declare the libmpv runtime dep.
- transparent:true is unconditional; it only serves the Windows mpv overlay and risks desktop-bleed on Wayland.

## Team (models by complexity, haiku-first where possible)
- ci-eng (sonnet): release.yml + ci.yml libmpv/webkit provisioning
- bundle-eng (haiku): tauri.linux.conf.json deb/rpm depends + transparent override
- verify-eng (sonnet): launch app on Wayland, confirm codec coverage of HTML5 path
- validation (haiku): tsc/vitest/clippy/cargo-check gates

## Core skills all members load first
core:anti-fabrication, core:git, core:documentation, core:security, core:mise, core:nushell

## Acceptance
- A clean Linux cargo build of src-tauri links libmpv and succeeds in CI
- release.yml produces a Linux bundle that declares/bundles libmpv
- transparent disabled on non-Windows
- Wayland playback verified; codec gaps documented to scope Phase 2

title: Release pipeline broken: ANGLE not provisioned + Windows DLLs unconditionally bundled
description:
v0.7.0 release (tag pushed) failed on ALL 4 platform legs. Two root causes:

1. WINDOWS: release.yml provisions libmpv (MPV_SOURCE) but NOT the ANGLE DLLs (libEGL.dll, libGLESv2.dll). bin/ is gitignored; build.rs copy_angle_dlls finds nothing; tauri_build then errors 'resource path bin/libGLESv2.dll doesn't exist'. The real in-surface DComp render (#16) loads ANGLE at runtime with SHA-256 pins (EGL=a86422d6..., GLESv2=b4856527...) + Authenticode (src/player/angle_loader.rs). ci.yml only STUBS empty ANGLE DLLs for headless resource validation — not usable for a real release. Known TODO at angle_loader.rs:22.

2. macOS/LINUX: tauri.conf.json declares bin/{libmpv-2,libEGL,libGLESv2}.dll as unconditional bundle.resources. Those files never exist off-Windows, so tauri bundling fails 'resource path doesn't exist'. Must move the resources block to a Windows-only tauri.windows.conf.json (Tauri v2 platform config merge).

Fix both, then re-tag v0.7.0 (current tag produced no GH release).
close_reason: Closed

title: [epic] Modernization: tooling gaps + structural debt (TNCR audit)
description:
Whole-repo TNCR modernization audit (2026-06-16). Deps are current (React 19.2, Tauri 2.10, Vite 7, Zod 4, axum 0.8) — no version bumps needed. Debt is tooling gaps + code structure.

## Team

## Sequencing

2. Code-judo: React Compiler (P1), DetailPageShell + TanStack Query collapse duplicate pages (P1)

## Verified findings

- player/mod.rs 1784 / popout.rs 1148 / events.rs 1000 LOC

All tasks: load /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git, /core:security on start. Use claude task-list tool.

title: Early-stop (<60s) does not clear Plex in-progress/resume marker
description: Resuming/starting a video and stopping before 60s leaves the Plex in-progress tag + old viewOffset (e.g. 3:08) unchanged. Root cause: reportStopped relies on Plex implicitly dropping the marker on state=stopped time<60s, which does not hold when a prior resume point exists; beacon is also fire-and-forget with no delivery logging. Fix: on early stop call /:/unscrobble (markAsUnwatched) to deterministically clear; add success/failure logging to both stop paths.
close_reason: Closed

title: SkipSegmentButton/PostPlayScreen global key handlers fire inside text inputs
description: src/components/player/SkipSegmentButton.tsx:37-47 — keydown fires onSkip+preventDefault on any s/S, including typing in SubtitleSearchPanel filter. Same for PostPlayScreen.tsx:192-204 Enter/Escape. Fix: bail when e.target is input/textarea/select/contentEditable.
close_reason: Closed

title: downloads.rs progress emit flood when total=0
description: src-tauri/src/downloads.rs:148-150. file_size 0 + missing content-length -> total=0 -> downloaded>=total true every chunk -> emit flood returns. Guard final-emit branch with total > 0, or drive completion off stream end.
close_reason: Closed

title: subtitle-search: missing ISO 639-2/B codes reproduce the HTTP 500 the map prevents
description: src/services/subtitle-search.ts:44-49. ISO_639_2_TO_1 has only /T codes (fra,deu,zho,nld); Plex commonly sends /B variants (fre,ger,chi,dut,cze,gre) which fall through unmapped. Add /B aliases + test.
close_reason: Closed

title: player_load_url: allowlist http(s) schemes, reject CR/LF/comma in headers
description: src-tauri/src/player/commands/playback.rs:20-61. loadfile accepts any mpv protocol (file://, raw paths, ffmpeg://); header values with comma corrupt http-header-fields list (acknowledged line 33, unenforced); CR/LF can reach ffmpeg request line. Compromised webview gains local-file playback + header injection. Fix: allowlist http/https on url; hard Err on header names/values containing comma, CR, LF (same approach as player_load_external_sub).
close_reason: Closed

title: PlayBridge: relay query param from deep link unvalidated (attacker relay host)

title: LibraryView default sort fetches entire section for alpha jump bar
description: src/pages/LibraryView.tsx:139-147. shouldLoadAll true for default titleSort:asc -> loads thousands of items with Media[] just to power AlphaJumpBar. Fix: keep paging; feed jump bar from Plex firstCharacter index endpoint, scrollToIndex against per-letter offsets.

title: Dashboard: heroSlides rebuilt per render; pullDistance state per touchmove
description: src/pages/Dashboard.tsx:233-239,281-328. heroSlides IIFE does filter+sort every render; setPullDistance per touchmove runs O(n log n) per touch frame. Fix: useMemo([onDeck, recentMovies, dismissedKeys]); pull indicator via ref + direct transform write or rAF throttle.

description: src/hooks/useServerActivity.ts:281 returns fresh object per render; every PosterCard subscribes (PosterCard.tsx:84). Any sessions refetch/activity event/TTL expiry re-renders every mounted card. Fix: useMemo value; better, split scanningIds into own context or useIsScanning(ratingKey) selector backed by subscription store.

title: prepareSource waterfalls metadata fetch -> local-path IPC on every playback start
description: src/services/plex-playback.ts:364-394. Promise.all([getItemMetadata(...), getLocalFilePath(ratingKey).catch(()=>null)]).
close_reason: Closed

title: timedFetch rework: 60s worst-case latency, retries non-idempotent methods, dedup clone race, dedup key ignores token
description: src/services/plex-api.ts:18-19,43-91. (1) 30s timeout + default retry=1 -> 60s worst case, dedup pins URL whole time; restore 15s default or opt-in. (2) retries apply to POST/PUT (switchHomeUser:597, downloadSubtitle, setSelectedSubtitleStream) — default retries only for GET. (3) clone race: first caller holds original Response; owner should resolve never-consumed Response, hand r.clone() to all consumers. title: [bug] Main window restores to wrong size after maximize→unmaximize
description:
REPRO (dashboard, no player open):
1. Launch app (opens at configured 1280x800-ish).
2. Resize window wider/taller to size B.
3. Click OS maximize (square) button — fills screen as expected.
4. Click maximize/restore again to un-maximize.
EXPECTED: restores to size B (pre-maximize geometry).
ACTUAL: restores to a hybrid — launch HEIGHT but a width narrower than B. Not the pre-maximize rect.

NOTES:
- Separate from the native-player/popout path (Player overlay not mounted on dashboard; popout SetWindowPos code does not run here).
- Main window uses tauri-plugin-window-state (window-state:default). Suspect the plugin's persisted inner-size restore is racing/overriding the OS restore rect, possibly compounded by per-monitor DPI (user on 4K secondary, work area origin negative).
- lib.rs WindowEvent::Resized handler fires window://resized + sync_geometry on maximize/restore/fullscreen; verify it isn't stashing/replaying a stale rect.
- Win11 invisible DWM resize borders cause ~7px GetWindowRect vs set_size drift (see popout.rs read_window_rect doc) — relevant if any code round-trips outer/inner size on restore.

INVESTIGATION START: tauri-plugin-window-state registration in lib.rs; whether StateFlags include SIZE/POSITION and when it saves (on every resize?). Try excluding maximized state or deferring restore.
notes:
DETERMINISTIC REPRO FOUND — trigger is Windows Snap:
1. Drag window to a screen edge so Windows Snap resizes it (e.g. half-screen / Discord size).
2. Maximize on the dashboard (maximize button).
3. Click maximize again to un-maximize.
=> Restores to the PRE-snap size, not the snapped size.

ROOT CAUSE (high confidence): tao/tauri-plugin-window-state don't record an OS Snap as a 'normal' resize, so the window's cached restore rect stays at the pre-snap geometry. Maximize→restore returns to that stale rect. Snap rect is lost.

FIX DIRECTIONS to try:
- On WindowEvent::Resized in lib.rs, when the window is neither maximized nor fullscreen, capture inner_size+position as the restore rect ourselves and re-apply on un-maximize (don't trust tao's cached normal rect).
- OR detect WM_EXITSIZEMOVE/snap (WM_SIZE with SIZE_MAXIMIZED vs restored) and persist the snapped rect.
- Verify tauri-plugin-window-state StateFlags / whether it's overriding restore. Possibly exclude MAXIMIZED or stop it re-applying a stale SIZE.
Needs its own branch + Rust rebuild (dev app must be closed — libmpv DLL lock).
close_reason: Closed
