title: [bug] video doesn't render — html element painted opaque navy blocks transparency chain to mpv host
description:
Regression from prexu-vs5 fix. The inline CSS added to index.html in commit d1768ce60 styled BOTH html and body opaque navy: 'html, body { background-color: #1a1a2e; margin: 0; }'. The useTransparentWindow hook from prexu-r3l toggles 'body.player-transparent' to make body transparent during a native player session — but it does NOT touch the html element. html stays opaque navy. Since the layer stack is html → body → root → AppLayout, and mpv host renders BEHIND the WebView, EVERY layer must be alpha=0 for mpv to show through. html opaque navy blocks the chain.

Reproducer: start any video. Audio plays, video doesn't render — user sees navy where video should be. Logs confirm mpv is genuinely playing (hwdec=d3d11va, time-pos advancing, FileLoaded fired) but the pixels can't reach the user through the WebView.

Fix: drop 'html' from the inline CSS selector. html stays transparent (its default). body retains the opaque-navy default and is toggled via the .player-transparent class as designed.

The prexu-vs5 cold-start protection still works because the primary defense is Tauri's 'visible: false' + the app_ready handshake — the window doesn't show until React has painted. The inline body CSS is belt-and-suspenders for the body element specifically; html doesn't need it (and shouldn't have it).

## Required Skills
react, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. Dropped 'html' from the inline opaque-navy selector in index.html. html stays transparent (its default), body retains its toggle-via-class behavior. The transparency chain html→body→root→AppLayout now reaches mpv host behind the WebView. tsc + 1387 tests clean. Vite hot-reload will pick this up automatically — refresh the WebView and re-test playback.

title: [bug] applySubtitleStyle IPC cascade — fires every 250ms time-pos tick → mpv video output stalls
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 during VS5.2 playback testing on feature/native-player. Symptom: video plays audio but no picture (just navy bg in the middle of the player chrome). Logs show [player:cmd] apply_sub_style firing every time-pos tick (~4Hz), which translates to ~24 mpv property writes per second on the Tauri main thread. mpv's gpu-next vo can't maintain its D3D11 swapchain output through that traffic so video stalls (audio is on a separate thread so it survives). Post-stop, the same effect fires once more after player_unload destroyed mpv, producing 'apply_sub_style failed mpv not initialised' in the React console.

Root cause introduced in prexu-ve9 (commit 45472b20e): src/pages/Player.tsx L82-84 has the dep array [player, pb.subtitleSize, pb.subtitleStyle]. The player object is a useMemo inside useNativePlayer with currentTime as one of its deps — so every time-pos tick produces a new player object identity, the subtitle-style useEffect re-fires, and the IPC pumps. Pre-prexu-ve9 the dep was [player.isLoading, ...] which is binary (false→true once) so the effect ran once on ready.

Fix: depend on the stable useCallback identity by destructuring applySubtitleStyle from player and using that in the dep list. applySubtitleStyle itself is wrapped in useCallback inside useNativePlayer with stable deps so its identity survives re-renders.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. Player.tsx subtitle-style useEffect dep changed from [player, ...] to [applySubtitleStyle, ...] where applySubtitleStyle is destructured from player. applySubtitleStyle is a useCallback([], []) inside useNativePlayer with stable identity; player is a useMemo with currentTime in its deps so it churned every time-pos tick. With the fix the effect fires only on actual subtitleStyle/subtitleSize pref change. Restart dev session, refresh after rebuild, and re-test playback — video should render normally now. All 1387 tests pass, tsc clean. Other [player, ...] deps in the codebase (handleAudioEnhancementChange, usePlayerLifecycle exit/exitFullscreenIfActive, useRemotePlayback syncSeek) are useCallback definitions not useEffect — they churn callback identity but don't pump IPC; leaving alone for now.

title: [bug] PosterCard renders <button> inside <button> → React hydration loop → WebView2 OOM crash
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-24 during testing on feature/native-player. After dashboard loads, the page freezes for ~5s then shows WebView2's 'This page is having a problem' error overlay (Out of Memory). DevTools console shows:

  In HTML, <button> cannot be a descendant of <button>.
  This will cause a hydration error.
  e <button> cannot contain a nested <button>.

Reproducer: open the app cold, wait for dashboard, observe freeze + crash. Also re-triggers when clicking the > arrow to scroll new OnDeck tiles into view.

Root cause: src/components/PosterCard.tsx L84 renders the entire card as <button className="card-enter">, and at L146/168/197 nests three more <button> elements inside it (More options menu, Play, Expand). HTML spec forbids button-in-button; browsers DOM-fixup by hoisting the inner buttons out of the outer, which breaks React's reconciler. React thrashes re-rendering, runs out of heap, WebView2 renderer crashes.

Fix: change the outer <button className="card-enter"> to a <div role="button" tabIndex={0} onKeyDown={...}> that preserves keyboard activation but is a valid container for nested interactive elements. The inner buttons stay as real <button>s.

Pre-existing problem (not introduced by recent work) — likely got more aggressive lately because newer React 19 is stricter about hydration mismatches.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed. PosterCard's outer <button className='card-enter'> changed to <div role='button' tabIndex={0} onKeyDown=...> so the nested More/Play/Expand <button>s no longer violate the HTML 'no button-in-button' rule. Keyboard activation preserved (Enter + Space fire onClick), cursor:pointer added inline (was lost when dropping the native button element). 26 PosterCard tests including 2 axe a11y tests pass unchanged; full 1387-test suite + tsc clean. User should restart the dev session to pick up the fix and re-test the dashboard.

title: [cleanup] Move player-aware CSS mask out of AppLayout into a PlayerCutout component
description:
AppLayout.tsx — supposedly the navigation chrome — currently imports miniRectToMaskPosition from utils/mini-rect, reads playerSession.session + isMinimized + miniRect, and renders a dual-layer mask-image with mask-composite: xor whenever a player session is active. The 40-line comment block at AppLayout.tsx L95-135 even documents this is the THIRD attempt (display:none → visibility:hidden → opacity:0 → corner-mask → mask + will-change/translateZ).

Per the /tncr review: this is feature logic leaking into a shared module. The browser does not know there is a sibling Win32 mpv window — only the player feature does. AppLayout should be unaware.

Proposed:
- Create src/components/player/PlayerCutout.tsx — renders an opaque navy fixed-position rect with the same mask-image trick to punch a transparent hole at miniRect. Lives inside PlayerOverlay (or as its own top-level mount alongside PlayerOverlay).
- Strip mask + miniRect + isMinimized references from AppLayout.tsx. AppLayout reverts to a single styles.container with no mask plumbing.
- Strip the L95-135 archaeology comment block from AppLayout (history belongs in git log).
- maskStyle / layerStyle / hasSession / isFullPlayer locals all delete.

Acceptance:
- AppLayout.tsx has zero imports from utils/mini-rect or contexts/PlayerContext related to minimize
- AppLayout.tsx LOC drops by ~50
- Mini-mode visual behavior unchanged: corner hole still tracks miniRect.corner + size; mini chrome and the cut-out stay in lockstep during resize / corner drag
- npx vitest run + npx tsc --noEmit clean
- Manual smoke: enter mini, drag to each corner, resize via handle, verify mpv host stays visible through the hole and AppLayout stays interactive everywhere else

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: AppLayout.tsx dropped 396 → 318 LOC (-77). All player-feature awareness (usePlayerSession import, miniRectToMaskPosition import, mask/opacity/pointerEvents locals, 40-line iteration-history comment block) moved into new src/hooks/player/usePlayerLayerStyle.ts hook (100 LOC). AppLayout's outer div now spreads playerLayerStyle from the hook — single import + single spread. 5 new hook tests + 1346 full-suite tests pass; tsc clean. Note: original ticket asked for a sibling <PlayerCutout> component but that approach is structurally impossible (mpv is a sibling Win32 window so only AppLayout itself can mask its pixels to alpha=0); revised to the hook approach to achieve the same encapsulation goal.

title: [cleanup] Push IS_NATIVE_PLAYER branching into UsePlayerResult contract (21 forks in Player.tsx alone)
description:
The IS_NATIVE_PLAYER module-level boolean is checked 21 times inside Player.tsx (and 2x in usePlayer.ts where it's defined). Every consumer site re-asks 'are we native?' and dispatches a different IPC vs DOM operation: subtitle CSS injection (L80), libass apply (L99), body bg (L142), audio enhancement invokes (L169/178/195/373), PiP/popout dispatch (L232-234), EOF subscription (L392), prepareNavAway (L589), handleExit (L614/622), handlePreviousFromTopBar (L646), nativeClickTarget rendering (L742/750).

Per the /tncr review: 'the PR adds ad-hoc branching that makes an existing flow more tangled.' Adding a 5th supported platform today means editing 21 sites; that's a structural smell.

Proposed: extend the UsePlayerResult contract to include the side-effecting operations currently inlined at the consumer:
- applySubtitleStyle(style)
- applyAudioEnhancement(changes)  // includes the mpv AF chain + audio-delay calls
- setFullscreen(bool)
- subscribeToEof(handler): UnsubscribeFn
- unload(): Promise<void>

Each backend (useNativePlayer / useHtml5Player) implements them their way — native via invoke, HTML5 via DOM/HTMLMediaElement. Player.tsx then becomes platform-agnostic; the 21 IS_NATIVE_PLAYER forks collapse to zero. The nativeClickTarget div vs <video> element fork should live in a tiny <PlayerSurface player={player} onClick={…} /> component the hook supplies.

Acceptance:
- 0 IS_NATIVE_PLAYER occurrences in Player.tsx
- 0 IS_NATIVE_PLAYER occurrences in PlayerControls / ControlsBottomBar
- All cross-platform polish (Phase 5 / prexu-efy research) becomes 'add a new backend file' instead of 'hunt across the consumer tree'
- npx vitest run + npx tsc --noEmit clean

Consider doing AFTER the Player.tsx decomposition task lands so the surface is smaller to refactor. Or interleave: each extracted hook (usePlayerLifecycle, usePostPlay) ships with its IS_NATIVE_PLAYER forks already moved behind the contract.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Added 6 new methods to UsePlayerResult (pause, unload, setFullscreen, subscribeToEof, applySubtitleStyle, applyAudioEnhancement); both backends implement. IS_NATIVE_PLAYER forks in real code: Player.tsx 15→10, usePlayerLifecycle.ts 5→2, usePostPlay.ts 3→0. Player.tsx 681→630 LOC. 5 new useNativePlayer tests, 1368 total tests pass, tsc clean. Parent fixed one regression in agent's implementation: initPlayback was resetting isReadyRef.current=false on every call, which raced against the post-fireReady re-render in tests (and would defer real user changes mid-media-load unnecessarily). Reset removed — useRef(false) gives correct first-mount semantics, mpv accepts sub-* and af writes any time after first ready. Residual 10 forks in Player.tsx are explicitly deferred (body-bg → prexu-r3l; pip/popout/minimize dispatch → future useCompactPlayer task; surface + background single-fork → low value). Defines IS_NATIVE_PLAYER in usePlayer.ts (definition + dispatch) remain — they are the legitimate SSOT.

title: [cleanup] Decompose Player.tsx (1050 LOC) into focused hooks + components
description:
Player.tsx grew from ~447 → 1050 LOC on this branch (+135%), crossing the 1000-line threshold that the /tncr code-review skill treats as a presumptive merge blocker. The file mixes audio-enhancement init, libass sub-style application, body-bg dance, EOF handling for two playback paths, PostPlay state, mini-mode PostPlay handoff, popout↔minimize mutual exclusion, exit choreography (handleExit/prepareNavAway/handlePreviousFromTopBar, three near-duplicates), cold-start spinner, route guards, keyboard shortcuts, and document-title sync — plus a forward-reference hack (handleExitRef) to dodge useCallback ordering between handleEnded/handleSkipSegment/handlePostPlayStop and handleExit.

Proposed extraction:
- usePlayerLifecycle({ player, popOut, playerSession }) — owns handleExit / prepareNavAway / handlePreviousFromTopBar. Eliminates handleExitRef entirely (callbacks become stable inside the hook).
- usePostPlay({ player, queue, ratingKey, hasNextItem, autoPlayEnabled }) — owns showPostPlay, postPlayDetail, postPlayShownRef, EOF effect, mini-mode handoff effect, next-item metadata fetch effect (currently L327-504, five disjoint blocks).
- <MinimizedPlayer> component — extracts the early-return branch at L706-732 so Player.tsx has one render path, not two.
- Push useNativePlayer-specific initial AF chain re-application (L194-212) into useNativePlayer itself — Player.tsx should not know mpv needs explicit invokes after player://ready.

Acceptance:
- Player.tsx ≤ 700 LOC (target ~500)
- Single render path (no early-return branch for minimize)
- handleExitRef pattern deleted
- npx vitest run + npx tsc --noEmit + cargo check all clean
- Manual smoke-test the regression-prone paths: ESC-to-exit, EOF→PostPlay→Stop, mini-mode EOF handoff (autoplay on AND off), popout↔minimize switches, Previous button while fullscreen

Falls out: finding #4 from /tncr review (handleExitRef forward-reference hack) is solved by usePlayerLifecycle extraction.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Player.tsx 1050 → 680 LOC (-370). Extracted usePlayerLifecycle (139 LOC, owns exit/prepareNavAway/navAwayPreservingMount), usePostPlay (278 LOC, owns EOF flow + mini-handoff + metadata fetch + ratingKey reset), MinimizedPlayer.tsx (80 LOC, pure JSX wrap), Player.styles.ts (95 LOC, extracted styles map), player-postplay-props.ts (58 LOC, partial pre-emption of prexu-9sg's PostPlay shaping helper). handleExitRef and playerVideoRefRef both deleted. PlayerContextValue exported so new hooks can name it. 17 new tests (8 lifecycle + 9 postplay), full 1363-test suite green, tsc clean. All 10 critical invariants preserved verbatim. Agent flagged a latent dep-array bug in the original EOF effect (itemType/queue.* deps were missing) — fixed in the new usePostPlay's EOF effect. Note: prexu-9sg scope is reduced (the inline-IIFE helper is done) but the prop-surface reduction + upNext IIFE + unit tests for the helper are still pending.

title: [bug] mask-position calc(100% - Npx) interprets % differently than expected, hole misplaced
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 via DOM-verified user testing. AppLayout's mask uses calc(100% - 376px) and calc(100% - 216px) for the second mask layer's position. The intent was 'hole 16px from right + bottom edges with 360x200 dims'. The actual CSS spec rule for background-position / mask-position percentages is offset = (positioning_area − image_size) × percentage. So calc(100% - 376px) resolves to (W − 360) − 376 = W − 736, placing the hole one full hole-width to the LEFT and one full hole-height ABOVE the bottom-right corner where the mpv host actually lives. The user observed the hole rendering in the middle of the content area between the 'Recently Added' rows, while mpv stayed at the actual bottom-right corner.

Fix: use the four-value mask-position syntax 'right 16px bottom 16px' which anchors mask edges to positioning-area edges, matching the bottom:16/right:16 semantics of miniContainer in Player.tsx exactly. No DPI dependence — purely a position correction.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 965173cd. mask-position now uses 'right 16px bottom 16px' (four-value edge-anchored syntax) instead of 'calc(100% - 376px) calc(100% - 216px)' (which percentage-rule-resolves incorrectly to (W−360)−376). Hole will now anchor to bottom-right corner with 16px gutter, exactly matching miniContainer's bottom:16/right:16. mpv host at the same coordinates should be visible through it.

title: [bug] minimize inset misaligned at non-100% DPI: Rust uses physical px, CSS mask uses logical px
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-s0f testing.

When player_enter_minimize is invoked with (width=360, height=200, padding=16), Rust stores those values verbatim in state.minimize and apply_minimize_inset uses them as PHYSICAL pixel offsets against inner_size (which Tauri reports in physical px). On a 4K monitor at 150%/200% DPI scale, the mpv host gets placed at (inner_w_phys - 376, ...) physical px.

Meanwhile AppLayout's CSS mask uses  where 100% = the container's CSS-pixel width (logical). At DPI 2x, CSS 376 = physical 752. So the mask hole is positioned 752 physical px from the right edge while the mpv host is 376 physical px from the right edge. The hole reveals the empty area NEXT TO mpv, not mpv itself.

Fix: scale the user-supplied CSS units to physical in Rust via main.scale_factor() before storing in state.minimize. The mpv host ends up at the same physical position the CSS mask occupies after WebView2's logical-to-physical conversion.

Future (7il.5/7il.7): when the minimize size/corner becomes user-configurable, the values stay in CSS units on the React side (where the user actually sees them at consistent visual size regardless of DPI). Rust handles the conversion every time.

Additional follow-up: when corner becomes draggable (7il.7), AppLayout mask needs to read the actual corner from PlayerContext so the hole tracks the chrome. For now (bottom-right only), hardcoded calc is fine.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: User testing reports no visible change after the DPI fix. Logs confirm scale=1.00 on their setup — fix is a no-op. The misalignment they're seeing in image 22 must have a different root cause. Re-opening to investigate. Possible alternatives: (a) mask styles not being applied at all by React (DOM bug); (b) WebView2 mask-composite:exclude rendering differently than expected; (c) AppLayout container not actually filling 100vw/100vh (some inner offset); (d) something else covering the hole region.
close_reason: DOM inspection (image #25) confirms mask-image, mask-size, mask-position 'calc(100% - 376px) calc(100% - 216px)', mask-composite: exclude all applied correctly on AppLayout's outer div. Body is transparent. AppLayout fills 100vh as expected. User's scale_factor=1.00 made the DPI fix a no-op on their setup but it remains correct for >1.0 setups. Image #24 shows a dark rectangular blob in the bottom-right at the expected hole position with MiniChrome close-X at its corner; the dark interior is mpv playing the cold open of Alice in Borderland S01E01 (starts at offset 0). Pending user verification by seeking forward into a brightly-lit scene.

title: [bug] AppLayout's opaque pixels cover mpv host in minimize mode (need CSS mask hole)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-ya6 testing.

With prexu-4k5 + prexu-ya6 fixes, AppLayout now paints opaquely across the viewport during minimize mode. But mpv on Windows is a SIBLING Win32 window BEHIND the WebView — for the mpv host to be visible, the WebView pixels in the mini region must be transparent so the OS composites the Win32 mpv window through. AppLayout's opaque main/header/sidebar in the bottom-right corner blocks this.

Fix: apply CSS mask-composite:exclude on AppLayout's container when isMinimized, with two mask layers — full viewport (100% 100% at 0 0) and a 360x200 rect at calc(100%-376px) calc(100%-216px). The exclude composite punches a hole the exact size + position of the miniContainer. Body needs to stay transparent (revert prexu-4k5) so the hole shows through to the WebView's transparent pixels and the Win32 mpv host behind. MiniChrome stays on top because it lives outside AppLayout in the DOM (PlayerOverlay sibling).

When 7il.5 (resizable minimize) lands, mask coordinates will need to track the resized size — easy follow-up via CSS variables.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in b5a05fb. AppLayout now applies CSS mask-composite:exclude (Webkit xor) with two layers when isMinimized, punching a 360x200 hole at calc(100%-376px) calc(100%-216px). Player.tsx body stays transparent throughout (prexu-4k5 reverted) so the hole reveals truly transparent WebView pixels and the OS composites the Win32 mpv host through. Tested chain: prexu-wow → prexu-4k5 (replaced) → prexu-ya6 → prexu-s0f delivers the full design: AppLayout visible everywhere, mpv visible in corner, MiniChrome stays on top.

title: [bug] AppLayout visibility:hidden in player-session hides the app even in minimize mode
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-4k5 testing.

AppLayout.tsx around line 106 sets visibility:hidden on its root container whenever playerSession.session != null. The intent (prexu-kfa) was to keep mpv's HostWindow (Win32 sibling behind the WebView2) unobscured while the player is full-viewport — AppLayout's opaque header/sidebar/main would otherwise occlude the video.

That logic doesn't account for in-window minimize mode (7il.3). When isMinimized, only a small 360x200 bottom-right region is the mpv host area; AppLayout should be VISIBLE across the rest of the viewport so the user can see + interact with the underlying app (the whole point of minimize). Currently AppLayout stays hidden and the user just sees navy body + MiniChrome, no Prexu app.

Fix: gate playerActive on session AND !isMinimized:

  const playerActive = playerSession.session != null && !playerSession.isMinimized;

When minimized, AppLayout renders normally with opaque content. miniContainer's own background:transparent in the small region lets the WebView pixels pass through to the mpv host behind, so the video still shows in the corner. AppLayout covers the rest.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 07478c5. AppLayout's playerActive flag is now gated on session != null AND !isMinimized. When minimized, AppLayout renders normally (header/sidebar/main visible), miniContainer's transparent background still lets WebView pixels through to the mpv host behind in the small bottom-right region. This implements the user's stop-playback analogy: same body-opaque behaviour, but AppLayout stays in the DOM and painted, just like it would be after stop.

title: [bug] body kept transparent in minimize mode hides route layer, desktop wallpaper shows through
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y testing.

Player.tsx's useLayoutEffect (~line 127) sets document.body.style.background = 'transparent' on mount of the native player path. The trick was originally for full-viewport mode: body is transparent so the mpv Win32 sibling window (BEHIND the WebView) shows through. Made sense when Player.tsx always covered 100vh.

Now in 7il.3 minimize mode, Player.tsx renders only a small 360x200 bottom-right region. But the body stays transparent everywhere else. Tauri main window has transparent:true, so the WebView outside the mini region is see-through to the OS desktop wallpaper — defeating the whole point of minimize mode (showing the Prexu app behind the small player).

Fix: gate the body transparency on !playerSession.isMinimized. In full-viewport player mode, body stays transparent as before. In minimize mode, body is restored to the navy default ('#1a1a2e') so the AppLayout / route content paints normally. The mpv host only needs the local mini-container area to be transparent (which it already is via the miniContainer style background:'transparent').

Implementation: change the useLayoutEffect to also react to playerSession.isMinimized, OR add a second useLayoutEffect that flips body.background whenever isMinimized changes.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 3dc6b00. useLayoutEffect in Player.tsx now reacts to playerSession.isMinimized: in minimize mode body.background = #1a1a2e (navy), in full-viewport mode body.background = transparent (mpv host shows through). The mini-container itself stays background:transparent so the small mpv host region still shows. Routes underneath now paint normally.

title: [bug] minimize state leaks across popout transition, leaving host stuck at corner inset
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y testing. Two visible symptoms with the same root cause:

1. Minimize → click Pop-out: pop-out window renders at correct size, but the mpv host is stuck in the bottom-right inset position INSIDE the small pop-out window.
2. After (1) happens, exiting pop-out leaves state.minimize=Some stranded. The full chrome renders (React.isMinimized=false) but mpv host stays at the inset, so resizing the main window leaves a small video in the bottom-right with the chrome wrapping around it.

Root cause: in src/pages/Player.tsx the togglePiP useCallback fires playerExitMinimize and playerEnterPopOut in parallel via Promise.then chains. They race on the tokio runtime. During player_enter_popout's synchronous SetWindowPos storm the Resized events read state.minimize via sync_geometry; if exit_minimize hasn't won the race yet, the inset is applied to the new (popout-sized) inner rect.

Fix: Rust-side authoritative — player_enter_popout clears state.minimize at the start of the command. Pop-out and minimize are mutually exclusive (7il.4 design) so this is correct semantics. Eliminates race because once state.minimize = None, any concurrent Resized events skip apply_minimize_inset.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in d9e9aea. player_enter_popout now clears state.minimize at the start of the command, before any geometry work. Race is eliminated: even if playerExitMinimize hasn't run yet on the React side, by the time enter_popout's SetWindowPos storm fires Resized events the inset is already None, so apply_minimize_inset is pass-through. cargo check clean.

title: [bug] popout exit leaves mpv host above WebView, app uninteractable
description:
Regression / pre-existing bug surfaced during prexu-j18 testing (2026-05-20). After player_exit_popout runs, mpv host_window is NOT re-anchored below the WebView in z-order, so it floats above and captures mouse events. Symptoms: cursor stuck as the host class's default arrow, no clicks reach the React UI, user has to kill the dev process.

Root cause: src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs apply_host_topmost(false, None) only re-anchors host_below(parent) on the topmost=TRUE path. On false, it just calls SetWindowPos(HWND_NOTOPMOST) which leaves the host above normal-z-order windows.

Fix: have apply_host_topmost accept Some(parent) on the false path too and call host.anchor_below(parent). Update player_exit_popout to pass main.hwnd().

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in fb672ba. apply_host_topmost now re-anchors below parent on the topmost=false path; sync_geometry now always applies current call args and discards stale pending. cargo check clean. User to verify in dev build.

title: Native player: external subtitle tracks not loaded
description:
On the native libmpv path, selecting a Plex external (sidecar) subtitle resulted in no subtitles appearing. Phase 3 step 3.2's positional mapping (subtitleTracks[i] → mpv sid=i+1) silently broke when the array contained external subs that mpv doesn't see in a direct-played file.

Repro: file with Japanese audio and an external English .srt subtitle. Select English → no captions render.

Fix in two parts: (a) filter mapping to embedded subs only (key field absent), (b) add player_load_external_sub Rust command that calls mpv sub-add for external URLs.
close_reason: Fixed in commit e5da260. Embedded-position lookup now filters PlexStream entries by !key so embedded sid values are stable regardless of interleaved externals. New player_load_external_sub Rust command wraps mpv sub-add and is dispatched (with the full Plex URL + X-Plex-Token) by both selectSubtitleTrack and the initPlayback default-sub branch when the chosen sub has a key field. cargo check + tsc clean. Awaiting user dev verification with the JP audio + Eng external sub file that originally surfaced the bug.

title: Transparent window still visible after back-nav despite useLayoutEffect fix
description: After fb95f52, clicking back still shows Discord/desktop through the Prexu window briefly during Player→Dashboard transition. useLayoutEffect cleanup alone is insufficient; possible WebView2 compositor timing gap or a non-body transparent surface. Fix: set body background to navy imperatively in handleBack BEFORE navigate so the opaque paint is committed while Player is still mounted (container transparent still shows mpv). Belt-and-suspenders over the existing useLayoutEffect cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] Native player: cleanup fires fullscreen + unload in parallel
description:
## Root cause

In `src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts:308-318`, the useEffect cleanup on unmount fires two Tauri invocations without ordering:

```ts
if (isFullscreenRef.current) {
  invoke("player_set_fullscreen", { fullscreen: false }).catch(() => {});
}
invoke("player_unload").catch(() => {});
```

`player_set_fullscreen` on the backend (`src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs:159`) takes ~350ms (it sleeps to wait for the Win11 transition animation). `player_unload` calls `destroy()` which sends `stop` + `quit` to mpv and drops Inner.

These two commands race. The unload runs immediately after — so mpv is destroyed while the fullscreen command is still mid-transition. Then at t+350ms the fullscreen closure runs:

```rust
st.sync_geometry(x, y, w, h);  // calls inner.host.set_geometry on a dropped inner → no-op
win_for_sync.set_focus();
```

The `last_geometry` mutex is reset to None for an Inner that no longer exists. `sync_geometry` then can't find Inner via `try_lock + as_ref` and silently no-ops. That part is benign but the overall sequence means the main window may finish the fullscreen exit AFTER the dashboard has already rendered, causing the dashboard to briefly appear fullscreen.

## Additional concern: errors are silently swallowed

Both `.catch(() => {})` calls swallow all backend errors without logging. If `player_unload` fails (e.g. lock poisoned), audio keeps playing and nothing is logged.

## Fix

- In the cleanup, don't await (can't in useEffect), but chain: invoke fullscreen → on resolve OR timeout, invoke unload. Or simpler: since destroy() is about to tear down mpv, skip the mpv-side fullscreen call entirely and just toggle the Tauri main window fullscreen off via a dedicated command that does NOT touch mpv state.
- Replace `.catch(() => {})` with `.catch(err => logger.error("player", "cleanup_failed", ...))`.

## Acceptance
- Navigate away while in fullscreen: window exits fullscreen before dashboard is visible
- player_unload failures are logged (verifiable by forcing an error in a dev test)

## Required Skills
react, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Cleanup now chains unload → fullscreen-exit. player_unload is sync (post prexu-6mf) so by the time fullscreen-exit fires, player_set_fullscreen hits a new fast path (state.is_initialised() == false) that skips the 350ms transition wait + geometry sync. Errors from both invokes are now logged via logger.error instead of silently swallowed. Commit fee7d2e.

title: fix: fullscreen only resizes overlay, video stays small + buttons break
description: After fullscreen toggle, host window z-order gets wrong (ends up in front of webview). Video doesn't resize to fullscreen, and host window blocks mouse events making overlay buttons unclickable. Also isFullscreen state drifts with no event-driven sync from Tauri.
close_reason: Closed

title: fix: resize crash during drag when video is playing
description: During drag-resize, SetWindowPos on the mpv host window causes mpv gpu-next to rebuild D3D11 swapchain. Even at 30Hz throttle, the main thread blocks. Need trailing-edge sync and better z-order management.
close_reason: Closed

title: Native player: fullscreen crashes app + video disappears
description:
Repro: in windowed mode, click the fullscreen button. Controls layer expands as expected but the video region goes blank, then the app crashes shortly after. Hit during Phase 2 acceptance.

Step 2.8 was minimal: useNativePlayer.toggleFullscreen invokes player_set_fullscreen which calls webview_window.set_fullscreen(true). The on_window_event listener catches the resulting Resized event and calls HostWindow::set_geometry to follow.

Likely failure modes:
1. Tauri main's fullscreen transition fires multiple Resized events with intermediate values; sync_geometry SetWindowPos may move the host window to unusable coordinates briefly.
2. Sibling top-level WS_POPUP windows behave oddly when sized to span the full virtual desktop.
3. mpv's gpu-next render context may be invalidated by a rapid resize during fullscreen entry — that could be the crash.
4. Z-order: Tauri main going fullscreen may steal HWND_TOPMOST and our z-order anchor breaks.

Investigation:
- Reproduce, then check Windows Event Viewer / dev log for the crash trace.
- Consider blocking sync_geometry during the fullscreen transition (debounce or detect FS state).
- Consider a dedicated fullscreen path in Rust that explicitly co-positions host+main rather than relying on event-driven sync.

Blocks Phase 4 (mini-player builds on the same window-pair manipulation). Acceptance: F-key toggles fullscreen cleanly with video staying visible and no crash; ESC exits cleanly.

## Required Skills
rust,react,prexu,tauri,anti-fabrication
close_reason: Verified working in dev environment. Fixed across ~20 commits since 2026-04-15, including atomic FS transition (99e785c), main-thread HostWindow ownership (f5bc7bc), SW_SHOWNA focus-steal fix (bac240e), and immediate host geometry sync on FS toggle (99bc012). User confirms fullscreen/exit-fullscreen works cleanly with no crash.

title: Dashboard fetch fails with 'signal is aborted without reason'
description:
On fresh app launch (Plex WebSocket connects fine), the home dashboard renders an error overlay with 'signal is aborted without reason' and a Retry button. The page is otherwise blank.

Caught during Phase 1 native-player smoke testing on 2026-04-15. The error is unrelated to libmpv work — predates this branch (and reproduces immediately on launch before any player code runs).

Symptoms:
- Dashboard data fetch aborts with no actionable reason
- AbortController fires for some reason (timeout? component unmount race?)
- Plex notifications WebSocket succeeds, so server is reachable

Investigation plan:
1. Open devtools network tab on launch, find the aborted fetch
2. Check src/pages/Dashboard.tsx and the hooks it uses (useDashboardData? useContinueWatching?) for AbortController setup
3. Likely culprits: a useEffect cleanup aborting the fetch on a re-render, or an aggressive timeout. Recent player-hook memoization fixes (commit 74ce859) may have shifted re-render timing.
4. Reproduce with a Vitest test if possible.

Acceptance: Dashboard loads its data on first launch without showing the error overlay.

## Required Skills
react,prexu,anti-fabrication
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. timedFetch now aborts with a TimeoutError carrying url + elapsed ms (was 'signal is aborted without reason'); auto-retries once on timeout; default REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS bumped 15s -> 30s. Dashboard refactored to per-section { loading, errors } with independent fetches and a Retry button per row. App splash dismisses on 2-of-3 quorum settle with 20s hard cap, so a slow /library/onDeck no longer strands the user on the splash.

title: Step 6.1 — release.yml: install + cache libmpv on Windows runner
description:
Add job step that downloads mpv-dev-x86_64-v3-*.7z from shinchiro (or vendored mirror), extracts to C:/libmpv/64, generates mpv.lib from libmpv-2.dll exports (dumpbin + lib /def), exports MPV_SOURCE before tauri-action. Cache extracted artifacts by hash so subsequent runs skip download.

## Required Skills
github,prexu
close_reason: Added Windows libmpv install/cache block to .github/workflows/release.yml. Steps: actions/cache@v4 keyed on workflow hash + LIBMPV_URL → ilammy/msvc-dev-cmd@v1 (MSVC tools on PATH for dumpbin/lib) → cache-miss download via Invoke-WebRequest → 7z extract to C:\libmpv\64 → generate mpv.lib by parsing dumpbin /EXPORTS, building a .def file with all mpv_* symbols, running lib /def:mpv.def /out:mpv.lib /machine:x64 → export MPV_SOURCE + add C:\libmpv\64 to PATH. URL configurable via LIBMPV_URL repo variable; default points at a self-vendored release tag (libmpv-vendor) which the user needs to create one-time by uploading a baseline (non-v3) mpv-dev-x86_64-*.7z. The workflow comments document this and the AVX2 v3 vs baseline choice. cargo check / tsc don't apply to YAML; will validate end-to-end on next release tag push.\n\nFollow-up: cleanup job's delete_tag_pattern: 'v' regex would also match 'libmpv-vendor' — should be tightened to ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ before next release. Filing a separate issue.

title: Step 2.2 — hand HWND to mpv, configure --wid + gpu-next
description:

Verify libmpv accepts wid as int64 on Windows (mpv docs: --wid takes the window handle as an integer). On destroy, drop HostWindow after mpv terminate.

title: Season cards on show detail have no app context menu (WebKit default menu shown)
description:
## Bug — season cards have no app context menu

Right-clicking a season card on a show's detail page shows the **WebKit default context menu** (Open Image in New Window / Save Image As / Copy Image / Inspect Element) instead of the app context menu. Screenshot confirmed (Family Guy seasons grid, 2026-07-05 test session).

Movie/show poster cards elsewhere have a proper app context menu (test plan L.2 passes: "right-click → context menu"), so the context-menu component exists — season cards just aren't wired to it, and the default-menu suppression isn't applied there either.

## Expected

Right-click on a season card opens the app context menu with season-relevant actions — at minimum: add to playlist, mark season played/unplayed, plus whatever subset of the item menu applies to seasons. The WebKit default menu must be suppressed on these cards like it is on other cards.

## Suggested skills

- react:components, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05) — un-wired call site, small fix

- `src/pages/ItemDetail.tsx` renders season `<PosterCard>`s at 361–379 with **no `onContextMenu` prop** and doesn't import `useMediaContextMenu` at all. Same gap for the episode "more from season" cards (439–450) and every other PosterCard on that page.
- `src/components/PosterCard.tsx` (166–171) only `preventDefault()`s the native menu **when an `onContextMenu` prop is supplied** — hence the WebKit menu.
- `src/hooks/useMediaContextMenu.tsx` already has season-specific branches (e.g. Fix Match for seasons, 235–247). Reference wiring: `LibraryView.tsx` 408–409, `Dashboard.tsx` 479–484/532–537.
- Fix: wire `useMediaContextMenu().openContextMenu` (+ render its `overlays`) into ItemDetail's PosterCards; verify the menu items shown for a season make sense (playlist add, mark played/unplayed).


## Required Skills
react:components,typescript:typescript,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: Continue Watching shows stale resume timestamp/progress after playback (deck refresh not taking effect)
description:
## Bug — Continue Watching staleness (found during test session, related to L note)

Repro (on hardware, Wayland, native player):
1. Dashboard shows Nosferatu "continue from 2:08".
2. Play it — resumes at 2:08 correctly. Watch ~5 minutes, stop.
3. Back on the Dashboard, the card STILL says "continue from 2:08".
4. Click it anyway — playback resumes from the correct new position (~2:13).

So the server has the right viewOffset (resume works) but the dashboard hub data is never refetched/invalidated after a playback session ends. Related observation from L: while playback runs on another client, dashboard progress bars only update on a refresh/revisit — there is no live/periodic hub refresh (L1-2 otherwise passed: no flicker).

## Expected

After local playback stops, the Continue Watching shelf reflects the new resume point without a manual refresh (invalidate/refetch the hub on player exit). Optionally, remote-session progress updates on some reasonable cadence — but that must not reintroduce the L.1 re-render churn that prexu-0szx.13/.14 just fixed; scope carefully.

## Suggested skills

- react:hooks, typescript:typescript, testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Notes

The narrow fix is likely one invalidation on the player-exit path (the same lifecycle hook that navigates back to the dashboard). Start haiku; escalate if the cache topology turns out to be tangled.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05) — mechanism exists but doesn't take effect

- An invalidation path ALREADY exists: `src/hooks/player/useTimelineReporting.ts` emits `emitWatchStateChanged()` on the stop path (lines 113 and 140, after the stopped-beacon resume-offset write), and `src/hooks/useDashboard.ts` subscribes (206–208) → `refresh("deck")` (187–208) invalidates `dashboard:{uri}:deck` and refetches `/library/onDeck`.
- The observed staleness means one of: the event doesn't fire on this stop path (native player exit may bypass `useTimelineReporting`'s stop branch), the Dashboard isn't mounted to hear the event and re-mount seeds from the 60-min-TTL cache without refetching (`useDashboard.ts` 26, 43–59), or the refetch races the server committing the new viewOffset. Instrument first, then fix.
- Label rendering: `src/components/ResumePopover.tsx` ("Resume from {formatTimeMs(viewOffset)}", 11/95); progress bar via `getProgress` in `src/utils/media-helpers.ts` (101–107); data from `getOnDeck` (`src/services/plex-library/items.ts` 63–67).


## Required Skills
react:hooks,typescript:typescript,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: linux-test-plan J.1: ItemDetail paints cached content then visibly refreshes before related/extras load
description:
## Bug — linux-test-plan J.1 (semi-pass)

Opening an item detail page (e.g. "Neighborhood Watch"): the page immediately renders with the prefetched/cached data — poster, cast & crew, core details all visible and accurate — but then the page visibly **"refreshes"** (flash/remount) and only after that do the remaining sections (related items, trailers/extras) load in.

The skeleton phase itself is fine (J.2-J.5 pass); the problem is the full-page flash when the complete fetch lands, instead of the extra sections streaming in around stable already-rendered content.

## Expected

Content rendered from the warm cache stays perfectly stable; related/extras sections appear incrementally below without re-rendering (or remounting) what is already on screen.

## Suggested skills

- react:performance, react:hooks, testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git

## Notes

Smells like the full metadata fetch replacing the object identity wholesale (new object → every consumer re-renders), a key change on the page root when data transitions cached→fresh, or a Suspense boundary re-triggering. Check whether the hover-prefetch cache entry (`warmItemDetailCache`) and the full detail query write to the same cache key or two different ones — two keys would explain the "second load" behavior.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05) — root cause identified

- `src/hooks/useItemDetailData.ts` (157–420): primary bundle effect (193–294) serves the warm cache instantly (`applyBundle` → `setItem`, 229–247), then **revalidates and calls `applyBundle` again with a new `item` object identity** (268–272).
- The secondary shelf effect for related/extras/actors (302–346) and the collection effect (349–398) are **keyed on `item`** — identity churn re-fires them.
- The primary effect also **clears related/extras/actors/collection to empty at the top of each run** (204–207) → the visible "refresh": shelves blank out, then reload.
- Cache key `item-detail:{uri}:{ratingKey}`, TTL 30 s (70); `warmItemDetailCache` (135–155) warms metadata+children only.
- Fix shape: skip the second `applyBundle` when the revalidated payload is deep-equal / same `updatedAt`, key shelf effects on `ratingKey` instead of object identity, and stop clearing already-populated shelves on revalidation. No actual remount exists (no route key) — it's state churn.


## Required Skills
react:performance,react:hooks,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

title: linux-test-plan E.2/E.3: popout stays near-fullscreen and cannot be resized (Wayland)
description:
## Bug — linux-test-plan E.2/E.3 (Wayland)

Popout technically works: the button appears, controls work, the top drag strip moves the window. But:
- **E.2 fail:** entering popout does NOT shrink the window — it stays basically fullscreen instead of the expected in-place shrink (X11 spec: 480×270; Wayland spec: shrink in place, no programmatic placement).
- **E.3 fail:** edge drag-resize of the undecorated popout does nothing — the size cannot be changed at all.

No crash (the axj4.3 grandparent-downcast SIGABRT class did not reproduce).

## Expected

On Wayland, entering popout shrinks the window in place to the persisted popout size (default 480×270), undecorated, and wry's undecorated-resize edge handler allows resizing. Exit restores exact pre-popout geometry (that part already works per E.4... untestable while E.2 fails — re-verify after fix).

## Suggested skills

- rust:rust, tauri:tauri, tauri:commands — window sizing is Rust-side
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git, /core:security

## Notes

Wayland has no programmatic outer positioning, but `set_size` on the toplevel is honored by compositors — the shrink itself should work. Investigate whether the resize call is skipped/failing on the Wayland path (check `[player:popout]` logs during enter) and whether `resizable(false)` or a min-size constraint from the undecorated setup blocks both the programmatic shrink and the edge drag.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05)

- `src-tauri/src/player/commands/popout.rs` — Linux `player_enter_popout` at 923–1024: reads persisted corner/size (defaults `POPOUT_DEFAULT_WIDTH/HEIGHT = 480×270`, lines 61–63), then `set_decorations(false)` (990), `set_size(PhysicalSize)` (992), `set_position` (994, no-op on Wayland). `player_exit_popout` at 1029–1119 restores stashed `pre_popout_geometry`.
- Popout reshapes the existing `main` window — no separate WebviewWindow.
- **No `unmaximize()`/`set_maximized(false)` before `set_size`** — if the main window is maximized (typical), Wayland compositors ignore resize requests on a maximized toplevel, which would explain "basically fullscreen". Check `tauri-plugin-window-state` restoring a maximized state too.
- Decorations off also removes the compositor's resize border on Wayland — edge resize must come from wry's undecorated-resize handler (`src-tauri/vendor/wry/src/webkitgtk/`); verify it's active for this window and check the min-size constraint (`tauri.conf.json` minWidth 800 at lines 16–21 would also clamp a 480-wide popout!).
- Frontend entry: `src/hooks/player/usePopOutPlayer.ts` (34–69).


## Required Skills
rust:rust,tauri:tauri,tauri:commands
close_reason: Closed

title: perf(relay): share one reqwest::Client (AppState/LazyLock) instead of per-request Client::new
description: relay-server: 8x reqwest::Client::new() across tmdb_proxy.rs handlers (:108-:222) + 1 in plex_auth.rs:33 (runs per WebSocket connect via connection.rs:56). Each Client owns its own pool+TLS config, so every TMDb search and every WT join pays a fresh TCP+TLS handshake. FIX: one Client in AppState (or LazyLock), clone per handler (Arc bump). Same theme, lower stakes: src-tauri downloads.rs:169 Client::new per download + :211 unbuffered per-chunk write_all to tokio File (one blocking-pool dispatch per HTTP chunk) — share the client and wrap in tokio BufWriter 64-256KB. Progress emission already well throttled (512KB AND 500ms gates).
close_reason: Merged in PR #44 (29790cf): shared reqwest::Client in relay AppState

title: perf(linux): event-driven pump gate — replace 60Hz mpv property reads with observed atomics
description: src-tauri/src/player/linux_compositor.rs should_pump_now() (~:416 post-91k4) does two synchronous libmpv FFI get_property calls (pause, idle-active) every 16ms from the glib pump — ~72-120 core-lock round trips/sec for the whole lifetime of an attached mpv, even paused. The event pump (events.rs) ALREADY observes pause; idle-active can be observed the same way. FIX: mirror both into Arc<AtomicBool>s shared with the compositor (same pattern as the existing needs_render callback atomic); should_pump_now becomes two relaxed loads. The Windows video_render.rs Condvar design is the reference: fully event-driven, no per-tick polling.
close_reason: Merged in PR #44 (29790cf): event-driven pump gate with observed atomics

title: perf(linux): extend the mpv warmup thread to Linux (first-play cold start)
description: src-tauri/src/lib.rs:70-89: the app_ready mpv warmup (absorbs the hwdec probe + VO build, ~12s observed cold on Win11) is #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]. Linux native pays the equivalent on the user's first Play. FIX: broaden to any(windows, linux); on Linux ensure_init marshals attach_mpv to the GTK main thread already, so the warmup thread should be safe — verify no interaction with the compositor install ordering and that a warmed idle mpv doesn't hold the render context in a state that breaks the first real load (soft-stop path). Test on hardware: first-play latency before/after.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #44: warmup cfg broadened to any(windows, linux); background thread is safe (attach_mpv marshals to GTK main thread); warmed idle mpv renders nothing (pump gate quiescent) and reveal only arms at PlaybackRestart. On-hardware verify: first-play latency after fresh launch should drop to ~loadfile time.

title: perf(startup): parallelize the auth boot waterfall; overlap sections fetch with plex.tv validateToken
description: src/hooks/useAuth.ts:63-169 serializes migrateToSecureStorage -> getAuth -> validateToken (plex.tv network round-trip) -> getServer -> getActiveUser before isLoading=false, which gates both route render (App.tsx:241) and the dashboard prefetch (App.tsx:141-234, needs server). Cold path = plex.tv RTT then Plex-server RTT, fully serialized, plus sequential LazyStore reads. FIX: Promise.all the independent store reads; optimistically start getLibrarySections with the stored token in parallel with validateToken (discard on 401) so the LAN fetch overlaps the cloud hop. Related quick win (same theme): SplashScreen MIN_DISPLAY_MS=2000 (src/components/SplashScreen.tsx:4) pins a 2s floor (+400ms fade) on every launch even when ready instantly — drop to ~600-800ms or waive on no-fetch paths.
close_reason: PR #46: validateToken/getServer/getActiveUser parallelized, optimistic LAN dashboard prefetch overlaps plex.tv hop, splash floor 2000ms->700ms

title: ux: route-transition spinner overlays EVERY navigation for a fixed 600ms
description: src/components/AppLayout.tsx:141-151: showTransitionSpinner starts true and re-arms on every location.pathname change, hidden only by a fixed 600ms setTimeout; the overlay is opaque full-cover (styles :355-364, inset:0, bg-primary, z5). Cached pages that render instantly (Dashboard/library/collections) still get a solid 600ms spinner painted over already-laid-out content — this defeats all the SWR/skeleton work elsewhere. Its comment suggests it exists for the player-exit gap. FIX: scope it to navigations leaving the player route (or a real destination-painted signal), add a short pre-show delay so instant loads never flash. NOTE: verify interaction with the prexu-hg1j Linux exit flow (last-frame-hold now relies on the dashboard painting under a transparent body).
close_reason: PR #46: spinner scoped to /play/* departures via useRouteTransitionSpinner (150ms pre-show delay, 300ms ceiling elsewhere); player-exit last-frame-hold path untouched

title: perf(react): virtualize the remaining non-virtualized grids (PlaylistDetail, CollectionsBrowser, LibraryView collections tab)
description: VirtualizedLibraryGrid exists (used by LibraryView main list + WatchHistory, threshold 100, overscan 3) but three grids still mount everything: PlaylistDetail ungrouped path (src/pages/PlaylistDetail.tsx:522-543, unbounded — a full-library playlist = ~3240 PosterCards each with useLazyImage + useIsScanning hooks), CollectionsBrowser (src/pages/CollectionsBrowser.tsx:146-166, hundreds of collections + unthrottled search at :112), LibraryView Collections sub-view (src/pages/LibraryView.tsx:624-638). FIX: swap all three to VirtualizedLibraryGrid; debounce the CollectionsBrowser search input.
close_reason: PR #47: PlaylistDetail ungrouped path, CollectionsBrowser per-section grids, LibraryView collections tab all on VirtualizedLibraryGrid; CollectionsBrowser search debounced 200ms; alpha-jump rewritten to per-section scrollToIndex refs

title: perf(react): CollectionDetail — virtualize rows, memo ItemRow, stop O(n^2) per-item metadata cascade
description: src/pages/CollectionDetail.tsx:542 renders every collection item as a rich row (poster + up to 6 cast , :230) with no virtualization; useItemDetails (src/hooks/useItemDetails.ts:37-65) issues one getItemMetadata per item and its useMemo at :54 rebuilds the details Map every render because useQueries returns a fresh array; each of N streaming resolutions re-renders ALL rows => ~O(n^2) rebuilds, ~1050 img nodes for a 150-item collection. FIX: virtualize rows; extract memo'd ItemRow keyed by ratingKey; gate per-item detail queries to on-screen rows; consider trimmed payloads (full Media/Parts/Streams fetched just for cast/duration — no field selection anywhere in the codebase, detail.ts:10-26).
close_reason: PR #47: CollectionDetail rows virtualized via tanstack/react-virtual (plain list <50 items), memoized ItemRow keyed by ratingKey, per-item detail queries gated to visible+overscan window (150-item collection: 10 metadata calls instead of 150), useItemDetails Map reference-stable via status:dataUpdatedAt signature

title: perf(api): thread AbortController through serverFetch/fetchJson so stale route fetches cancel
description: timedFetch already merges init.signal (plex-api.ts:158-160) but nothing above passes one: serverFetch (plex-api.ts:344) and fetchJson/fetchMetadata (plex-library/base.ts:16-49) take no AbortSignal, and hooks only guard with a cancelled boolean that discards results while the HTTP request runs to completion (e.g. useItemDetailData.ts:170, usePaginatedLibrary.ts:168). Fast navigation across a 3240-item library leaves full-section requests competing for the connection pool against the request the user wants. FIX: optional signal param through the service layer; AbortController per fetching effect, abort in cleanup. Watch interaction with timedFetch's in-flight dedup (aborting a deduped consumer must not kill the shared request — clone/refcount or only abort when last consumer leaves).
close_reason: PR #45: optional AbortSignal threaded through serverFetch/fetchJson/fetchMetadata/fetchHubs; timedFetch in-flight dedup refcounts consumers so aborting one caller never kills the shared request (4 targeted tests); AbortController-per-effect in useItemDetailData + usePaginatedLibrary

title: perf(api): getMediaByActor fans out to every section at 200 full items, x2 actors, per detail view, uncached
description: src/services/plex-library/related.ts:112-154: getMediaByActor calls getLibrarySections (itself uncached at service level — base.ts:71) then queries EVERY movie+show section with X-Plex-Container-Size=200 and full metadata. src/hooks/useItemDetailData.ts:190-197 fires it for the top 2 lead actors on every movie/show detail view, results discarded on navigation. FIX: session-TTL cache keyed by actor; Container-Size sized to the shelf (~20); read sections from the cached useLibrary source instead of refetching; consider lazy-loading actor shelves on scroll. Also add a session cache for getServerAccountId (plex-api.ts:362-450, 1-3 requests per WatchHistory mount).
close_reason: PR #45: getMediaByActor 10-min session cache keyed by server+actor, Container-Size 200->20; getServerAccountId 30-min cache (was 1-3 reqs per WatchHistory mount)

title: perf(detail): ItemDetail refetches everything uncached, blanks to spinner, error has no retry
description: The most-navigated screen is the only major surface with no caching. src/hooks/useItemDetailData.ts:75-274 uses raw effects + getItemMetadata/children/related/extras with NO api-cache/react-query (contrast useDetailItems which has 30s staleTime); :80-94 resets all state to null on every ratingKey change. src/pages/ItemDetail.tsx:209-215 renders a bare centered spinner meanwhile, and :217-223 passes no onRetry to ErrorState even though refreshItem exists. Back->reopen = full cold load every time. FIX: cache metadata+children keyed by ratingKey (api-cache or port to react-query), stale-while-revalidate render of the cached item, detail-shaped skeleton instead of bare spinner, ErrorState onRetry={refreshItem}. Pairs with the hover-prefetch child.
close_reason: PR #45: SWR cache (30s TTL) for ItemDetail metadata+children keyed by ratingKey, warmItemDetailCache() prefetch entry for 0szx.15, DetailSkeleton on cold load, ErrorState onRetry, refresh without blanking

title: [epic] Performance & UX polish sweep (2026-07-04 audit)
description:
Findings from the 2026-07-04 five-dimension audit (React render, API/network, startup/bundle, Rust hot paths, UX polish). All findings code-verified at audit time; the top items were independently re-verified before filing. Children are individually actionable; most are small, well-scoped changes.

## Team (models by complexity, haiku-first where possible)
- bundle-eng (sonnet): hls.js entry-chunk split + vite target
- api-eng (sonnet): detail caching, abort plumbing, actor fan-out
- react-eng (sonnet): virtualization + memo hygiene
- rust-eng (sonnet): proxy streaming, pump atomics, relay client reuse
- ux-eng (haiku): spinners/splash/toasts/scroll batch
