Everything merged so far: .1/.2/.10/.11/.12 via PRs #42-#44.

title: Linux: subtitles render at full-surface scale (and clipped) in mini-player mode
description: On-hardware axj4.5 testing (2026-07-03): with subtitles on, minimizing the player leaves subtitle text at full-player size inside the mini rect — one line fills the whole mini width and clips at the rect edge (screenshot in session). Cause: Linux mini mode is video-margin-ratio-* insets on the full GLArea surface (prexu-axj4.5 design); mpv's subtitle/OSD layer is NOT affected by video margins and keeps rendering at full-surface scale (see mpv docs for video-margin-ratio: OSD/subs may render into margins per sub-use-margins). Windows mini is a genuinely small host window so subs scale naturally — Linux needs manual compensation. Fix directions: while minimized, (a) apply a proportional sub-scale factor (mini height / full height) via sub-scale, restoring on exit — must compose with the user's sub style size from apply_sub_style; (b) plus sub-use-margins=no so subs stay inside the video rect. Apply/restore in the same enter/update/exit minimize commands that set margins (minimize.rs linux section), keyed off the computed ratios so window resizes while mini stay correct.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #37: sub-scale set to mini video height fraction + sub-use-margins=no in apply_margin_ratios (single choke point for enter/update/exit/resize-while-mini); zero ratios restore defaults. On-hardware verify: subs in mini mode should render proportionally small inside the mini rect.

title: Linux: mute state not preserved across stop -> next play (test plan A3)
description: On-hardware axj4.5 test plan A3 (2026-07-03): user muted during playback, STOPPED the item, then played the next episode — it started WITH audio. Test plan expectation: 'stays muted after the reveal (the restore must not unmute you)'. Two hypotheses to check: (a) the reveal-mute restore applies isMutedRef.current which was reset when the player session ended (new session initializes isMuted=false rather than from persisted preference); (b) mute is deliberately per-session and the expectation itself needs a decision — if so, decide + document, and persist mute like volume if persistence is wanted. Note the strict A3 scenario ('start a DIFFERENT item' without stopping, e.g. episode handoff while muted) may behave differently from the stop->play sequence the user ran — verify both.
close_reason: Resolved as hypothesis (b): mute is per-session BY DESIGN (bd memory player-mute-scope-decision) — kept across in-session episode handoff/autoplay, deliberately lost on stop. PR #38 (persistence) was wrong and reverted by PR #39, which documents the decision in code and pins the per-session reset with a regression test. In-mount handoff retention already worked and is covered by reveal-mute test (a). No behavior bug remains.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Root cause confirmed as hypothesis (a): useNativePlayer initialized isMuted with useState(false) while volume used useState(getSavedVolume) — stop unmounts the hook, so the next play started a session with isMuted=false and the reveal-mute restore applied that false (audio on). Episode handoff (same mount) kept mute, which is why the two A3 variants behaved differently. DECISION: persist mute like volume (localStorage prexu_muted via getSavedMuted/saveMuted in plex-playback.ts) — matches the A3 test-plan expectation and volume-slider behavior. Applied to BOTH engines for parity: useNativePlayer + useHtml5Player initialize from storage and persist on toggleMute and on setVolume's auto-unmute. New tests: plex-playback round-trip, A3 regression (persisted mute survives fresh mount + reveal restore keeps it), toggle-persists.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: CORRECTION: PR #38's persistence contradicted the prior-session design decision (now recorded as bd memory player-mute-scope-decision): mute is PER-SESSION — kept across episode handoff/autoplay within one session, deliberately lost on stop (stop → resume or different item starts unmuted). PR #39 reverts #38, documents the decision at both useState sites, and pins the reset with a regression test. This resolves jphh as hypothesis (b): the A3 stop→play expectation itself was wrong; in-mount handoff retention (the strict A3 variant) was already correct and remains covered by reveal-mute test (a).

title: Security: install syft + grype (mise), generate SBOMs, scan and triage vulnerabilities
description:
Add open-source supply-chain visibility to Prexu: syft (SBOM generation) and grype (vulnerability scanning) from Anchore. We want standing awareness of vulnerable dependencies across all three dependency surfaces: npm (frontend), cargo in src-tauri (including the vendored wry fork — vendor/wry won't appear in a registry-based scan, so scan the source tree, not just lockfiles), and cargo in relay-server.

## Scope
1. Install both tools via mise (mise.toml tool entries with platform asset patterns, consistent with how beads/dolt are pinned) — not curl|bash into PATH.
2. Generate SBOMs: syft against the repo (catalogers for package-lock.json + both Cargo.lock files) — emit CycloneDX or SPDX, decide and document which.
3. Scan: grype against the SBOMs; also scan a built AppImage/binary artifact if practical.
4. Triage the initial findings: classify true/false positive, severity, and whether fixes are available (npm audit fix / cargo update candidates). File follow-up beads for anything actionable rather than fixing in-line.
5. Wire a repeatable entrypoint: a mise task (e.g. 'mise run vuln-scan') so this is one command; evaluate adding a non-blocking CI job (report-only at first — do NOT make it a required check until the baseline is triaged).
6. Document in README or docs/: how to run, where reports land, triage policy.

## Suggested Skills
- security: triage policy, severity assessment
- mise: tool pinning + task definition
- nushell: report post-processing if needed
acceptance_criteria: syft + grype pinned via mise and runnable by any contributor with one command; SBOMs generated for npm + both cargo trees (vendored wry included); initial grype scan completed with findings triaged and follow-up beads filed for actionable items; repeatable mise task committed; docs updated.

title: [GATED] Playback feature parity: map player command surface to libmpv on Linux
description:
GATED on axj4.3. Map the full player command surface to libmpv on Linux and reuse the existing TS player hooks (usePlayer + sub-hooks): play/pause/seek, volume/mute, audio + subtitle track selection, fullscreen, playback rate, and Plex timeline reporting. Keep the Tauri command names identical across engines so the React layer is engine-agnostic.

## Suggested Skills
rust, react, prexu, tauri

## Acceptance
All player controls + timeline reporting work via libmpv on Linux at parity with the HTML5 path.
notes: ON-HARDWARE TEST PLAN RESULTS (2026-07-03, Wayland, HEVC 10-bit, dev build): A1 A2 A4 A5 A6 PASS; A3 FAIL/unclear (mute lost across stop->next-play — filed as follow-up bug); M1 M2 M3 M4 M5 PASS; M6 FAIL (expand does not restore — prexu-skr2, instrumented + busy/ready parity fix in working tree, awaiting repro); M7 M8 NOT YET RUN (do after M6 fix confirmed); R1 R2 R3 R4 PASS; R5 skipped (no Windows box in this session; popout correctly absent on Linux). Session also found+fixed (merged PR #34): exit freeze (detach GL context + card-enter fill livelock), splash hard-cap false warning. Open follow-ups: prexu-91t8 (reveal gap), prexu-skr2 (M6), A3 mute persistence, exit-latency polish, click-to-pause delay.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: RE-REVIEW note: minimize/mini-player + popout are NOT in this task's listed surface but ARE invoked by PlayerContext on the native path. axj4.4 gates them to Windows short-term; this task should implement Linux minimize via mpv video-margin-ratio-* options (no widget geometry needed) and decide popout scope (separate GTK window with own GLArea, or defer). Fullscreen on Linux = Tauri window.set_fullscreen (no host geometry).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: POLISH OBSERVATION (2026-07-02, Linux fix-A verification launch): ~1s of audible audio under the loading screen before the first video frame reveal. Expected mechanics (loading UI holds until player://host-window-ready; mpv audio starts before first video frame decode) and the reveal itself is now clean post-compositing-fix — but consider whether audio should stay muted until reveal. Likely identical on Windows (same handshake) → cross-platform polish decision for the parity pass, not a Linux defect.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: SCOPING DECISIONS (user, 2026-07-02): (1) POPOUT DEFERRED — gate the popout control off on Linux; follow-up bead filed under the epic for a second GTK window + GLArea. (2) AUDIO-UNDER-LOADING is LINUX-ONLY (user corrects the earlier 'likely identical on Windows' hypothesis — Windows does not exhibit it). Treat as a Linux defect in this parity pass: hold audio muted (or equivalent) until the player://host-window-ready reveal completes. (3) Minimize/mini-player on Linux via mpv video-margin-ratio-* as previously decided.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: PARITY AUDIT (2026-07-02, pre-implementation): Linux playback surface is ALREADY at parity — all 18 playback commands registered in both lib.rs handler blocks and every TS native-path invoke maps to a registered Linux command (SUPPORTS_PLAYER_WINDOWING kept the Windows-only surface unreachable). Fullscreen works via Tauri set_fullscreen (Linux needs none of Windows' host-geometry resync). Timeline reporting cross-platform. REAL GAPS: (1) minimize/mini-player — implement via video-margin-ratio-* computed from the stored logical-px MinimizeState against the GLArea allocation, recomputed on resize; compile minimize.rs on Linux (its non-Windows 'clear error' stubs are currently dead code — commands/mod.rs gates the module to Windows). (2) popout — deferred to prexu-axj4.10. (3) audio-under-loading (Linux-only per user) — TS-side mute before player_load_url, restore user mute on player://host-window-ready. NON-GAP: playback rate — no rate control exists on ANY path incl. HTML5; dropping from parity acceptance.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: IMPLEMENTATION COMPLETE (2026-07-03, PR #33): (1) Linux in-window minimize via video-margin-ratio-* — minimize.rs compiled on Linux with real command bodies (same names/signatures + busy/ready contract as Windows), pure compute_margin_ratios with 18 unit tests (all corners, defaults, degenerate-window clamps), margins applied via bounded main-thread dispatch and recomputed live in the GLArea resize handler; registered in the Linux invoke_handler. (2) TS gate split: SUPPORTS_PLAYER_MINIMIZE (Win+Linux) / SUPPORTS_PLAYER_POPOUT (Win-only, axj4.10); all consumers re-gated, PiP mutual-exclusion stays on the popout side. (3) Reveal-mute: Rust arms mute in player_load_url post-ensure_init (covers cold first load — TS pre-load invoke can't reach mpv before init), TS restores latest user mute intent on player://host-window-ready with HOST_READY_FALLBACK_MS safety net; mid-load toggles deferred through an applyMuted funnel. GATES ALL GREEN: tsc, vitest 1777/1777, cargo check/clippy -D warnings/test 99/99, relay-server check. PENDING BEFORE CLOSE: on-hardware verify — (a) minimize lands in corner + tracks resize + letterboxes correctly, (b) exit restores full-frame, (c) loading screen silent until reveal on cold first load AND autoplay handoff, (d) mute toggle during loading honors latest intent at reveal.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
ON-HARDWARE TEST PLAN (2026-07-03, run against main after PR #33 merges, Wayland session, dev build for debug+trace logs, HEVC 10-bit item; X11 out of scope → prexu-5jxx). Report pass/fail per ID.
REVEAL-MUTE: A1 cold first load — loading screen silent, audio only at reveal (grep 'reveal-mute armed (linux)'). A2 autoplay handoff silent between episodes. A3 start-while-muted stays muted after reveal. A4 mute toggle during loading — no audio pre-reveal, latest intent wins at reveal, UI icon updates instantly. A5 volume drag during loading (auto-unmute path) — no early audio. A6 post-reveal mute toggles respond instantly.
MINIMIZE: M1 enter — video letterboxed inside corner mask hole, no bleed/black band, UI interactive. M2 browse while mini — playback continues. M3 drag/geometry updates smooth, no busy/ready flicker per tick. M4 main-window resize/maximize/tile while mini — rect stays corner-anchored at same px size (grep 'resize while minimized — recomputed ratios'). M5 pause+resize — paused frame redraws clean, no stale bands. M6 exit — instant full frame, no residual shrink. M7 stop-while-mini then new playback — full frame, no stale margins. M8 fullscreen toggle before/after using minimize.
REGRESSION: R1 no popout/PiP affordance on Linux, minimize present. R2 pause/unpause click, seek, track switching, volume. R3 controls auto-hide ~3s + crisp fade (PR #32 log-level change must be behavior-neutral). R4 Plex timeline progress advances. R5 optional Windows minimize+popout smoke.

title: [GATED] Engine selection + HTML5 fallback + Settings toggle
description:
GATED on axj4.2. Native libmpv is the DEFAULT engine on Linux with graceful fallback to the HTML5 <video> path when: libmpv.so is missing, the render path is unsupported on the current compositor/GPU, or the user toggles it. Add a Settings toggle. Detect-and-fallback must be robust (no black screen / crash — fall back cleanly).

## Suggested Skills
react, rust, tauri, prexu

## Acceptance
Native plays by default on a supported box; forcing libmpv absent or render-unsupported falls back to HTML5 with no crash; Settings toggle switches engines.
close_reason: Engine selection/fallback/Settings toggle shipped in PR #31; controls auto-hide hardware-verified; attribution logging kept permanently with mousemove re-arm demoted to trace (PR #32)

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
RE-REVIEW (2026-07-02) — decisions locked for implementation:
(1) 'libmpv.so missing → fallback' via dlopen is REJECTED: it would forfeit reuse of the entire libmpv2-typed Windows core (commands/events). Instead libmpv presence is guaranteed by packaging (AppImage bundles libmpv.so, rpm declares the dep — axj4.7), mirroring Windows' vendored libmpv-2.dll. Fallback contract narrows to: render/GL init failure at runtime + user Settings toggle. Epic acceptance wording updated accordingly.
(2) Engine selection today is a module-level constant (usePlayer.ts:45 IS_NATIVE_PLAYER, Windows-UA gate) consumed by Player.tsx/PlayerOverlay/usePlayerLifecycle + 3 test files. Becomes: platform-capable (Windows OR Linux) AND prefs playerEngine (auto|native|html5, default auto) AND no session-fallback flag. Must stay stable per Player mount (rules of hooks).
(3) Contract with axj4.3 Rust side: Linux registers the playback command subset + player_engine_status → {available, reason}; render-init failure emits player://engine-failed (string payload) → TS sets session fallback, remounts to HTML5.
(4) NEW GAP: minimize/mini-player/popout commands are Windows-only window management; PlayerContext invokes them whenever native. TS must gate those behind a SUPPORTS_PLAYER_WINDOWING (Windows-only) constant so Linux native doesn't call unregistered commands. Full Linux mini-player parity (mpv video-margin-ratio approach) belongs to axj4.5.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: ENGINE-ENG round 2 complete (gates green, 1767 tests): controls auto-hide — exact-coordinate mousemove guard was insufficient on hardware; replaced with 5px anchored displacement threshold (slow deliberate movement accumulates, bounded jitter never crosses) + TEMPORARY diagnostic logging (grep 'controls-visibility'; each timer re-arm logs its reason). Residual suspect if still pinning: player://paused flapping re-firing show-on-transition (Rust side) — the logs will attribute it. On-hardware confirmation pending next app run.

title: [GATED] Render-API integration: libmpv render context + frame loop on Linux
description:
GATED on axj4.1 (spike) + axj4.2 (ADR) — detail locks after the architecture is proven. Wire the libmpv render context (GL / gpu-next, hwdec=auto-safe) onto the spike-proven surface: create render context, drive the render loop / frame callbacks, handle resize + DPI + teardown. Linux x11 + wayland. Mirror the structure of the Windows player module where it makes sense; this is a second rendering backend (render API), not the wid path.

## Suggested Skills
rust, cpp, async, tauri

## Acceptance
mpv decodes + renders into the app surface on Linux with no leaks/crashes on resize/teardown; HEVC 10-bit plays smoothly.
close_reason: Acceptance met on Wayland: render context + frame loop shipped in PR #31, all smoke defects root-caused (DMABUF retained-composite) and hardware-verified; X11 verification split to prexu-5jxx

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
RE-REVIEW (2026-07-02, pre-implementation) — plan confirmed GO with refinements:
(1) REUSE: use the libmpv2 crate on Linux (NOT the spike's raw FFI). Windows already drives libmpv2::render::RenderContext over OpenGL/ANGLE (video_render.rs:31) — same API GtkGLArea needs. Un-gating mod.rs/events.rs/lifecycle.rs/timeline.rs/commands/playback.rs gives the whole command surface + event pump nearly free. Cargo: [target.'cfg(target_os="linux")'.dependencies] libmpv2 v4 WITHOUT build_libmpv (links system libmpv); gtk 0.18 (v3_24) + webkit2gtk 2.0 (v2_40) match Tauri's transitive versions.
(2) TRANSPARENCY GAP (new finding): tauri.linux.conf.json has transparent:false, so wry does NOT set the WebKitWebView background transparent (vendor/wry webkitgtk/mod.rs:289 only does it when attributes.transparent). The spike set it manually. axj4.3 MUST call webkit2gtk WebView::set_background_color(RGBA 0,0,0,0) on the real webview at setup or the webview paints opaque over the GLArea.
(3) REPARENTING is the one integration point the spike did NOT test (PoC built its own widget tree): move wry's vbox/webview under a GtkOverlay with GLArea as base, via gtk_window()/default_vbox() on the main thread. Do this FIRST and prove UI still renders before wiring mpv.
(4) EFFICIENCY: run the ~60Hz queue_render timer ONLY while a file is loaded/playing; pair with mpv update-callback via glib channel (fix the spike's Box::into_raw sender leak).
(5) Teardown ordering: free RenderContext in GLArea unrealize BEFORE mpv_terminate_destroy (same ordering bug class as prexu-60mz.4 on Windows).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
ON-HARDWARE SMOKE (2026-07-02): pipeline works end-to-end — compositor install → transparent webview → reparent → render ctx → HEVC 10-bit via hwdec=nvdec, frames under React chrome, time-pos advancing. TWO DEFECTS found by user during smoke:
(1) VIDEO VERY DARK (UI normal brightness). NOT HDR (stream is SDR bt709/tv-range per Plex metadata). Discriminator: spike testsrc evidence (swdec 8-bit) is FULLY BRIGHT through the same GLArea path; spike HEVC/NVDEC evidence was also near-black (pre-existing since spike, unnoticed). Hypothesis: 10-bit NVDEC→GL interop (P010/16-bit alignment or colour-range) — suspect magnitude ~1/64 multiply. A/B plan: hwdec=no vs nvdec vs nvdec-copy, compare mean luminance vs an mpv-CLI reference frame.
(2) RESIZE ARTIFACTS: resizing mid-playback leaves persistent mismatched frame bands/seams (stale regions at different scales). Suspect GLArea buffer reallocation vs mpv render size, and/or needing a forced re-render on size-allocate (pump gating may also starve redraws when paused).
Fix + on-hardware re-verify assigned back to render-eng.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: SMOKE DEFECT #5 (2026-07-02 09:49): pause→unpause click crashed the app (SIGABRT). Coredump: Rust panic inside a gtk connect_button_press_event trampoline (panic_cannot_unwind → abort) — panic in a GTK signal-handler closure added during the darkening/resize debug iteration (not in the first-delivery linux_compositor.rs). Suspect RefCell double-borrow or unwrap in the click handler. Fix requirement recorded with render-eng: all GTK signal closures must be panic-free by construction (try_borrow + logged early-return, no unwrap); repro re-test pause/unpause on hardware. Also outstanding from user testing: controls never auto-hide (with engine-eng, TS side) and audio blown out (with render-eng, likely multichannel downmix clipping — user notes EasyEffects caveat).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: ROOT-CAUSE CONVERGENCE (2026-07-02, user live-testing): defects #1 (progressive darkening) + video-invisible-until-resize + likely #2 (resize artifacts) unify into ONE compositing bug. Decisive user evidence: (a) audio+controls fine at playback start but NO video until a resize, despite logs proving mpv rendered 60/s from attach; (b) the DOM loading screen ALSO progressively darkened; (c) after resize, video pops at full brightness then immediately re-dims; (d) a left-edge sliver stays brighter (region outside the scrim element). Model: WebKitGTK created with attributes.transparent=false marks itself opaque at creation; runtime webkit set_background_color(0,0,0,0) does NOT flip its opaque/compositing state. GTK/GDK culls the GLArea blit beneath the 'opaque' webview (video only reaches screen on full redraws like resize) and WebKit's translucent repaints (controls scrim at ~4Hz, pinned visible by defect #4) blend repeatedly onto the stale retained buffer → exponential dimming. Fix directions dispatched to render-eng: (A, preferred) creation-time webview-only transparency via the existing vendor/wry fork (mirror the Windows composition-hosting opt-in; toplevel stays transparent:false per prexu-duna Wayland-bleed), (B) a runtime API that truly flips WebKit opaque state, (C) full-overlay queue_draw per frame as diagnostic/bandaid to confirm the model.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
RENDER-ENG CHECKPOINT (2026-07-02, post-limit-reset) — tree coherent, all cargo gates green (check/clippy -D warnings/test 81/81):
FIXED+HW-VERIFIED — crash (defect #5): tauri-runtime-wry undecorated_resizing.rs:546 attaches a Linux button-press handler doing webview.parent().parent().downcast::<gtk::Window>().unwrap(); the original window→overlay→vbox→webview tree made the grandparent a GtkOverlay → every click panicked in a GTK trampoline → abort. Fix: webview pulled OUT of wry's vbox, added as direct overlay child (window→overlay→{GLArea, WebView}); invariant restored; 6+min playback with clicks, no crash. INVARIANT for future work: webview's grandparent MUST be the GtkWindow (or vendor tauri-runtime-wry).
FIXED (GL side, measured) — darkening #1 partial: render handler now clears with scissor disabled + forces alpha opaque per frame (mpv leaves scissor/FBO state dirty — re-bind before post-render GL). GL buffer provably fresh+opaque every frame. RESIDUAL dimming (gray-mean 0.110→0.024 over 60s, corrected pipeline: spectacle needs -alpha off before -colorspace Gray) is ABOVE the GL layer — WebKit retained surface blending semi-transparent content over the previous composite, per the opaque-at-creation model. NEXT: fix A = vendored-wry Linux pending-flag forcing attributes.transparent=true at webview creation (mirrors Windows set_pending_composition_hosting; wry mod.rs:289 then does creation-time set_background_color); fix B = gdk_window_set_opaque_region(empty) post-realize + full-overlay queue_draw. PREXU_NO_WEBVIEW=1 env isolates GL-only rendering.
PLAUSIBLY FIXED (needs soak) — resize artifacts #2: per-frame clear + connect_resize→queue_render (paused repaints at new allocation).
FIXED (one cause) — audio #3: file is AAC 5.1; mpv unnormalized 5.1→stereo downmix clips. Set audio-channels=auto-safe + audio-normalize-downmix=yes (Linux-gated, validated against libmpv 2.5.0). Cause 2: user's persisted volume=200 → mpv soft-vol 200 (+6dB) — re-test at ≤100, EasyEffects bypassed. Windows parity filed as separate bead.
HARDENING: all GTK closures panic-free (try_borrow + logged early-return) — panic in any GTK signal aborts the process.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: MODEL CONFIRMATION #4 (user, 2026-07-02 ~13:15): controls auto-hide LOGIC verified working via diagnostics (re-arms only on genuine displacement; 'auto-hide fired' 3s after pointer settled; NO isPlaying-transition flapping — that suspect is cleared). But VISUALLY the hidden controls lingered as ghost pixels and 'faded away over minutes, like the video' — the CSS fade painted onto the stale retained composite still holding the visible controls; only the multiplicative darkening erased them. Fourth face of the single opaque-webview compositing root cause (with video-invisible-until-resize, progressive dimming, resize bands). Verification criterion added to fix A: crisp sub-second chrome fade, no ghosting.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: USER CONFIRMATIONS (2026-07-02 ~13:20): (1) pause/unpause crash fix VERIFIED by user on hardware — play/pause works. (2) Audio at mid-slider (~100) no longer blown out with downmix normalization in — loud-scene confirmation pending. (3) New retained-buffer evidence: stale pause icon ghosts behind the play icon after toggling — icon swaps leave uncleared pixels, same compositing fingerprint at pixel scale. All remaining visual defects await the vendored-wry creation-time transparency fix (in progress).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: AUDIO STATUS (user, loud-scene check): still slightly blown out at louder sections with downmix normalization in and volume at mid-slider (~100, 0dB soft-vol). Attribution pending: user testing EasyEffects tweaks; suggested A/B = bypass EasyEffects once (clean⇒their chain clips; still hot⇒our side) and/or enable Prexu's built-in normalization preset (Settings→Playback; session log showed af_chain=off) which compresses mpv-side before EasyEffects. Note: theatrical mixes are inherently hot-peaked; not necessarily a player defect. Keep open on the defect board until attributed.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: AUDIO ATTRIBUTED + CLOSED (user): residual loud-section clipping is the user's EasyEffects chain — an ~+11dB boost with a limiter (threshold now -4dB) downstream; +11dB clips any full-scale theatrical mix regardless of player output. Player-side defect (unnormalized 5.1→stereo downmix) remains genuinely fixed via audio-channels=auto-safe + audio-normalize-downmix=yes. In-app normalization preset (Settings→Playback) documented as the no-EasyEffects alternative. Defect #3 closed on the player side; Windows parity tracked separately.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: CLOSING (2026-07-02): all defects resolved and hardware-verified. Final root cause of the compositing cluster: WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 (our z5mz workaround) — WebKit's fallback renderer never clears the transparent webview's retained composite. Fixes shipped: forced disable removed (z5mz crash no longer reproduces; env escape hatch respected+warned), creation-time webview transparency via vendored-wry set_pending_webview_transparency (necessary contract), opaque-region cleared on realize/size-allocate, per-frame GL clear + forced-opaque alpha, resize→queue_render, panic-free GTK closures, webview as direct overlay child (tauri-runtime-wry grandparent invariant), audio downmix normalization. Verified: brightness within 1.3% of ffmpeg source truth, flat over 60s, 10-resize soak clean (222 resize events), controls fade to 0.000 no ghosts, pause/unpause crash-free (user), HEVC 10-bit via nvdec smooth (user: 'video and audio are great'). ADR amended with the DMABUF finding. Remaining scope moved out: X11 session verification → prexu-5jxx; DMABUF-crash risk on other GPU stacks documented (first-run probe idea) — watch reports. Acceptance met on the target (Wayland) session.

title: ADR: render-API native player architecture (docs/adr-native-player-render-api.md)
description:
Write the architecture decision record from the spike findings, superseding the Linux NO-GO in docs/adr-native-player-cross-platform.md.

## Cover
- Chosen compositing approach (from the spike) + why; Wayland + X11.
- Render loop / threading model; resize + DPI handling.
- Input model (UI input goes to the React webview on top; video surface passive).
- HW decode plan (VAAPI / NVDEC) and hwdec mode.
- libmpv distribution + LGPL (dynamic link; AppImage bundles libmpv.so, rpm declares dep).
- Engine selection + HTML5 fallback contract.
- Must-cover codec scope from prexu-duna.3 (HEVC 8/10-bit, AV1, AC3/E-AC3/TrueHD/DTS).

## Suggested Skills
documentation, rust, tauri, anti-fabrication

## Acceptance
- docs/adr-native-player-render-api.md committed; records the proven approach + codec scope; cross-platform ADR updated to point at it for Linux.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: composite mpv render-API frames under the webview on Wayland (PoC or dead-end)
description:
THE make-or-break unknown. mpv's render API (libmpv/render.h) hands us decoded frames in a caller-owned GL context (vo=gpu-next equivalent). On Wayland there is NO wid embedding, so we must get those frames to display UNDER the transparent Tauri/wry webview (React UI on top). Prove it works or document a hard dead-end.

## Investigate (candidate approaches)
- Wayland **subsurface** placed below the webview surface (does wry/Tauri expose the toplevel wl_surface? can we attach a wl_subsurface?).
- A **separate GL surface/window** the compositor stacks under the Tauri window (Wayland has no client-side stacking — likely needs subsurface).
- Offscreen FBO + present into the page (canvas/WebGL/texture stream) — likely too slow; measure if considered.
- Confirm the chosen path also works on **X11** (fallback session).

## Output
- A minimal build that shows an mpv-decoded video frame rendered BENEATH the React overlay on a live Wayland session (screenshot/recording), OR a documented dead-end naming the exact blocker.
- A go/no-go recommendation + the approach to carry into the ADR.

## Suggested Skills
rust, tauri, cpp (mpv C render API), anti-fabrication

## Acceptance
- Render-API frame visible under the webview on Wayland (and note X11), OR a definitive blocker documented with evidence. Threading/resize behavior noted.
notes:
NEXT-SESSION KICKOFF PROMPT (added 2026-06-30):

Goal: prove (or definitively refute) that mpv's render API (libmpv/render.h, GL / gpu-next) can composite decoded video frames UNDERNEATH the transparent Tauri/wry webview on a Wayland session. No --wid embedding exists on Wayland, so the likely path is a wl_subsurface placed below the toplevel wl_surface — investigate whether wry/Tauri exposes that surface and whether we can attach our own subsurface + GL context. Note X11 behavior too.

Make it a THROWAWAY PoC, not production code — put it under spike/ alongside the existing spike/dcomp-* dirs (Windows DComp spikes; mirror that style). Deliverable: either a minimal build showing an mpv-decoded frame rendered beneath a React/HTML overlay on live Wayland (capture a screenshot), OR a documented dead-end naming the exact blocker — plus a go/no-go recommendation and the approach to carry into the ADR (axj4.2). Everything downstream is gated on this; don't over-build.

Environment (the verified box — actually run it, don't reason about it):
- Nobara/Fedora 43, KDE Plasma Wayland, NVIDIA GPU. System libmpv-dev installed; nvh264dec/nvh265dec/avdec_h265 present.
- App already sets WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 on Linux (z5mz) — webview needs it; keep in mind if the spike touches GL/EGL on NVIDIA.
- Launch with 'npm run tauri dev'; cargo on PATH via ~/.cargo/bin. Plex signed in; davfs media mounts at /mnt/bad-dong-serv/{16tb,22tb,24tb} (mount with 'mount /mnt/bad-dong-serv/16tb' if not mounted; they're noauto).

Codec must-cover scope (from duna.3): HEVC 8/10-bit, AV1, AC3/E-AC3/TrueHD/DTS — all transcode on HTML5 today; native must direct-play them.

Process: load core skills + rust/tauri/cpp, work on a feature branch, claim this issue first. Spike = tests optional, but the finding MUST be real (run on hardware, capture evidence) per /core:anti-fabrication.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text:
SPIKE RESULT: GO. Proven on hardware (Nobara/F43, KDE Plasma Wayland, RTX 5090 / driver 595.71.05, libmpv 2.5.0, webkit2gtk-4.1 2.52.3, gtk 3.24.52).

KEY INSIGHT: the Wayland NO-GO premise was wrong. On Linux wry packs the webview as a WebKitWebView GTK *widget* in a gtk::Box inside gtk::ApplicationWindow (wry-0.54.2 webkitgtk/mod.rs:290 sets webview bg RGBA(0,0,0,0) for transparent). The whole window is ONE wl_surface — no --wid to embed under, no wl_subsurface needed. Compositing reduces to GTK widget stacking: GtkOverlay { base: GtkGLArea (mpv render-API target), overlay: transparent WebKitWebView }. Windowing-agnostic (works the same on X11).

EVIDENCE (spike/wayland-render-compositing/evidence/): lavfi testsrc AND a real HEVC 10-bit library file (Nosferatu.2024.x265.10bit) both direct-play under the transparent React/HTML overlay with true alpha. mpv log confirms hardware decode: '[vd] Trying hardware decoding via hevc-nvdec / Selected decoder: hevc' — NVDEC, not software. Codec-scope (duna.3) decodes through the render API.

GOTCHAS for axj4.3: (1) query GL_FRAMEBUFFER_BINDING 0x8CA6 NOT 0x8CA9 (=GL_DRAW_FRAMEBUFFER target, glGetIntegerv rejects it -> fbo 0 -> black). (2) call gtk_gl_area_attach_buffers() before reading the FBO. (3) drive redraws with a ~60Hz glib timeout->queue_render (mpv update-callback alone didn't pump). (4) setlocale(LC_NUMERIC,C) before mpv_create. (5) WEBKIT_DISABLE_DMABUF_RENDERER=1 is compatible. Resize/HiDPI handled by GTK allocation (no Win32-style geometry sync). X11 not run on this Wayland box — low risk, verify as a checkbox in axj4.3.

Full writeup: spike/wayland-render-compositing/FINDINGS.md. Next: axj4.2 ADR records this architecture.

title: [epic] Phase 2: native libmpv render-API player on Linux (HTML5 fallback)
description:
Build a native libmpv-backed player on **Linux** using the mpv **render API** (libmpv/render.h — caller-owned GL surface), so HEVC 8/10-bit, AV1, and AC3/E-AC3/TrueHD/DTS direct-play instead of forcing a Plex transcode. Supersedes the ADR's Linux NO-GO (the wid path only works on X11; render API is the only Wayland-viable approach). Evidence: prexu-duna.3 codec-gap (HTML5 direct-plays ONLY H.264/AAC; ~17% of movies transcode on video, ~170 on audio).

## Decisions (2026-06-30 scoping session)
- SCOPE: **Linux only**. macOS (blocked on Apple-Silicon hardware) and the Windows wid->render-API migration are **separate later epics**.
- STRUCTURE: **spike-first, gated**. The make-or-break unknown is compositing mpv render-API frames UNDER the transparent Tauri/wry webview on Wayland (no wid embedding on Wayland). The epic opens with a compositing spike + a new ADR; downstream implementation tasks are sketched but GATED on the spike outcome (don't lock their detail until the architecture is proven).
- ENGINE: native libmpv is the **default on Linux** with **graceful HTML5 <video> fallback** (libmpv.so missing / render path unsupported on a compositor-GPU / user toggle) exposed as a Settings toggle. Mirrors how Windows degrades.

## Team (models by complexity, haiku-first where possible)
- lead (opus): orchestration, architecture/ADR decisions, go/no-go gate
- spike-eng (opus): render-compositing PoC on Wayland/X11 — highest uncertainty
- render-eng (opus->sonnet): render-context + frame-loop integration (post-spike)
- engine-eng (sonnet): engine selection + HTML5 fallback + Settings toggle
- parity-eng (sonnet): map player command surface to libmpv; reuse TS hooks
- bundle-eng (haiku): libmpv.so distribution (AppImage bundle + rpm dep) + CI provisioning
- validation (haiku): tsc/vitest/clippy/cargo gates + on-hardware codec re-verify

## Core skills all members load first
core:anti-fabrication, core:git, core:documentation, core:security, core:mise, core:nushell

## Reference
docs/adr-native-player-cross-platform.md (ADR being superseded for Linux), docs/native-player-status.md, prexu-duna.3 notes (codec gap = must-cover scope), native-player-cross-platform-direction memory.

## Acceptance (epic)
- Spike proves (or definitively refutes) render-API compositing under the webview on Wayland; new ADR records the chosen architecture.
- Native player direct-plays the duna.3 codec set on Linux (no transcode), with HTML5 fallback intact.
- libmpv dynamically linked + LGPL-clean; AppImage bundles it, rpm declares the dep; CI builds it on Linux.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: WINDOWS-CONVERGENCE NOTE (from axj4.1/.2 review): once this Linux render-API path lands, migrating Windows off wid onto the same render API would unify frame production (one mpv_render_context + FBO loop + frame driver + threading) across all platforms and RETIRE the Win32 SetWindowPos geometry-sync machinery (host_window.rs). The 'composite webview over video' glue stays per-platform but becomes DECLARATIVE z-order instead of manual geometry chasing: Linux=GtkOverlay(GtkGLArea under WebKitWebView); Windows=mpv into a D3D11/ANGLE texture in the SAME DirectComposition visual tree as a composition-hosted WebView2 (already prototyped in spike/dcomp-overlay + spike/webview2-hosting + spike/wv2-tauri-ipc); macOS=Metal/CAOpenGLLayer under WKWebView. Capture parity (Alt+Tab/WGC/taskbar thumbnail) becomes STRUCTURAL on Windows too — one DComp tree captures video+UI together, the same single-composited-buffer property Wayland gives for free (vs the engineering the wid build needed). TRADE-OFF: render path means mpv no longer owns an OS window, so Windows must take on input forwarding + cursor mapping (SystemCursorId->LoadCursor, see composition-host notes) + IME/composition parity (tracked as prexu-x2bt). Net: simpler in the dimensions that hurt most (geometry/capture), new work in input/cursor/IME. This is the convergence end-goal already stated in the native-player-cross-platform-direction memory (all platforms on one codepath; host_window.rs/Win32 geometry retires). Sequence it as a SEPARATE later epic after the Linux epic proves the render path end-to-end.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: CONVERGENCE PAYOFF NOTE (2026-07-04, from prexu-hg1j): Windows is ALREADY on the render API (vo=libmpv render context feeding the DComp video visual, no wid — prexu-zfyi), so the wid->render-API line in the convergence comment above is about unifying frame-production code, not transparency. The transparency choreography (busy/ready emits, useTransparentWindow's transition shield + 2xrAF paint-waits, prepareNavAway's early opaque paint, restoreFromMinimize's wait-for-IPC flip) exists ONLY because the Windows HWND is transparent:true with no full-window opaque layer — pixels covered by neither webview UI nor video visual fall through to the OS desktop during transitions. Linux never leaks: toplevel transparent=false + the GLArea base layer clears opaque every frame, which is why prexu-hg1j (PR #40) gated all of it off there. WHEN THIS EPIC'S WINDOWS LEG LANDS: add an opaque full-window backdrop visual at the bottom of the DComp tree (mirror of the GLArea's opaque clear) and/or drop transparent:true on the HWND (composition-hosted WebView2 resolves its transparency inside the DComp tree, so HWND-level per-pixel alpha may no longer be needed) — then DELETE the whole choreography app-wide, including the Rust busy/ready emits. Caveats: (a) popout is a separate OS window on Windows with its own busy/ready bracketing — handle separately; (b) the restore wait-for-IPC rationale cites SetWindowPos taking hundreds of ms, likely stale now that restore is DComp set_video_dest — measure; the optimistic flip may be safe on Windows even before the backdrop lands.

title: Continue Watching context menu: already-watched items only offer 'Mark as Unwatched', not both
description:
In the Continue Watching row, an item that was ALREADY fully watched before this play session (viewCount>=1) but now has resume progress (viewOffset>0) shows only 'Mark as Unwatched' in its context menu. Items that were NOT previously watched but have progress correctly show BOTH 'Mark as Watched' and 'Mark as Unwatched'. Observed on 21 Jump Street, 2 Fast 2 Furious, The 'Burbs (all previously watched) during the prexu-duna.3 Wayland test.

## Root cause
src/hooks/useMediaContextMenu.tsx ~L103-145. The toggle is binary on hasView (item.viewCount):
- if (hasView)  -> push ONLY 'Mark as Unwatched'
- else          -> push 'Mark as Watched', and IF hasProgress (viewOffset) ALSO push 'Mark as Unwatched'
The 'also offer the other action for partially-watched items' branch exists only in the UNWATCHED case. So a watched item that ALSO has resume progress (i.e. anything in Continue Watching that was previously watched) never gets 'Mark as Watched'.

## Suggested fix
Make it symmetric: when hasView && hasProgress (watched but has a resume point), also offer 'Mark as Watched' (clears the resume/marks complete) alongside 'Mark as Unwatched'. Net behavior: an in-progress item always offers both actions regardless of prior watched state. Add/adjust unit tests in the existing useMediaContextMenu / WatchedToggle test files.

## Required Skills
react, prexu
close_reason: Fixed in #29 (merged): watchedToggleActions() makes the Continue Watching context menu offer both Mark as Watched + Mark as Unwatched for any in-progress item, regardless of prior watched state. Unit tested + verified live on Wayland (marking an already-watched in-progress movie clears it from Continue Watching).

title: cfg-gate non-Windows dead-code warnings so Linux CI can use clippy -D warnings
description: The non-Windows src-tauri build emits ~18 warnings (unused imports like tokio::sync::Notify/Manager, unused consts/fields/fns such as CLOSE_REPORT_JOIN_BUDGET, stopped_report_request, fire_stopped_report, GeomState fields). They're Windows-path code that's inert on Linux. CI currently gates with plain 'cargo build' to avoid failing on them. Properly cfg-gate (or #[cfg_attr(not(windows), allow(...))]) the unused items, then tighten the Linux CI step from 'cargo build' to 'cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings' to match the Windows headless-mpv job's strictness. Acceptance: clippy -D warnings is clean on Linux and wired into ci.yml.
close_reason: Import-gated/split Windows-only imports; cfg_attr(not(windows),allow(dead_code)) on inert consts/fields/fns/methods (no deletions, struct layout + Windows behavior unchanged). ci.yml Linux step tightened to 'cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings'. Verified clean on Linux.

title: Validation: tsc + vitest + clippy + Linux cargo build gates green
description: Run the strictest gates after Phase 1 changes: npx tsc --noEmit, npx vitest run, cd relay-server && cargo clippy, and a Linux src-tauri cargo build (env has system libmpv). Feed failures back to the owning task. Acceptance: all gates pass on the feature branch.
close_reason: Closed

title: Re-add Linux/macOS release legs with AppImage+rpm bundle targets
description:
Once non-Windows builds (prexu-nesp, done), re-enable the macOS/Linux release matrix legs in release.yml (trimmed to Windows-only in PR #22).

Linux specifics:
- Set Linux bundle.targets explicitly to [appimage, rpm] (not 'all'); drop .deb unless wanted. AppImage is the portable format for the Fedora daily driver; rpm is the native Fedora package.
- Building .rpm needs rpmbuild on the ubuntu runner — install it (apt-get install rpm) or the rpm bundle step fails.
- macOS leg additionally needs libmpv only if/when the native player ports there (still deferred) — for now it builds the HTML5 shell.
- Keep build runner on ubuntu-22.04 (glibc 2.35 baseline) for forward-compat with Fedora.

See docs/linux-dev.md. Runtime testing happens on the user's Fedora box; this task is just CI/packaging wiring.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fully gate player module for non-Windows builds (link error: -lmpv)
description:
Non-Windows release legs (ubuntu, macos x2) fail at link: 'rust-lld: error: unable to find library -lmpv'. Root cause: Cargo.toml line 40 declares libmpv2 in the BASE [dependencies], so libmpv2-sys emits cargo:rustc-link-lib=mpv on every platform. All libmpv2 use sites are under src-tauri/src/player/ but 'mod player;' (lib.rs:2) and player/{mod,lifecycle,events}.rs reference libmpv2 ungated, so the player compiles on non-Windows and forces the -lmpv link. macOS/Linux runners have no system libmpv.

Fix (for Linux parity): gate the entire player module + its command registrations behind #[cfg(target_os="windows")] and move libmpv2 out of base deps to Windows-only, so non-Windows builds the HTML5-only shell with no mpv link. Then macOS/Linux installers can build. Part of the Linux native-parity track. Windows release is unaffected (builds + bundles fine).
close_reason: Closed

title: composition mini-player grow-resize shows black/app-behind until async swapchain rebuild lands
description: When resizing the mini-player LARGER under composition hosting, the video lags the resize — the DComp video visual exposes the new (bigger) inset region immediately, but mpv's D3D11 swapchain is rebuilt asynchronously on the render thread (request_resize), so for a few frames the newly-exposed area shows black / the app behind. Does NOT happen when shrinking (old larger swapchain still covers). Legacy WS_POPUP resized synchronously (SetWindowPos) so never showed this; exposed now that composition (prexu-kcmg) is the default render path. Fix direction: on GROW, withhold the visual expansion (or clip the visual to the old size) until the render thread confirms the larger swapchain/frame is ready, OR drive the swapchain resize synchronously enough to close the gap. Investigate video_render request_resize + swapchain rebuild ordering vs set_video_offset/visual sizing.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: FIXED + verified (commit 0225992): DComp scale transform on the video visual stretches the current swapchain to fill the inset during the async rebuild gap; render thread reports rebuilt size (set_video_size) so scale snaps to 1:1. Scale clamped >=1 so shrink over-covers (masked by opaque React UI) instead of under-filling. User verified: grow no longer shows app-behind, shrink no longer flashes desktop. cargo check clean, 186 tests pass.

title: C3h: make composition hosting the unconditional default (remove opt-in flag)
description: Flip PREXU_COMPOSITION_HOST from opt-in (default off) to the unconditional default render path so the alt-tab/DirectComposition epic (prexu-ga3x) actually ships to Windows users. C4 parity (prexu-0oc3) is verified (IME deferred, low-risk for English). Steps: composition_host::enabled() always true (or remove the gate), update the 3 gate sites + module docs, verify the app installs composition WITHOUT the env var. Unblocks prexu-zfyi (delete legacy WS_POPUP host_window.rs + taskbar_preview.rs).
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: DONE + verified (commit 33ca58f): enabled() flipped to default-on, opt-out via PREXU_LEGACY_HOST. Launched with NO env flag — DComp tree committed + C4e suppressor installed at startup, proving composition is now the default render path. cargo check clean, 198 tests pass.

title: [bug] composition video freezes on first frame until window focus changes (audio advances, video stuck) — render wake/present stall
description: Repro: start playback via the show banner OR a continue-watching card play button (Dragon Ball episode). Video sticks on one frame while audio progresses normally; switching focus to another app unsticks it and it plays correctly thereafter. HYPOTHESIS (NOT confirmed — needs runtime instrumentation): the render loop only renders on signal.wake()=true AND (have_frame||force_render); the 100ms WAIT_TIMEOUT path renders nothing (video_render.rs:682-713). If mpv's render update_callback stops waking the loop after the first frame, video freezes while mpv's independent audio thread keeps going; a focus/geometry event later fires request_resize->force_render and resumes it. Alternative: Present on the composition swapchain isn't recompositing the DComp visual until a Commit, which the focus-change window message triggers. Needs: add per-frame/timeout trace logging in render_loop to determine whether wakes stop or presents stop; then fix (e.g. periodic force-render, or DComp Commit per present). Severe: unusable playback in composition mode.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE NARROWED (live repro w/ instrumentation, 2026-06-25). Added wake/render/present counters + heartbeat to render_loop. During a CONFIRMED freeze ('actively freezing now'): heartbeat ACTIVE, woke≈frame≈present all climbing ~23/s (24fps content), size stuck at install 1918x2128, NO geometry/resize event the whole run. A WORKING run instead received a resize->1918x939 early and played fine. So: wakes OK, renders OK, Present() OK — frames present continuously but the DComp visual does NOT recomposite. The ONLY thing the working run did extra = a geometry event, which on the MAIN thread calls composition_host::set_video_offset -> IDCompositionDevice::Commit() (composition_host.rs:180). HOST is thread_local to main, so set_video_offset only commits when the geometry path runs on the MAIN thread (window resize/focus events) — NOT when the React-driven sync command runs on a tokio worker (HOST.with -> None -> no-op). HYPOTHESIS (matches all evidence, one commit suffices since the working run committed once and kept playing): the displayed visual needs a DComp Commit on the main thread after presents begin; today that only happens incidentally on a main-thread window event. SAME ROOT as the crop bug prexu-0qri (no initial geometry sync on composition playback start). Next: confirm a bare window-resize (not just focus) unsticks it, then fire a one-shot main-thread DComp commit after the render thread's first present.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: FULL ROOT CAUSE (HiDPI). User repro: play/stop on 100% monitor (fine), move app to 150% monitor, resize, play -> black/freeze. 100% monitor ALWAYS fine. Log shows a RESIZE STORM on the DPI transition: sync_geometry fed a monotonically growing size (1925->1927->...->2016px) as Windows rescales the webview across the DPI change; the render loop rebuilds the shared texture + swapchain + re-imports the ANGLE FBO on EVERY intermediate size (~15 rebuilds in ~2s), thrashing the render target -> black/garbage, and can leave mpv's render context stuck (mode-2 woke=1). The storm CONVERGES (…2014->2015->2016 then stops) so it self-settles after ~1-2s. Source of the resize bursts: DPI rescale events; React Player.tsx ResizeObserver (#224) also nudges reflow per event. FIX DIRECTION: debounce the surface rebuild in video_render render_loop — only rebuild once the requested size has been stable for ~100ms (collapse the burst to one rebuild at the settled size), instead of rebuilding per intermediate size. SEPARATELY VERIFIED: composition_host::commit() one-shot after first present FIXES the mode-1 present-not-compositing freeze (DComp commit fires, frames flow). This unifies with prexu-0qri (crop = surface stuck at a wrong/intermediate size) and C4c (DPI/rasterization). NOTE: 15-min incremental dev builds on this machine make live iteration very slow — consolidating fixes into one build.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: FIXED + user-verified (full 8-step repro, no freeze). Root cause: composition playbacks reuse the persistent geom dedup cache (last_geometry) but spawn a FRESH render thread at the stale install surface size (1918x2128). A replay at the same window size fires no window-event geometry sync, so the dedup-skip means request_resize never reaches the new render thread — it stays at install size with no swapchain rebuild + no main-thread DComp commit → frozen (and cropped). FIX (mod.rs ensure_init): on composition playback start, reset geom.last_geometry=None and schedule a one-shot main-thread sync_geometry_now(current window rect) → drives request_resize (swapchain rebuild to the real player viewport) + set_video_offset (DComp commit). Verified: log shows 'sync_geometry_now -> video 1913x889' + 'DComp commit' + 'resized surfaces to 1913x889 settled' on the previously-frozen 150%-monitor banner play. Supporting fixes kept: resize-debounce (collapses the HiDPI resize burst, was rebuilding the surface ~15x), prime first render, commit-after-first-present. Remaining: strip debug scaffolding, run quality gates, commit.

title: [bug] composition video starts at stale install-time surface size -> cropped to top until a geometry event resizes it (persistent under DPI change)
description: Composition video render thread spawns with claim_surfaces() = the surfaces published once at composition_host::install (install-time client size, e.g. 1918x2128). It only resizes to the real player viewport (e.g. 1918x913) when a later sync_geometry fires request_resize (mod.rs:724). Until then mpv renders the full frame into a too-tall buffer and the visual viewport shows only the top -> 'cropped to sky' (Dragon Ball, Image #3). On a same-DPI monitor the corrective sync_geometry lands fast enough to hide it; after moving to a 150% monitor the size is wrong/late and the crop persists. ROOT: render thread has no initial size-to-current-viewport; legacy host path solved the analogous 'first frame black/clipped' by pre-sizing the first set_geometry (mod.rs:336-341) — composition path needs the equivalent: start the render thread at (or immediately request_resize to) the current DPI-correct player viewport, not the stale published size. Evidence: log shows 'render thread up (1918x2128)' then 'resized surfaces to 1918x913' seconds later on every playback start.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Very likely fixed by the same change (commit 3959afb / prexu-3fxj): the crop was the render thread stuck at the stale install size (1918x2128) on playbacks that got no geometry sync; the new initial sync_geometry_now on composition playback start now resizes the video to the real player viewport every time (verified in logs: 'resized surfaces to 1913x889 settled' on the 150% monitor). Keeping OPEN until the cropped-to-top symptom is explicitly visually confirmed gone on a 150% monitor.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: NEXT SESSION: visually confirm the cropped-to-top symptom is gone on a 150% monitor (play an item on the 100% monitor, move to 150%, replay — video should fill the frame, not show only the top). If clean, close. Fix already landed in commit 3959afb (initial geometry sync now sizes the render thread to the real viewport on every composition playback).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: VERIFIED PASS (user visual): reproduced the prior crop scenario (DPI/150%); video now plays correctly, NOT cropped-to-top. The cropped-to-sky symptom is gone (fix commit 3959afb — initial geometry sync sizes the render thread to the real viewport on composition playback start). Closing per the issue's own 'confirm visually on 150% then close' note.

title: C3b: hwdec through render-context spike (hwdec=auto-safe via ANGLE EGL-share)
description: GATE. C0/C2 proved the mpv->ANGLE->shared-D3D11->DComp pipeline with hwdec=no. Production needs hardware decode (codec breadth = the reason for native mpv, see memory prexu-player-native-required). Verify hwdec=auto-safe survives the libmpv2 RenderContext + ANGLE EGL share-handle interop, on real codecs (h264/hevc/av1). Biggest unverified production risk. Extend spike/dcomp-overlay with hwdec enabled. Acceptance: hardware-decoded frames render correctly into the DComp visual AND are captured by WGC; no black/garbage from the share-handle path under hwdec.
notes: C3b GATE PASS (verified on hw + PNG). Spike: spike/dcomp-hwdec (C2 overlay pipeline + hwdec ON). hwdec=auto-safe selected ZERO-COPY d3d11va (hwdec-current) through libmpv2 RenderContext + ANGLE EGL share-handle path. Decoded frames rendered correctly into the DComp visual (video sample [255,24,0] non-black; PNG shows sharp testsrc2 bars + live timestamp 00:00:05.567 + frame counter = real decoded frames, no corruption) and were WGC-captured WITH the webview overlay + alpha bar. The C0 feared device-mismatch (risk #3) did NOT materialize: mpv's render API bridges its decode device to ANGLE's GL device automatically; NO eglGetPlatformDisplayEXT device-binding fix needed on this dev machine. Tested with a real H.264 yuv420p clip (lavfi is CPU-only, won't engage hwdec) generated via ffmpeg. PREXU_HWDEC env overrides decoder mode. CONCLUSION: hwdec is NOT a Path C blocker; C3d can use hwdec=auto-safe unchanged. Re-confirm on production GPUs (Intel/AMD/NVIDIA) during C3d if different.
close_reason: C3b PASS: hwdec=auto-safe -> zero-copy d3d11va works through the render-context + ANGLE path; frames render correct + captured. No device-binding fix needed. hwdec not a Path C blocker.

title: C3a: fork-vs-bypass integration spike — prove visual-hosted WebView2 keeps Tauri IPC
description: GATE for the C3 approach decision (user chose spike-first then fork). Build a thin prototype proving a visual-hosted WebView2 (CreateCoreWebView2CompositionController + SetRootVisualTarget) created via a FORKED wry 0.54.2 still carries Tauri's IPC bridge: window.__TAURI__ invoke -> Rust command -> emit -> JS listener round-trips. Today the app uses Tauri-managed wry in windowed mode with NO controller access (confirmed). Decide fork (patch wry, keep IPC/custom-protocol stack) vs bypass (own everything, re-impl IPC). Acceptance: a Tauri command invoked from the React build inside the composition-hosted webview returns to JS, AND the webview is transparent over a DComp visual. Informed by C1 (prexu-tfip). Builds on spike/webview2-hosting.
notes: C3a GATE PASS (verified on hw). Spike: spike/wv2-tauri-ipc. title: Path A spike: reparent mpv as WS_CHILD of main window (capture fix)
description:
Lowest-effort path to put video in the main window's DWM surface. SetParent(mpv_hwnd, main_hwnd) + WS_CHILD instead of WS_POPUP (host_window.rs). Video becomes layer-3 of the main window's composition -> Alt+Tab and Windows.Graphics.Capture of the main window include it; could DELETE taskbar_preview.rs iconic workaround. Keeps vo=gpu-next + wid (no render-API rewrite).

PRECONDITION: user reports a prior child-window attempt 'did not work'. Capture the EXACT prior failure mode before retrying (video drawing over webview? z-order? clipping? transparency not showing the sibling child?).

OPEN RISKS to prototype: z-order + WS_CLIPCHILDREN interactions between wry's WS_CHILD webview container and the mpv WS_CHILD under the same parent; whether the transparent webview hole correctly reveals the sibling mpv child within one top-level (sibling-airspace case); geometry sync now relative to client coords instead of screen coords; fullscreen/popout/minimize geometry paths (geometry.rs, commands/) all assume a top-level host.

Acceptance: Alt+Tab tile shows live video AND webview controls still overlay correctly AND no regression to popout/minimize/fullscreen.
close_reason: Path A (WS_CHILD reparent) NON-VIABLE for overlay — confirmed empirically across 2 spike runs + prior native-player attempt. Win32 airspace: sibling child HWNDs (mpv vs wry webview) don't alpha-blend; transparent:true main window punches through to DESKTOP not the sibling child. Child-behind=shows desktop/no video; child-on-top=occludes webview (app unclickable). Video-in-surface and usable controls cannot coexist with sibling children. Linchpin (DWM captures visible child) left unproven but MOOT for Path C (which uses a DComp visual, not a child HWND). Spike code committed flag-gated (PREXU_MPV_CHILD) on feature/prexu-ga3x-altbar-pathA for reference. -> Path C.

title: [epic] Alt-tab parity: in-surface mpv rendering (DirectComposition)
description: Win11 Alt+Tab live-captures the main window surface and ignores DWM iconic bitmaps (confirmed prexu-6iyp). To show video in Alt+Tab like Plex, mpv pixels must live INSIDE the main window's surface instead of a separate top-level window. Path B: DirectComposition + libmpv render API. Phase 0 = verify feasibility in the Tauri/wry windowed-WebView2 + mpv stack BEFORE rewriting the player rendering core. Linchpin unknown: can mpv composite behind a wry WebView2 in one window.
design:
# Alt-tab parity: in-surface mpv rendering — Phase 0 feasibility (VERIFIED)

## Problem
Win11 Alt+Tab live-captures the main window's composed surface and IGNORES DWM iconic bitmaps (confirmed prexu-6iyp: DwmSetIconicThumbnail returns set=Ok yet Alt+Tab stays black). mpv renders into a SEPARATE top-level window (WS_POPUP, hwndParent=None, z-anchored below main; transparent webview "hole" shows it via the desktop compositor). A separate top-level window is not part of the main window's DWM thumbnail -> black tile.

## Goal
Fold mpv pixels into the MAIN window's composed surface so Alt+Tab / Windows.Graphics.Capture include the video (like Plex).

## Verified findings

### DWM capture model
DWM composites a top-level window together with its CHILD HWNDs and its DirectComposition visuals into one per-window surface. Alt+Tab thumbnails and Windows.Graphics.Capture(CreateForWindow) capture that composed output -> they DO include child HWNDs and DComp visuals of the main window. WGC targets top-level windows only, not a child HWND directly.

### wry / WebView2 hosting
wry hosts WebView2 in WINDOWED mode: ICoreWebView2Controller bound to a wry-created WS_CHILD container HWND parented to the Tauri window. wry uses NO DirectComposition / visual hosting. Tauri exposes ICoreWebView2Controller (PlatformWebview::controller()) but NOT a CompositionController and no API to switch hosting mode. (wry src/webview2/mod.rs L396-424, L256-271)

### Airspace
Each pixel belongs to exactly one HWND; no cross-HWND alpha blending. A non-topmost DComp visual on a window sits BELOW child HWNDs and is clipped/occluded by them -> a DComp visual "behind" the windowed WebView2 child is NOT revealed through it. WebView2 transparent background reveals the HOST WINDOW content in the webview region, not an independent behind-the-child layer.

### libmpv render API (needed only for the DComp path, Path C)
mpv render API backends = OpenGL ("opengl") and Software ("sw") ONLY; no D3D11 backend (mpv#5979 open/unimplemented). To feed a DComp visual while keeping hardware decode: OpenGL via ANGLE + WGL_NV_DX_interop2 sharing the GL color texture with a D3D11 texture presented on a DComp visual (proven by media-kit). Software "sw" = full-res CPU readback per frame, defeats hwdec (fallback only). Render API REPLACES --wid embedding entirely (mutually exclusive). libmpv2 4.1.0 exposes RenderContext for the OpenGL path safely; software path needs raw libmpv2_sys FFI.

## Path evaluation
- Path B (mpv DComp visual BEHIND wry webview in one window): NOT FEASIBLE. Windowed WebView2 + airspace occludes it; would need visual hosting anyway. REJECTED.
- Path A (reparent mpv as WS_CHILD of main window): LOW effort candidate. See prexu-ga3x sub-issue. Keeps vo=gpu-next+wid (no render rewrite). RISK: sibling airspace/z-order between wry's WS_CHILD container and mpv WS_CHILD; user reports a prior child attempt "did not work" — must capture the exact prior failure first.
- Path C (full DComp visual hosting): HIGH effort/"proper". Fork wry / custom WebView2 host (ICoreWebView2CompositionController) + re-implement input/focus/IME/DPI scale + mpv render API (OpenGL/ANGLE + DX interop). Demonstrated only on macOS in Tauri ecosystem; no Windows precedent.

## Recommendation
Re-examine Path A first but reconcile with the user's "child window didn't work" report (capture exact failure). If Path A's sibling-airspace is unworkable, Path C is the only true fix and is a large project. Path B is dead.

## Sources
mpv render.h/render_gl.h; mpv#5979; WGL_NV_DX_interop2 (Khronos); media-kit#296; libmpv2 4.1.0 docs; wry src/webview2/mod.rs (commit 2f768ae); tauri PlatformWebview docs; MS DComp basic-concepts + CreateTargetForHwnd; DWM thumbnail-ovw; WebView2 DefaultBackgroundColor + windowed-vs-visual-hosting; tauri discussion #6343; wry #1540/#650(closed); WebView2Feedback #526.

title: Report final stopped timeline from Rust on window close during playback
description: If the app window is closed mid-playback (instead of exiting the player route), the JS unmount cleanup may not run before the webview dies, so the final state=stopped timeline report is lost and the server keeps a stale resume offset. The exit-route path was fixed in 4f96b6587d (GET fetch instead of sendBeacon POST); this covers the hard-close path. Approach: on Tauri window close-requested during active playback, have Rust fire GET {serverUri}/:/timeline?ratingKey=..&key=/library/metadata/{rk}&state=stopped&time=..&duration=..&X-Plex-Client-Identifier=..&X-Plex-Token=.. using last-known position (frontend can push position to Rust state via the existing timeline refs, or Rust can read mpv time-pos before destroy). Must complete (or be given a short grace window) before app exit.
close_reason: Rust fires stopped timeline GET on CloseRequested using mpv time-pos; frontend registers/clears context per playback.

Confirmed gaps for g8a.2/.3: (1) ZERO native-player coverage; (2) item-detail mocks /related but NOT /similar (passes only because unrouted /similar fails gracefully — real gap for 9i0 assertions); (3) shallow assertions (text-presence, not behavior/interaction); (4) webkit project not yet executed. So g8a.2 = strengthen + fix mock gaps + modernize locators, NOT repair-broken.

title: Regression test automation: Playwright web refresh + native spike
description: The e2e/ Playwright suite (14 specs + mock-tauri.ts) was last touched 2026-03-17 and predates the entire feature/native-player branch — zero coverage of the player overlay/minimize/popout/transparency. Goal: automate the content/UI/state half of the manual native-player test plan (sections 4-9) via the web build, and spike whether the native player half (sections 1-3) is drivable via tauri-driver. Constraint: e2e drives the WEB build (vite :1420) with mock-tauri stubbing invoke()->null, so native mpv/Win32 behavior is invisible to Playwright. Team: opus lead orchestrates; sonnet for spec de-staling + new coverage + tauri-driver spike; haiku for browser install + CI wiring. Members load /core:anti-fabrication,/core:git,/core:mise + testing:playwright,tauri:testing,prexu:prexu on start, and use the claude task-list tool.

Remaining: g8a.3 (new fix coverage), g8a.4 (web-mode chrome check), g8a.6 (CI). g8a.3/.4 both touch e2e/mock-tauri.ts so run sequential or one agent.
