All 7 sub-tasks shipped: 7il.1 (popout rename), 7il.2 (Rust mini path), 7il.3 (mini chrome), 7il.4 (button mutual exclusion), 7il.5 (resizable), 7il.6 (verification), 7il.7 (anchor-drag). Total of 13 hotfixes addressing user-reported bugs along the way: prexu-wow, prexu-ya6, prexu-s0f, prexu-7a2, prexu-4ml, prexu-di9, prexu-cay, prexu-9bk, prexu-7cb, prexu-quq, prexu-pa4 (plus prexu-4k5 reverted by s0f and prexu-134 superseded by quq). Architectural follow-ups filed for cleanup: prexu-ii3 (split PlayerContext), prexu-my6 (mpv as child window), prexu-aqd (popout drag stuck-out-of-window). Polish follow-ups still open: prexu-0p3 (chrome layout lag after popout exit), prexu-nhs, prexu-vs5, prexu-mjw, prexu-efy.

title: [bug] alphabet scrubber lands short and below target row
description: Two related symptoms when clicking a letter higher in the alphabet (e.g. 'D' from a viewport showing 'A-C'): (1) the first click scrolls but doesn't reach the target letter's first row — that row is below the viewport. A second click scrolls further but only just barely shows the target row at the bottom. (2) The user expects the target row at the TOP of the viewport, not the bottom. Root cause: VirtualizedLibraryGrid's scrollToIndex was calling tanstack/react-virtual's virtualizer.scrollToIndex with align:'center' + behavior:'smooth'. For long alphabet jumps the virtualizer estimates row sizes for unmeasured rows; cumulative estimate error puts the smooth scroll at the wrong offset. Once the destination window renders, measureElement updates the cache but the smooth scroll has already settled. Fix: changed to align:'start', behavior:'auto', and added two RAF settle passes that re-issue scrollToIndex once the just-rendered rows have been measured. Surfaced 2026-05-09 by Nick during library testing on a ~500-show library.
close_reason: Fixed in src/components/VirtualizedLibraryGrid.tsx scrollToIndex: switched align:'center' → 'start' (target row at top of viewport, matching user expectation for alphabet jumps) and behavior:'smooth' → 'auto' (instant). Added a two-RAF settle loop that re-issues scrollToIndex on the next animation frame and again the frame after — by then the just-revealed rows have been measured by the virtualizer's measureElement callback, so the corrected (rather than estimated) cumulative offset gets applied. Non-virtualized fallback also switched to block:'start' for consistency. tsc + vitest 1232/1232 clean. Browser verification still required (the bug only reproduces on a virtualized library with hundreds of rows + a long alphabet jump).

title: [bug] mini-player button no-op on native player path
description: The PiP button in ControlsBottomBar called usePictureInPicture.togglePiP, which calls video.requestPictureInPicture(). On the native (mpv) path videoRef.current is null because there's no <video> element (mpv renders into a sibling Win32 HostWindow), so the call silently returned without any logger output or invocation. Fixed by adding useMiniPlayer hook (calls playerEnterMini/playerExitMini from prexu-a6z.1) and branching by IS_NATIVE_PLAYER in Player.tsx so the same button drives the native mini-player on Tauri and the browser PiP everywhere else. Effectively the MVP of prexu-a6z.2 (useMiniPlayer hook + UI toggle) — defaults to bottom-right corner, 480x270. Surfaced during prexu-01f / prexu-a6z.1 verification.
close_reason: Added useMiniPlayer hook (calls playerEnterMini/playerExitMini from prexu-a6z.1 with bottom-right 480x270 defaults) and branched the existing PiP wiring in Player.tsx by IS_NATIVE_PLAYER. On native (mpv) the button now invokes the Win32 mini-player; on HTML5 the existing browser PiP is unchanged. Logger calls at info level on every transition. 4 unit tests cover initial state, enter, exit, and reject-on-enter. This is effectively the MVP of prexu-a6z.2 — full hook with persisted geometry + corner cycling can build on top.

title: Player as overlay (not route) — instant exit + page persistence
description:
**Goal**: clicking Stop returns the user to the page they were on with **no perceptible gap** — matching Plex's UX. Today, Player is a peer route to AppLayout (App.tsx:200-219), so stopping triggers a full AppLayout + destination route remount (~1s in dev, prexu-zq4). The fix is architectural: keep AppLayout always mounted, render Player as a top-level overlay driven by context, never unmount the underlying page when entering playback.

User-confirmed scope (2026-05-04):
- OK to lose: URL-based deep-linking into /play/X, browser back/forward toggling player open/closed, bookmarks of /play/X.
- Must keep: in-player Prev/Next episode for shows + playlists (currently navigate(`/play/${next}`)).
- Defer: Watch Together URL-based entry (separate WT refactor later — single-user UX is the priority).

## Architecture

**New: src/contexts/PlayerContext.tsx**
- State: `{ playing: { ratingKey: string; offset?: number; session?: WTOptions } | null }`
- Methods: `play(ratingKey, options?)`, `stop()`, `replaceRatingKey(nextKey)` (for in-player prev/next without unmount/remount churn)
- Provider mounted at App.tsx top level under AppProviders

**Modified: src/App.tsx**
- Remove the standalone `<Route path="/play/:ratingKey" element={...} />` route
- Render `<PlayerOverlay />` at top level (sibling to <Routes>) — conditionally renders Player when context.playing != null
- Keep a thin `/play/:ratingKey` bridge route inside AppLayout that, on mount, reads URL params, calls context.play(...), then navigates(replace) to lastNonPlayerRoute. This is what lets WT invite URLs and external deep-links still work without resurrecting the standalone route.

**Modified: src/pages/Player.tsx**
- handleExit → context.stop() (no navigate)
- handleNextEpisode / handlePrevEpisode → context.replaceRatingKey() (in-place ratingKey swap; existing usePlayer ratingKey effect re-inits)
- Remove sessionStorage lastNonPlayerRoute machinery (not needed when AppLayout never unmounts)

**Modified call sites (15 files)** — replace navigate(`/play/${k}`) with context.play(k):
- src/hooks/usePlayAction.tsx (4 sites — main play action helper)
- src/pages/Dashboard.tsx (3)
- src/pages/PlaylistDetail.tsx (2)
- src/pages/CollectionDetail.tsx (2)
- src/pages/ItemDetail.tsx (1)
- src/pages/Downloads.tsx (1)
- src/components/detail/ItemHeroSection.tsx (6)
- src/components/player/QueuePanel.tsx (1)
- src/components/InviteNotification.tsx (1) — WT, can stay as URL nav for now via the bridge route
- src/components/SessionCreator.tsx (1) — WT, same
- src/hooks/watch-together/useRemoteMediaSwitch.ts (1) — WT, same
- src/hooks/watch-together/useWatchTogetherSession.ts (1) — WT, same

**Test updates**:
- src/hooks/usePlayAction.test.tsx — mock PlayerContext instead of useNavigate
- src/components/InviteNotification.test.tsx — keep existing nav-based assertion (bridge route)
- App-level tests if any assume a /play route

## Cleanup once landed

- Remove the route-transition spinner in AppLayout (shipped 3306cff for prexu-zq4) — no longer needed because there's no transition gap.
- Re-evaluate Player.tsx body-bg manipulation — with AppLayout always mounted under the player, the body navy paint may also be removable. Verify Discord/desktop bleed isn't reintroduced (per the native-player-fullscreen memory).

## Acceptance

1. **Stop click**: time from click to underlying page being interactive is one frame (<16ms perceived). No spinner, no navy fill, no remount of AppLayout/destination page.
2. **In-player Prev/Next**: switching episodes within a show or playlist works as today (player chrome stays, content swaps). No AppLayout remount, no navigation.
3. **WT URLs**: clicking a WT invite link (which contains /play/X?session=...) still launches playback with the session active. Single-user playback no longer goes through any /play/* URL.
4. **Tests**: full vitest suite green; tsc clean.
5. **Manual**: dev tauri build — Stop on movie/episode is visually instant; library/dashboard/item-detail page is exactly as it was when Play was clicked (scroll position preserved via existing useScrollRestoration).

## Out of scope (file separately if needed)
- Player URL deep-linking
- Browser back/forward integration with player open/closed
- WT entry refactor (single-user is the priority — WT keeps working via the bridge route)

## Required Skills
react,typescript,prexu,anti-fabrication

title: Stop click: navy exit overlay visible for ~2s before navigate
description:
Symptom (2026-05-03 user report on dev tauri build): clicking Stop on the native player paints the navy exitOverlay (introduced by prexu-cuq) and leaves it visible for about 2 seconds before the previous page renders.

Root cause: src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs destroy() did a synchronous bounded-join on the mpv event pump with a 2s timeout (recv_timeout(Duration::from_secs(2))). The TS handleExit awaits player_unload, so when the join hits its timeout (often, in practice — mpv's wait_event(1.0) only breaks on the NEXT iteration after Shutdown), the React UI is held up for the full 2s. The exit overlay sits on screen for that whole window.

Fix: split destroy() into a synchronous silence phase (mute, pause, queue stop, queue quit) and a background teardown phase (pump join, host drop, terminate_destroy on Inner drop). The Tauri command returns in <50ms after silence; the slow teardown happens on a spawned thread without blocking the user. Audio is already cut by the synchronous mute, so the deferred work has no user-visible cost.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri,prexu,anti-fabrication
close_reason: destroy() now returns synchronously after mute+pause+stop+quit; pump join + host drop + terminate moved to a background thread so handleExit's await resolves in <50ms instead of up to 2s.

title: Next Episode prompt fires on movies due to stale queue + missing content-type gate
description:
**Symptom (verified)**: Played 10 Cloverfield Lane (a movie). Near end credits, a 'Next Episode' button appeared. Clicked it — went to Yu Yu Hakusho S01E02, which had been the test content from a prior day's session.

**Root cause** (found): src/pages/Player.tsx:335-337:
```ts
const hasNextItem =
  queue.currentIndex + 1 < queue.items.length ||
  episodeNav.handleNextEpisode != null;
```

For a movie, episodeNav.handleNextEpisode is null (correct — Plex movies don't have episode siblings). But queue.items still contains yesterday's Yu Yu Hakusho episode because src/contexts/QueueContext.tsx:48 persists the queue to localStorage and never clears it. So hasNextItem becomes true via the queue-fallback branch, useSkipSegments synthesizes the 'Next Episode' segment (commit 8e42202), and clicking it advances the queue to whatever's there.

**Two distinct issues, one user impact**:

1. **Synthesis doesn't gate by content type.** A 'Next Episode' label is meaningless for a movie. Even with a populated queue, clicking it shouldn't say 'Next Episode' for a movie. Either:
   - Gate hasNextItem synthesis to player.itemType === 'episode' (current item is an episode, so 'next' makes sense), OR
   - Rename the synthesized prompt to 'Up Next' / 'Play Next' for non-episode contexts, OR
   - Don't synthesize at all when current item isn't an episode.

2. **Queue persists stale items across app sessions.** Even if (1) is fixed for movies, a queued YYH ep from yesterday surviving into today is questionable UX. Options:
   - Clear queue on app launch (aggressive — loses 'oh I queued these last night, let me resume').
   - Add per-item timestamps + TTL (e.g. 24h).
   - Clear queue on Stop / when leaving the player without auto-advancing.
   - Leave as-is and rely on (1) to gate the prompt — accept that the queue may contain unrelated content but only surface it when the *current* content matches its type.

Recommend doing (1) first — minimal risk, fixes the user-visible bug directly. Decide on (2) afterward as separate UX policy.

**Acceptance**: While playing a movie, no 'Next Episode' button appears, even with non-empty queue. While playing an episode with a queued unrelated item (e.g. movie), behavior is intentional per the (2) decision.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: Reproduced 2026-05-03 by user after testing prexu-aua. ratingKey for 10 Cloverfield Lane was 26800; clicking Next Episode took user to YYH S01E02. Queue persistence file: src/contexts/QueueContext.tsx:48.
close_reason:
Fixed in src/pages/Player.tsx by introducing a single 'logical next' notion (hasNextItem = itemType==='episode' AND (queue has next OR Plex episode-nav has next)) and threading it to all three sites that previously had per-call-site gates that didn't agree:
1. useSkipSegments hasNextEpisode arg — was already gated indirectly via hasNextItem prior to this change.
2. SkipSegmentButton hasNextEpisode prop — was '!!handleNextEpisode' (always true). Now uses hasNextItem.
3. PostPlayScreen useEffect trigger — was 'remainingCount > 0' (any queue items). Now uses hasNextItem.

Verified by user on dev: movies show 'Skip Credits' (not 'Next Episode') and don't pop PostPlay at end. Episodes still get 'Next Episode' + PostPlayScreen at credits/EOF (regression-free).

title: Audio leaks 2-3s after Stop — destroy is async to React unmount
description:
**Symptom**: Clicking Stop in the player. Video disappears immediately, dashboard renders (background painted), but audio continues playing for ~2-3 seconds before fully cutting out.

**Reproduced** on installed Prexu_0.6.3 (commit 09054f0) playing ratingKey=63168 with audio enhancements preset=night. Logs show full destroy() sequence completes within the same wall-clock second (mute → pause → stop → quit → pump joined in 18ms → host dropped at 28ms total). So the leak isn't from a slow Rust-side teardown.

**Root cause**: src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts:382 fires `invoke("player_unload")` from a useEffect cleanup. useEffect cleanups CANNOT be async — returning a Promise from one is a no-op for React. So when the player unmounts:
1. React cleanup runs, fires `invoke("player_unload")` and immediately returns
2. React continues unmounting the player overlay → navigates back → dashboard renders → user sees background paint
3. *Concurrently*, Tauri command thread is still inside Player::destroy() doing mute/pause/stop/quit/join/mpv_terminate_destroy
4. mpv's audio-buffer (~200ms decoded-ahead) + WASAPI's already-submitted samples continue playing during steps 2-3

The historical 'fix' captured in the superseded memory ('destroy() joins event pump before dropping Inner so mpv_terminate_destroy runs sync and silences audio') is correct on the Rust side but doesn't help — the Rust side runs concurrently with React's unmount, not before it.

**Fix candidates** (not decided):
1. **React side**: Move player_unload OUT of useEffect cleanup. Have the Stop button (and PostPlayScreen Stop, and back-nav) AWAIT `invoke("player_unload")` before navigating. Component then unmounts after mpv is already terminated. Simplest fix; won't catch unmount-via-tab-close paths.
2. **Rust side**: Detach WASAPI before stop. Set `ao=null` (or `command ["ao_reload"]`) at the start of destroy() to immediately route mpv audio to a null sink, killing the OS audio session within mpv's tick rather than waiting for buffered samples to drain. Keeps the React side simple but adds a libmpv2 audio API touchpoint.
3. **Both**: 1 for the fast path, 2 as backstop for unmount-via-other-paths.

Recommend evaluating fix 1 first — small, contained to useNativePlayer.ts, and explains the user-observed race directly.

**Acceptance**: After Stop, audio cuts within 200ms of video disappearing (or simultaneously). Reproduce by playing 30s into any file then clicking Stop.

## Required Skills
react,rust,prexu,anti-fabrication
notes: Surfaced during prexu-2zo.3 retest 2026-05-03. Smoke test still soft-passing on direct play but this is a clear UX regression vs the SUPERSEDED 'fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed' memory which claimed destroy was synchronous end-to-end.
close_reason: Fixed in src/pages/Player.tsx handleExit: await invoke('player_unload') BEFORE navigate(target). The useEffect cleanup that ran after unmount was firing player_unload fire-and-forget, racing mpv terminate against React's next paint and leaving 2-3s of buffered audio playing while the dashboard was already showing. Awaiting in handleExit pushes the route change until destroy() returns; the cleanup then no-ops via 'destroy: nothing to destroy'. User-verified: 'audio is correctly cut off on stop'.

title: Native player: 30s first-play cold start on installed build
description:
**Symptom**: First HEVC playback after a fresh install takes ~30 seconds before first frame is presented. Subsequent plays (after closing/reopening Prexu) start in <1 second. Reproduced on installed Prexu_0.6.3_x64-setup.exe at C:\Users\grand\AppData\Local\Prexu\prexu.exe.

**Diagnostic data** (from %LOCALAPPDATA%\com.prexu.client\logs\Prexu.log, UTC):

First play (cold):
```
23:19:01  load_url offset=94339ms
23:19:01  ensure_init OK, HostWindow created, loadfile sent
   ⏱ 12 second gap with NO log entries from mpv
23:19:13  apply_sub_style fires (React side reacted to something — likely FileLoaded that we didn't observe in this session at our log level)
```

Second play of a different file (warm), after closing/reopening Prexu:
```
23:26:27  load_url offset=120500ms
23:26:27  ensure_init OK, HostWindow created, mpv created, loadfile sent
23:26:27  FileLoaded → player://ready    ← all in same wall-clock second
```

**Likely contributors** (from highest to lowest confidence):
1. Windows Defender / AV first-execution scan of the unsigned Prexu.exe (19MB) and libmpv-2.dll (117MB). Plausible because second run is unaffected.
2. Cold mpv hwdec probing (auto-safe → d3d11va selection on Windows).
3. Cold Plex direct-streaming connection — first byte-range request to plex.direct on a fresh run can stall.

**Possible mitigations to evaluate** (no decision yet):
- Code-sign the installer/binary (prevents some AV scan delays; needs signing cert).
- Warm-init mpv at app launch (create a dummy player + libmpv handle so the DLL is paged in and hwdec is probed before user clicks play). Trade-off: small idle CPU/memory cost.
- 'Preparing playback…' UI affordance during the cold-load phase so the user knows we're working.
- Investigate whether the 12s gap is mpv side or Plex side by adding more granular logging (track when first byte-range response arrives).

**Acceptance**: First-play time-to-first-frame on a fresh install drops to ≤5s OR an unambiguous loading affordance is shown during the cold load. Whichever lands first.

## Required Skills
rust,react,prexu,anti-fabrication
notes: Surfaced during prexu-2zo.3 (HEVC smoke test on installed build, 2026-05-03). Smoke test otherwise green: install layout correct, libmpv-2.dll loads, HEVC decodes, no crashes. Second-play warm path is <1s — clean architecturally.
close_reason: Shipped the explanatory loading affordance (issue accepted EITHER ≤5s OR an unambiguous loading affordance). Underlying perf investigation tracked in prexu-ps1.

title: Episode list: hover thumbnail to play, click text to view detail
description:
On the season episode list (e.g. /item/<show>/season/<n> — see screenshot of Yu Yu Hakusho 'Spirit Detective Saga' S1), each episode row has a thumbnail on the left and metadata (episode #, date, title, description) on the right. Currently the entire row is one click target that goes to the episode detail page.

Proposed:
- **Hover the THUMBNAIL** → reveals a play overlay (similar to dashboard cards). Clicking the thumbnail starts playback directly via /play/<episodeKey>.
- **Click the right-side text region** (episode #, date, title, description) → unchanged behaviour: navigates to the episode detail page (/item/<episodeKey>).

This makes binge-watching dramatically faster — no detail page round-trip — while keeping the existing per-episode info path for users who want to read details first.

Considerations:
- Hover-to-play on TV/remote doesn't translate; keyboard focus on the thumbnail should also reveal the play affordance, and Enter on a focused thumbnail starts playback.
- For mobile/touch: tap on thumbnail = play, tap on text = detail (no hover needed).
- Should respect viewOffset (resume vs from-start) — same logic as the existing play buttons on detail pages.

Acceptance:
- Hovering an episode's thumbnail on desktop reveals a play button overlay.
- Clicking the play overlay (or the thumbnail) navigates to /play/<ratingKey> with the saved viewOffset.
- Clicking anywhere in the right-side text column navigates to /item/<ratingKey> as today.
- Keyboard focus on the thumbnail also shows the play affordance and Enter triggers playback.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: EpisodeListSection refactored: each row is now a <div> with two sibling buttons. Thumbnail button reuses usePlayAction.getPlayHandler (resume/start logic shared with PosterCard, honors viewOffset, /play bridge), reveals a centered play overlay on hover OR keyboard focus, with falls-back to direct /play/<key> nav if non-playable. Text button navigates to /item/<key>. playOverlay is rendered once at section level. logger.debug calls added for both nav paths. vitest 1188/1188, tsc clean. Browser/UI verification not run from this session — flag for next visual pass.

title: Library alphabet scrubber: clicking does nothing + needs to be larger
description:
Two issues with the A-Z letter scrubber on the right edge of LibraryView (the vertical strip of '#', 'A', 'B', ... 'Z' next to the poster grid):

1. **Clicks are non-functional.** Clicking any letter does not jump the grid to items starting with that letter. Either the click handler isn't wired, the scroll-to logic doesn't find a matching item, or the scrubber is non-interactive entirely.

2. **Hit target is too small.** Each letter is ~12px tall and the strip itself is narrow (~24px). On desktop this is hard to click precisely; on touch / TV it's unusable. Increase both height per letter and the overall strip width.

Acceptance:
- Clicking a letter scrolls the poster grid to the first item whose title starts with that letter (case-insensitive, ignoring leading 'The'/'A' articles per existing library sort behaviour). '#' jumps to numeric / non-alpha titles.
- Letters get noticeably more vertical breathing room (suggest ~28-32px per letter on desktop) and the strip widens to ~40-48px.
- Hover/active state is visible on the letter under the cursor.

Files likely involved: src/pages/LibraryView.tsx (or the scrubber subcomponent); the poster grid that needs to expose a scroll-to-letter API.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Root cause: handleAlphaJump used querySelectorAll on the grid container, which only sees rendered DOM — under VirtualizedLibraryGrid's @tanstack/react-virtual, off-screen rows aren't mounted, so all cross-letter jumps silently no-oped. Fix: extracted normalization into src/utils/library-sort.ts (titleSort > stripped-article-title > '#' bucket), exposed scrollToIndex(i) imperative handle on VirtualizedLibraryGrid via React 19 ref-as-prop, and rewired LibraryView.handleAlphaJump to call findFirstIndexForLetter + scrollToIndex. AlphaJumpBar enlarged to 44x30px per letter with hover/active states. 17 unit tests for library-sort, vitest 1188/1188, tsc clean.

title: Re-enable autoplay UX flow after Stop/disable
description:
Once a user clicks 'Stop' on PostPlayScreen or unchecks the AUTO PLAY ON checkbox, there's no UI surface to re-enable autoplay for subsequent episodes' PostPlay overlays. autoplayEnabled is local React state in PostPlayScreen, so it resets to true when the overlay re-mounts — but if EOF detection breaks (or the user navigates via SkipSegmentButton's Next Episode instead of letting EOF fire), they may go many episodes without seeing the PostPlay overlay at all and lose track of the setting.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification: user lost the autoplay flow entirely once SkipSegmentButton-driven navigation took over.

Possible fixes:
- Persist autoplay-on preference in playback prefs (alongside skipIntroEnabled / skipCreditsEnabled).
- Add a small autoplay toggle in PlayerControls or in a settings flyout so it's always reachable.
- Show the PostPlay overlay's autoplay toggle even when the next-episode jump came from SkipSegmentButton (would require triggering PostPlay on user-initiated nav, which is a different UX).

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Persisted autoPlayEnabled to PlaybackPreferences with a Settings toggle so users can re-enable from outside the PostPlay overlay flow

title: Add Stop button in player controls; make Back navigate history
description:
The current back-arrow in PlayerControls performs two roles:
- Exit the player (return to library)
- Implicitly halt playback (player_unload runs in cleanup)

After clicking Next Episode (or letting autoplay advance), clicking Back returns to the PREVIOUS episode (browser history pop) instead of exiting the player. From the user's perspective there's no obvious way to stop watching short of using ESC or pressing Back multiple times.

Proposal:
- Replace (or augment) the back arrow with an explicit Stop button that quits the player session entirely (navigate to /library or to the last non-/play/* route).
- Or keep both: Back = navigate(-1) (history pop), Stop = navigate to safe non-player route.
- Stop should also dispatch player_unload eagerly so audio stops immediately even if the next route's render is slow.

Discussion needed on:
- Which icon / position
- Whether to map Stop to a keyboard shortcut (Q? Shift+Esc?)
- TV-app future: a remote 'Back' button typically maps to history-pop; we need a separate 'Exit' or 'Home' affordance for the player

Could be combined with prexu-w0j (Skip Credits / Next Episode separation) since both are player-control UX rethinks.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification.

## Required Skills
react,prexu,documentation
close_reason: Implemented in commit-pending. PlayerControls top bar now has two buttons: top-left Previous (chevron, title=Previous, conditional on queue.currentIndex>0 || episodeNav.handlePrevEpisode), top-right Exit (X icon, title=Exit, always present). Exit calls a new handler that reads sessionStorage 'prexu.lastNonPlayerRoute' (written by App.tsx on every non-/play route change) so the user lands on the page they pressed Play from rather than walking back through every auto-advanced episode in history. ESC keyboard shortcut also maps to Exit. Skip-credits-vs-next-episode separation (prexu-w0j) remains a separate epic for the SkipSegmentButton's behaviour.

title: ErrorBoundary: Cannot read properties of null (reading '1') during HMR
description:
During an active dev session with Vite HMR firing on backend pushes, the React ErrorBoundary fallback briefly displayed 'Something went wrong / Cannot read properties of null (reading '1')'. Playback resumed afterward. Likely a transient HMR state where one module reloaded before another caught up.

Reproducer attempt: difficult — only seen during heavy edit-save cycles in dev. Production builds shouldn't hit this since modules aren't replaced live.

Triage path:
1. Check src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx — capture err.stack and componentStack to a logger so we have a stack trace next time. console.error already exists but doesn't surface in our log file.
2. Grep for any `x[1]` / `x['1']` patterns where x might be null after a dev-time reload.
3. Common HMR offenders: useRef holding stale refs after module replacement, useMemo/useCallback retaining old closures, event listeners attached to torn-down DOM nodes.
4. If reproducible, capture the browser DevTools stack trace (Vite shows mapped sources) — that's the fastest path to root cause.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Instrumented ErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch to log error.name/message/stack and componentStack via logger.error so the next HMR trip leaves a trace in the tauri log file. Phase B grep was clean (no credible arr[1] null-read in our code paths); the runtime '[1]' is most likely inside a third-party dep during module replacement, which the new logger output will pin down on next reproduction. Test added covering logger call. vitest 1188/1188, tsc clean.

title: Skip segment dismiss persists across episodes
description:
When the user clicks the 'x' to dismiss the SkipSegmentButton (intro/credits/Next Episode prompt), the dismissal silently persists into subsequent episodes — the button never reappears.

Two compounding causes in useSkipSegments.ts:
1. useSegments cache key is markers.length + chapters.length only (line 106-110). Most TV episodes have 2 markers (intro + credits), so the cache returns the OLD episode's segment objects with their old startMs values. The dismissedRef set keys on `${type}-${startMs}` and matches the stale entries — segments get filtered out before reaching state.
2. dismissedRef (line 37) is a per-hook-instance useRef with no reset. Player.tsx stays mounted across React Router navigations, so the Set lives forever for the session.

Fix: switch useSegments cache to reference equality on markers/chapters arrays (Plex returns fresh arrays per metadata fetch, so === changes between episodes). Reset dismissedRef in a useEffect when markers/chapters references change.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Fixed in commit ff149b3. useSegments cache now keys on markers/chapters reference identity (was length-only, which collided across same-shape episodes). Added a useEffect in useSkipSegments that clears dismissedRef + activeSegment when markers or chapters references change — Player.tsx stays mounted across React Router navigations so the Set otherwise persisted for the entire session. Existing 9 useSkipSegments tests pass; full vitest suite 1163/1163 pass.

title: PostPlay: Stop button doesn't stop playback when autoplay is still on
description:
Clicking Stop on PostPlayScreen while 'AUTO PLAY ON' is checked closes the overlay but playback restarts (the next episode keeps loading/playing). Workaround: uncheck AUTO PLAY ON first, then click Stop — that actually halts.

Likely cause: handlePostPlayStop in Player.tsx:243 only does setShowPostPlay(false). It doesn't:
- Cancel the PostPlayScreen autoplay-on-zero countdown interval
- Pause/unload the player

So the autoplay countdown keeps ticking even after the overlay is hidden, eventually firing onPlayNext via the unmount cleanup or interval racing the unmount. Or a second EOF-reaction handler runs because the underlying player wasn't paused.

Likely fix:
- onStop in PostPlayScreen should ALSO clear the autoplay interval (mirror handleToggleAutoPlay's clearInterval branch).
- handlePostPlayStop should also explicitly pause the player or call player_unload to stop the underlying playback (which also resolves bug #1's underlying-playback-progressing concern).

Acceptance: with AUTO PLAY ON checked, clicking Stop closes the overlay AND halts playback (no further loading, no audio bleed-through). Same behavior as clicking Stop with autoplay off.

Related to prexu-postplay-autoplay (filed alongside) — probably fixable in one change.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Root cause was the event pump emitting player://eof for every libmpv EndFile event regardless of reason. STOP/QUIT (which fire when player_unload tears down mpv during episode-change navigation) were misinterpreted as natural EOF, so when the user clicked the SkipSegmentButton's 'Next Episode' label (mid-credits), the OLD player's STOP fired player://eof, React showed PostPlay for the queue position AFTER the just-loaded next ep, and the audio/video underneath was the legitimately-loaded new episode. Fixed in events.rs by filtering EndFile to reason==0 (EOF only). Player.tsx also got defensive player_pause dispatches on PostPlay open and Stop click as belt-and-suspenders for any future regression. cargo check + tsc clean.

title: PostPlay: next episode starts playing under the banner before user picks Play Now
description:
When an episode ends and PostPlayScreen appears with a queued next item, the next video starts playing UNDERNEATH the overlay automatically — the user can hear/see it via the bottom controls (timer counting up) without having clicked Play Now.

Repro:
1. Queue an episode that has another episode after it (e.g. S01E02 with S01E03 queued).
2. Let it play to the end.
3. PostPlayScreen appears for S01E03 with countdown.
4. Observe bottom controls — playback timer is already advancing (~0:05 / 23:55 in screenshot) before any user interaction.

Likely cause: an extra EOF-reaction path is auto-advancing the queue or loading the next ratingKey before the user accepts. Check:
- useEpisodeNavigation auto-advance on EOF (if any)
- useQueueAutoPopulate side effects
- Whether useNativePlayer's reportTimeline 'stopped' is misinterpreted as a queue-advance signal
- Whether the PostPlayScreen autoplay countdown effect is firing at countdown=initial (off-by-one)

Acceptance: when PostPlayScreen is visible, the player is paused on the previous episode (or showing a static black frame) until the user picks Play Now or autoplay countdown reaches 0. Then and only then does the next ratingKey load.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification (commit e5da260).

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Root cause was the event pump emitting player://eof for every libmpv EndFile event regardless of reason. STOP/QUIT (which fire when player_unload tears down mpv during episode-change navigation) were misinterpreted as natural EOF, so when the user clicked the SkipSegmentButton's 'Next Episode' label (mid-credits), the OLD player's STOP fired player://eof, React showed PostPlay for the queue position AFTER the just-loaded next ep, and the audio/video underneath was the legitimately-loaded new episode. Fixed in events.rs by filtering EndFile to reason==0 (EOF only). Player.tsx also got defensive player_pause dispatches on PostPlay open and Stop click as belt-and-suspenders for any future regression. cargo check + tsc clean.

title: Tighten release.yml cleanup tag pattern
description:
The dev-drprasad/delete-older-releases step in release.yml uses delete_tag_pattern: 'v' which is a regex that matches any tag containing 'v'. After 6.1, the recommended setup involves a 'libmpv-vendor' release tag — the cleanup would delete that vendored archive after enough version bumps, breaking the next CI run.

Fix: change pattern to ^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$ (strict semver vN.N.N).

Acceptance: tag a fake release like 'libmpv-vendor-2026' and run the cleanup logic locally / verify it isn't selected for deletion.
close_reason: Tightened delete_tag_pattern in .github/workflows/release.yml from 'v' (regex matching any tag containing v, including libmpv-vendor) to '^v\d+\.\d+\.\d+$' (strict semver vN.N.N). Used single-quoted YAML scalar so the backslashes are literal regex metacharacters (in double-quoted YAML, \d would be an invalid escape). Won't auto-clean pre-release tags like v0.6.3-rc1 either, which is fine — those tend to want longer retention for triage. Will be exercised on next v* release tag push.

title: Change default directPlayPreference from 'never' to 'auto'
description: Default was set to 'never' in commit cdc9fd6 to force HLS transcoding during the HTML5 player era. Now that the native (libmpv) path can handle any codec and the HTML5 path guards with canDirectPlay() codec whitelist, 'auto' is the sensible default. Users currently on 'never' due to this default never benefit from the Direct Play fix in prexu-fmd.7.
close_reason: Closed

title: Native player: continue-watching prompt is slow to appear
description:
Clicking play on a partially-watched item shows blank for 1-2 seconds before the 'Continue from xx:xx / Start from beginning' prompt appears. Caught during Phase 2 acceptance.

Likely causes:
- getItemMetadata Plex API call latency (network-bound; hard to fix without caching)
- useNativePlayer's setIsLoading sequence holds back the prompt component until metadata resolves
- The prompt is a separate hook/component that waits on player metadata that didn't have to wait before

Investigation: profile the path between play-click and prompt-render. Add timing logs.

Acceptance: prompt appears within ~200ms of click for items with cached metadata, or shows a loading indicator immediately if data is still being fetched.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. 3uj: mpv default osd-level=1/osd-bar=yes drew a horizontal progress bar in the middle of the video on every seek; with the transparent webview it showed through alongside our React seek bar. Set osd-level=0 and osd-bar=no on mpv init. dcj: usePlayAction now trusts cached item.viewOffset (always present on onDeck/recents) and shows ResumePopover instantly with no network round trip. Slow path (no cached offset) shows a small loading popover at click point for immediate feedback.

title: Native player: misrendered UI element appears in middle of player route
description:
After Phase 2 transparency fixes (commit 6d74a0a), a horizontal bar / outlined rectangle appears in the middle of the player route, visible over the video. Most likely a piece of player chrome (SeekBar, buffered indicator, or controls overlay) that previously blended into a colored background and now reads weirdly with body transparent.

Diagnosis: right-click the artifact in Tauri devtools and identify the element. Likely either:
- SeekBar buffered fill rendering with buffered=0 (we stubbed that in useNativePlayer; step 3.10 lights it up)
- An overlay (PostPlay placeholder, KeyboardShortcuts) with previously-blending bg
- The hidden <video> element somehow still occupying space

Acceptance: artifact gone or styled to look intentional on both native + html5 paths.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Resolved on feature/native-player. 3uj: mpv default osd-level=1/osd-bar=yes drew a horizontal progress bar in the middle of the video on every seek; with the transparent webview it showed through alongside our React seek bar. Set osd-level=0 and osd-bar=no on mpv init. dcj: usePlayAction now trusts cached item.viewOffset (always present on onDeck/recents) and shows ResumePopover instantly with no network round trip. Slow path (no cached offset) shows a small loading popover at click point for immediate feedback.

title: Step 6.4 — code-signing impact assessment
description:
libmpv-2.dll is third-party — does Windows SmartScreen flag it inside the bundle? Does existing code-signing path cover bundled resources or do we need to also sign the DLL? Document findings.

## Required Skills
github,security,documentation
close_reason: Documented findings in docs/code-signing.md. Key conclusions: (1) No Authenticode signing exists today — TAURI_SIGNING_PRIVATE_KEY is for the updater (minisign), not signtool. (2) SmartScreen does not inspect bundled DLLs; warnings are caused by the unsigned installer/.exe themselves and would exist with or without libmpv. (3) Once we add Authenticode signing, also sign libmpv-2.dll (in CI before tauri-action runs, so the build.rs copy carries the signature into the bundle) — large unsigned third-party DLLs occasionally trip AV heuristics. (4) Recommended workflow change documented (signCommand in tauri.conf.json, EV cert via thumbprint, mandatory /tr timestamp). (5) Code signing is separate tech debt — not blocking the Phase 6 ship.

title: Step 4.4 — mini-player chrome (minimal controls)
description:
Compact controls inside the mini player.

In scope (user-confirmed 2026-05-22):
- Play / Pause (already shipped)
- Restore + Close (already shipped)
- **Scrub timeline** (seekbar) — primary ask
- **10s skip backward** + **10s skip forward** buttons — primary ask

Explicitly out of scope (user-confirmed 2026-05-22):
- Pop-out button while minimized (no use case in mini)
- Fullscreen toggle (Restore → main player → fullscreen still works)

Behavior:
- Hide title / episode list (no space).
- Auto-hide after N seconds of inactivity, matching the main chrome's visibility hook.
- Buttons should sit above the drag layer (z-index handling already in MiniChrome).
- All new buttons get data-mini-no-drag="true" so they don't trigger anchor drag.

Related:
- prexu-jk0 covers the EOF / autoplay / Skip Intro state machine in mini — distinct concern (post-play flow), some overlap in chrome density. Coordinate scope when implementing.
- prexu-lhs (closed) shared the same drag/click event handling that the new buttons must respect.

## Required Skills
react, prexu
close_reason: Closed

title: Step 3.10 — buffered range reporting
description:
Add observe_property for demuxer-cache-time / demuxer-cache-state to event pump, emit player://buffered. SeekBar buffered indicator currently reads video.buffered — needs equivalent.

## Required Skills
rust,react
close_reason: Added demuxer-cache-time observe_property in event pump (REPLY_BUFFERED=5), throttled to 2 Hz (BUFFERED_THROTTLE=500ms) to keep IPC light during streaming. Emits player://buffered as f64 absolute seconds — same shape as HTML5 video.buffered.end(...) so the SeekBar buffered overlay needs no shape change. Subscribed in useNativePlayer.ts; replaced placeholder _setBuffered with real setBuffered. cargo check + tsc clean.

title: Step 3.8 — resume position via start_offset_ms
description:
Pass viewOffset to player_load_url start_offset_ms instead of seeking after load. Removes visible jump on resume.

## Required Skills

Verified in src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs:40-42 where it's converted to mpv loadfile's per-file 'start={seconds}' option, eliminating the visible-jump from a separate post-load seek. Code path verified end-to-end.

title: Step 3.9 — PostPlay screen listens to player://eof
description:
useNativePlayer must surface onEnded callback or isEnded state. PostPlayScreen + useEpisodeNavigation react. Verify offline watch sync still queues correctly.

## Required Skills

close_reason: Wired native path PostPlay trigger in Player.tsx. Previously, the 'ended' event listener only attached to player.videoRef which is null on the native (libmpv) path, so PostPlayScreen never appeared even when remainingCount>0. Now the same handleEnded callback subscribes to player://eof via Tauri's listen() on the native path, and to the videoRef 'ended' event on the HTML5 path. Trigger condition unchanged (remainingCount>0, not in WT session, not already shown). useNativePlayer's existing player://eof handler (timeline reporting + offline watch sync) is unaffected. tsc clean.

title: Step 3.4 — wire audio enhancements UI to player_set_af_chain
description:
Find existing audio enhancement preset selector (or add it). Map values to player_set_af_chain off/light/night. Persist selection in prefs.

## Required Skills

close_reason: Wired audio enhancements UI to native player commands in Player.tsx. Normalization preset (off/light/night) dispatches player_set_af_chain. Audio offset (0-500ms) dispatches player_set_audio_delay_ms. Initial values are applied once mpv signals ready (player.isLoading→false). Volume boost left on the main slider for now (mpv volume-max=200 already supports the 0-2x range; bumping to support 3x would need a separate mpv config change). Vitest 1163/1163 pass, tsc clean.

title: Step 3.5 — Watch Together drives native player
description:
useRemotePlayback currently calls togglePlay/seek on UsePlayerResult. Verify that path still works after Phase 2 hook abstraction. Test sync drift detection works against player://time-pos events. Relay server unchanged.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Two latent bugs surfaced and fixed: (1) useRemotePlayback.ts:65 read player.videoRef.current and bailed if null, dropping every remote_play/remote_pause/remote_seek silently on the native path. Now routed through player.seek + player.togglePlay, which work on both backends; isPlaying is checked before togglePlay so play/pause set the desired state rather than blindly toggle. (2) useSyncDriftDetection.ts:36 had the same pattern for drift correction — also routed through player.seek. playerRef indirection lets long-lived listeners read the latest player state without re-binding watchSync subs on every render. Drift detection works against player.currentTime which is already populated from player://time-pos events on the native path, so no other changes were needed. tsc clean, vitest 1163/1163.

title: Step 3.6 — offline downloads use player_load_url with file://

Replace convertFileSrc + asset protocol path in usePlayer with player_load_url(file:///path/to/local.mkv). Drops dependency on local proxy for downloaded files. Local proxy stays for HTML5 path.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Already implemented in useNativePlayer.ts:247-250. getLocalFilePath returns the absolute Windows path; mpv accepts native paths directly (no asset protocol or convertFileSrc needed). The original convertFileSrc + asset protocol path remains in the HTML5 path (usePlayer.ts:252-255) which is correct — it's only used by non-Windows platforms. file:// prefix not added because mpv handles raw Windows paths fine and avoids URL-encoding edge cases (spaces, special chars in download paths).

On native path, libmpv decodes everything natively. canDirectPlay should return true unless user has bandwidth-constrained transcoding selected. Update plex-playback decision logic with a native branch.

title: Step 3.1 — audio track switching to player_set_audio_track
description:
Update useStreamSelection to dispatch player_set_audio_track(id) for native path. Plex stream IDs may not match mpv aid track index — likely need mapping via mpv track-list events.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Replaced direct Plex stream ID → mpv aid/sid pass-through (broken: Plex IDs are global Library IDs like 234567, mpv expects 1-indexed track positions). New mapping uses positional lookup against the categorized audio/subtitle arrays (categorizeStreams preserves file order, so audioTracks[i] ↔ mpv aid=i+1). Action callbacks read tracks via audioTracksRef/subtitleTracksRef so they don't rebind on track-list changes. initPlayback now also dispatches the initial track selection (after load_url, before await for ready) so preferredAudioLanguage and 'subtitles off' both take effect on file load instead of waiting for a user click. Subtitle 'off' explicitly dispatches sid=null since mpv's default would show the first sub track. Note: positional mapping assumes Plex's Stream array order matches the file's track order; track-list-event-driven mapping (more robust for files where Plex injects ordering) deferred — open follow-up if mismatches surface in the wild. tsc clean.

title: Library filters: cross-filtered options + result-aware pruning (needs live PMS probe first)
description:
Remainder of prexu-6qi5.8 (year ranges shipped in PR #57). Cross-filtering = already-set filters constrain the other filters' offered values; result-aware pruning falls out of it.

BLOCKED ON VERIFICATION: no documentation confirms Plex's section directory endpoints accept filter constraints, and python-plexapi's listFilterChoices does not support it. Before designing, probe the live server (user can run this, or extract from app logs a working base URL + token):

  curl "http://<pms>:32400/library/sections/<sectionId>/year?genre=<genreKey>&X-Plex-Token=<token>"

Compare the returned year Directory list against the unfiltered call. If constrained -> implement per the original design in prexu-6qi5.8: refetch other filters' options with active filter set applied, debounced ~200ms, AbortController on change, cache key includes the active filter set, currently-edited filter keeps unconstrained values. If NOT constrained -> the fallback is client-side pruning only for loaded sections, or drop the feature (decide with user).

## Required Skills
react:hooks,typescript:typescript,prexu:prexu,testing:vitest

title: linux-test-plan K follow-up: advanced library filtering (result-aware values, cross-filtering, year ranges)
description:
## Feature — linux-test-plan K follow-up (enhancement, all K items passed)

Advanced library filtering, three parts:

1. **Result-aware filter values** — only offer filter values that actually return results in this library (no dead options that produce an empty grid).
2. **Cross-filtering** — already-set filters constrain the remaining filter options (e.g. Genre=Documentary → the Decade/Year list only shows years that have documentaries).
3. **Year ranges** — filter by a range (e.g. 1980–1989) instead of a single year.

## Suggested skills

- react:components, typescript:typescript, prexu:prexu (Plex API), testing:vitest
- Load cores first: /core:anti-fabrication, /core:git, /core:documentation

## Notes

Plex's own API supports much of this: `/library/sections/{id}/{field}` endpoints return the distinct values present in the section, and query filters compose (`?genre=X&year>=1980&year<=1989` style operators — verify exact PMS filter operator syntax against the prexu:prexu plex-api reference before designing). Cross-filtering may need a cheap `size=0` totalSize probe per candidate value or a smarter single query — design first, then implement. Keep the filter bar responsive: probes must be debounced/aborted like the K.1 grid fetches.

## Code pointers (scouted 2026-07-05)

- `src/hooks/useFilterOptions.ts` — fetches genre/year/contentRating/resolution in parallel (54–72), cached 10 min under `filterOptions:{sectionId}` (22, 40–49). Values already come from the section's own directory endpoints, so part 1 (result-aware values) is mostly true today *for single filters* — the gap is cross-filtering (options don't account for other active filters).
- `src/services/plex-library/filter.ts` — `getFilterOptions` hits `/library/sections/{id}/{filterType}` (79–100); `getLibraryItems` builds query params with **single-value `year`** (39–52). `LibraryFilters` type has no min/max — range support touches the type, the query builder, and the UI.
- `src/components/FilterBar.tsx` — year is a single `<select>` (120–133); range needs a two-ended control; active-filter chips at 71–75.


## Required Skills
react:components,typescript:typescript,prexu:prexu,testing:vitest
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Partially delivered: year ranges shipped in PR #57 (operator syntax year>=/year<= verified against plexopedia + Arcanemagus wiki; legacy ?year= URLs and persisted filters migrate to yearMin==yearMax). Cross-filtering and result-aware pruning NOT implemented: no documentation source confirms the section directory endpoints (/library/sections/{id}/{filterType}) accept other active filters as constraints, and python-plexapi's listFilterChoices explicitly does not support it. Needs an empirical probe against a live PMS before building — follow-up issue filed. Also: PR #57 + PR #56 had a test-isolation collision on main (shared useSearchParams mock stub leaking across suites) — hotfixed in PR #58.

title: perf(api) batch: quick wins — sections/account caches, collections watched-map, parallel load-all
description: (1) getLibrarySections has no service-level cache (base.ts:71-82) — direct service callers refetch it; read from the useLibrary cached source or add short-TTL cache. (2) useSectionCollections fetches EVERY collection's children (3 at a time) just to compute fully-watched (useCollections.ts:140-195) — prefer leafCount/viewedLeafCount aggregates, fall back to children only when absent. (3) usePaginatedLibrary load-all fetches ~16 sequential 200-item batches on filtered alpha views (:109-138) — bounded Promise.all pool (3-4 concurrent) + coalesce setItems per few batches (the per-batch setItems also rebuilds the filtered list ~16x, react sweep #7). (4) Consider trimmed metadata fetches — zero field/element selection exists anywhere (no excludeElements/excludeFields usages).
close_reason: PR #45: getLibrarySections 60s svc cache (structuredClone on hit to preserve identity contract), watched-map from leafCount/viewedLeafCount aggregates with children fallback, loadAll bounded pool (4) with ordered coalesced setItems, keep-prior-items isStale flag + LibraryView aria-busy dimming, ItemDetail credit-aggregate memo; fixed isLoadingMore stuck on batch failure

title: ux batch: loading/error/scroll consistency (ActorDetail, DiscoverDetail, Requests, SearchResults, PlaylistsBrowser, library filter swap)
description:
Five small consistency fixes, one pattern: (1) ActorDetail.tsx:211-222 + DiscoverDetail.tsx:68-82 — bare spinner (no delay/skeleton) and plain

error with no retry; use ErrorState+onRetry+skeleton+~150ms spinner delay. (2) Requests.tsx:119-143 — empty-state flashes before first load; useContentRequests exposes no isLoading flag, add one and gate. (3) SearchResults.tsx:46 — ErrorState without onRetry. (4) PlaylistsBrowser — only grid page missing useScrollRestoration; back-nav jumps to top. (5) usePaginatedLibrary.ts:85 — setItems([]) on cache-miss filter/sort change blanks the grid; keep prior items dimmed (aria-busy) until the new page resolves. Also LOW from the react sweep: useMemo the ActorDetail credit aggregates (ActorDetail.tsx:76-108, ItemDetail.tsx:519-538).
close_reason: PR #46: ActorDetail/DiscoverDetail skeleton+ErrorState+retry with 150ms delay (useDelayedFlag), Requests isLoading gate, SearchResults retry, PlaylistsBrowser scroll restoration, ActorDetail credit memos

title: ux: toast on download complete/failure
description: useDownloads.ts:128-241 handles complete/error/cancelled transitions with state+logger only; the only download toast is 'Download started' (DownloadButton.tsx:75, useMediaContextMenu.tsx:200). A user who navigates away is never told the download finished or that all 3 auto-retries exhausted. FIX: emit success/failure toasts from the progress-listener transition. (Distinct from the Downloads-page polish pass tracked in prexu-5vc.)
close_reason: PR #46: success toast on download complete, failure toast after retries exhaust, none on cancel; toast dispatcher threaded into useDownloadsState via AppProviders

title: ux: hover-intent prefetch of item detail metadata from PosterCard
description: No prefetch call sites exist in the app; PosterCard hover (:131) only flips visual state. Once the ItemDetail cache child lands, add a short hover/focus intent delay (~150ms) that warms getItemMetadata into the same cache so the hottest navigation feels instant. Depends on prexu-0szx.3 (needs the cache to prefetch INTO).

title: perf(react): split sessions/activities out of the ServerActivity context value
description: useServerActivity.ts:404-414 bundles fast-churning sessions/activities into the one memoized context value; scanningIds were already correctly moved to a useSyncExternalStore selector (:160-173) but useDashboard consumes the context for completionCounter only (useDashboard.ts:36) and re-renders on every session update while ANYTHING plays on the server — re-running three shelf .map()s and (via the PosterCard child) all ~80 dashboard cards. FIX: same split as scanningIds — sessions/activities behind their own context or external-store selector so counter-only consumers don't subscribe to session churn.
close_reason: PR #47: completionCounter moved to its own external store (CompletionCounterStore mirroring ScanningStore pattern) with useCompletionCounter() selector; useDashboard no longer subscribes to session churn (test: counter-only consumer render count stays 1 while sessions change)

title: perf(react): PosterCard memo is defeated everywhere by unstable props
description: memo() at src/components/PosterCard.tsx:529 never skips: every list passes fresh-identity props — inline onClick/onContextMenu arrows, onPlay={getPlayHandler(item)} (usePlayAction.tsx:51 returns a new closure per call), mediaBadges={getItemMediaBadges(item)} (media-badges.ts:144 returns a fresh array). Blast radius is the full card list on Dashboard (~80 cards) / PlaylistDetail / CollectionsBrowser whenever the parent re-renders (compounds with the ServerActivity churn child). FIX: stable item/onSelect(ratingKey) card API; compute badge arrays once at fetch-map time; memoize play handlers per ratingKey. Land AFTER the virtualization child so remaining hot lists are known.
close_reason: PR #47: getPlayHandler caches one closure per ratingKey (fresh data via refs at click time), getItemMediaBadges memoized per item via WeakMap, useStableItemCallback applied to Dashboard three shelves; regression test proves memo skips 5 parent re-renders and re-runs on item change

title: Player: click-on-video pause feels delayed (~DOUBLE_CLICK_MS gate for dblclick disambiguation)
description: On-hardware observation 2026-07-03: clicking the video surface to pause takes ~0.5s to take effect; the pause button is instant. Cause: useVideoClickHandling defers togglePlay by DOUBLE_CLICK_MS on single click to disambiguate from double-click fullscreen (src/hooks/player/useVideoClickHandling.ts:30). Fix options: (a) optimistic pause — togglePlay immediately on first click; if a second click lands within the window, toggle back + fullscreen (YouTube/mpv behavior, brief flicker acceptable); (b) reduce DOUBLE_CLICK_MS. Prefer (a).

title: Linux: player transitions not visually instant (exit AND minimize-restore) — ~1s last frame, ~1s navy, then target state
description: Post-fix observation (2026-07-03, after prexu-3iv3 fixes): exit works reliably but takes ~2s visually: stop click -> last video frame holds ~1s -> prexu navy ~1s -> dashboard. Design intent (usePlayerLifecycle docblock) is 'back where they launched from instantly'. Investigate: destroy() awaits ~300ms before returning; prepareNavAway paints body opaque one render-cycle before unmount; dashboard repaint after webview recomposite. Cosmetic polish, low priority.
notes: 2026-07-03 (post prexu-skr2 fix): minimize->expand now WORKS but shows the same visual staging as exit: last frame holds ~1s -> prexu navy ~1s -> full video. Both transitions share the busy/ready -> transparent-class choreography (useTransparentWindow re-add is deferred 2x rAF + forced layout) plus restoreFromMinimize's wait-for-IPC-before-flip design. Investigate as one polish pass: tighten the busy/ready windows on Linux (the WebView-underneath-paint rationale is a WebView2/Windows artifact and may not apply to the webkitgtk single-surface path), and see if the restore flip can be optimistic on Linux since mpv margins clear near-instantly (the Windows SetWindowPos latency that motivated wait-for-IPC does not exist here).
close_reason: Fixed in PR #40: Linux skips the three WebView2-motivated staging mechanisms — busy-window class drop + 2xrAF ready deferral (useTransparentWindow), wait-for-IPC restore flip (PlayerContext), and the early opaque paint on exit (prepareNavAway). Windows/HTML5 unchanged. On-hardware verify steps in issue comment; if residual delay remains, instrument player_unload IPC duration next.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Fix in PR #40. All three staging sources were WebView2/Windows rationales applied cross-platform: (1) useTransparentWindow now ignores busy and re-adds on ready without the 2x-rAF wait on Linux — a transparent body on webkitgtk exposes our GLArea, never the desktop; (2) restoreFromMinimize flips React optimistically on Linux (margin clear is ~1-2 frames, no SetWindowPos latency); (3) prepareNavAway keeps the transparent class on Linux so exit shows last frame until the dashboard paints. NOTE: the issue also mentioned destroy()'s ~300ms and dashboard-repaint-after-recomposite as investigation leads — not touched; if on-hardware testing still shows residual delay, instrument player_unload IPC duration next. ON-HARDWARE VERIFY: (a) stop from playback → dashboard appears with video visible until swap, no navy stage; (b) mini → expand → video expands in place, no navy stage; (c) Windows unchanged.

title: Linux popout player: second GTK window + GLArea + render context
description: Deferred from prexu-axj4.5 (user decision 2026-07-02): Windows popout moves video into a separate always-on-top window; on the Linux render-API path this needs a second GTK window with its own GLArea and mpv render context (or a second render target off the same context). Until then the popout control is gated off on Linux (SUPPORTS_PLAYER_WINDOWING-style gate). Do after the single-surface path has soaked.
close_reason: Implemented in PR #41 as a main-window morph via Tauri/GTK ops — the second-GTK-window premise was stale (Windows popout never used a separate window; the single-surface GLArea follows the window allocation). X11 full parity; Wayland floats without placement/keep-above (documented). On-hardware verify plan in issue comment.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: Implemented in PR #41 — NOT as a second GTK window: the issue premise was stale. Windows popout is a main-window morph (shrink main + always-on-top + undecorate, stash/restore via pre_popout_geometry), so the Linux port rides the existing single-surface GLArea (it follows the window allocation automatically; no second render context, no host resync). Linux commands use Tauri/GTK ops: Monitor::work_area + shared corner_origin for placement, inner-size stash for a drift-free round-trip, minimize-margin clear on enter for mutual exclusion. Wayland: set_position/keep-above are protocol no-ops → shrink-in-place, drag via PopoutDragStrip (begin_move_drag works), pin compositor-side; X11 full parity. ON-HARDWARE VERIFY: (1) popout button appears on Linux native; (2) enter → small undecorated corner window (X11: bottom-right + on-top; Wayland: shrinks in place), video fills it, chrome + drag strip work; (3) drag/resize then exit → main restores to pre-popout geometry, size/corner persist to next enter (X11); (4) enter popout while minimized → margins clear, no mini inset in the popout; (5) exit player while popped out → lifecycle exits popout first, geometry restores; (6) edge drag-resize works while undecorated (wry undecorated-resize handler — the axj4.3 reparent kept its grandparent downcast valid; watch for any click-SIGABRT regression here, prexu-axj4.3 bug class).

title: Verify Linux native player on an X11 session (GLX path)
description: Carried from prexu-axj4.3 / the render-API ADR open questions: the GtkOverlay+GLArea architecture is windowing-agnostic by construction and the GL resolver loads both EGL and GLX, but only Wayland/EGL was exercised on the dev box. Log into an X11 session and verify: video renders (GLX proc resolution path), resize behavior, transparency compositing, controls fade. Also re-check DMABUF-renderer behavior under X11. Suggested skills: rust, tauri, anti-fabrication.

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: 2026-07-05 hardware test session: user ran the full Wayland pass (sections A-E, G-L of docs/linux-on-hardware-test-plan.md). Decision: X11 is maintenance-only, not actively developed — no X11 verification will be performed for now ('Wayland is the future'). Deferring this issue out of the ready queue; revisit only if an X11-specific regression is reported.

title: Windows: enable audio-normalize-downmix (multichannel→stereo clipping parity)
description: Found during prexu-axj4 Linux work: mpv's default 5.1/7.1→stereo downmix clips without normalization. The Linux native player now sets audio-channels=auto-safe + audio-normalize-downmix=yes (lifecycle.rs, Linux-gated). Windows ships identical mpv defaults through the same code path and has the same clipping risk on multichannel content. Apply the same two options on Windows and verify on a multichannel title. Suggested skills: rust, tauri.

title: Linux/Wayland: window doesn't reopen on its previous monitor/position (re-centers on primary)
description:
On KDE Plasma Wayland, moving the Prexu window to a secondary monitor and relaunching reopens it centered on the primary monitor instead of where it was last placed. Observed on nobara-pc during prexu-duna.3 / z5mz testing.

## Cause
tauri-plugin-window-state (src-tauri/src/lib.rs:464, Builder::default) saves+restores window size AND position. On Wayland this is a protocol limitation: clients cannot position their own toplevel surfaces — the compositor decides placement — so the position-restore is a silent no-op and KWin re-centers the window. (Window SIZE restore still works.) This is not fully fixable in-app on native Wayland.

## Options / mitigations
- User-side now: a KWin window rule for Prexu (Window-specific settings -> Position 'Remember' or 'Apply Initially') makes KDE place it where you want — the reliable workaround today.
- App-side: none reliable on native Wayland currently. Position restore DOES work under XWayland (GDK_BACKEND=x11), but forcing XWayland has other tradeoffs (and conflicts with the native-Wayland direction) — not recommended just for this.
- Revisit if/when Tauri/wry expose a Wayland positioning protocol (e.g. xdg-positioner for toplevels) — track upstream.

## Acceptance
- Decide: document as a known Wayland limitation (with the KWin-rule workaround in docs/linux-dev.md), or implement an app-side mitigation if one becomes available. At minimum, don't regress size restore.

## Required Skills
tauri, rust, documentation
close_reason: Known native-Wayland limitation (client can't position its own toplevel; tauri-plugin-window-state position-restore is a no-op there, size still restores). No reliable in-app fix. KWin window-rule workaround documented in docs/linux-dev.md (Wayland runtime notes), with kscreen-doctor steps to find the monitor origin. Revisit if Tauri/wry expose a Wayland positioning protocol.

title: Wire relay-server integration tests into CI
description: The ci job (.github/workflows/ci.yml) runs 'cd relay-server && cargo clippy' but never 'cargo test', so relay-server WS integration flakes/failures (prexu-waec keepalive race, prexu-3a78 rate-limit) slipped through. Add a 'cd relay-server && cargo test' step to the ubuntu ci job (these are tokio/axum tests, no Windows libmpv needed, so they belong in the ubuntu job not the headless-mpv Windows job). BLOCKED until the suite is green: prexu-3a78 (test_rate_limiting) must be fixed first, else the new step makes CI red. Acceptance: a failing relay integration test fails CI.
close_reason: Added 'Relay server tests' step (cd relay-server && cargo test) to the ubuntu ci job after relay clippy. Validated locally: 24 integration + lib tests pass. A failing relay test now fails CI.

title: relay-server test_rate_limiting fails deterministically (pre-existing, not in CI)
description: relay-server tests/integration.rs::test_rate_limiting asserts found_rate_limit after sending 35 pings (limit 30/window) but fails 3/3 on main (verified by baselining without the prexu-waec change) — fast assertion failure (~0.05s), so the rate-limit AuthError is never received. Separate from prexu-waec (keepalive race). Not caught by CI: the ci job runs 'cd relay-server && cargo clippy' but no relay cargo test step. Investigate connection.rs rate-limit path (RATE_LIMIT_MAX/RATE_LIMIT_WINDOW, msg_timestamps sliding window) vs the test's send cadence. Consider adding relay integration tests to CI once green.
close_reason: Test-only fix: assert disconnect (close or AuthError) instead of requiring the best-effort AuthError; tolerate mid-burst send closure. 24/24 pass 5x deterministic. Server rate limiter was working correctly.

title: Add Linux src-tauri build step to CI
description:
The Ubuntu `ci` job does not compile `src-tauri` (only frontend lint/tsc/vitest/E2E + relay clippy). src-tauri is built only by the Windows `headless-mpv` job. This is why a Linux build break landed on main unnoticed (PR #15 fixed it: an ungated Windows-only re-export in player/mod.rs, the build_libmpv feature, and the libmpv-2.dll bundle resource all broke off-Windows compilation).

Now that src-tauri links the system libmpv on Linux (no MPV_SOURCE needed), CI can and should compile it on Ubuntu to catch cross-platform regressions automatically.
acceptance_criteria:
- ci.yml installs the libmpv + webkit2gtk system deps (libmpv-dev/mpv-libs-devel, webkit2gtk-4.1, gtk3, librsvg) on the ubuntu runner
- Adds a step running `cargo build -p prexu` (or `cargo clippy`) for src-tauri on Linux
- A Linux-only compile break (e.g. an ungated #[cfg(target_os="windows")] item) fails CI
- macOS build coverage considered/noted (deferred is fine per the cross-platform ADR)
notes: Discovered while making the app build on Nobara Linux (PR #15, branch was feature/linux-build-support). See docs/adr-native-player-cross-platform.md for the platform strategy.
close_reason: ci.yml compiles src-tauri on Linux (libmpv-dev + webkit2gtk deps) and now gates on clippy -D warnings. A Linux-only compile break fails CI. Verified in committed workflow.

title: [bug] activity 'Now Playing' lingers for a long time after stopping playback
description: After stopping playback in Prexu, the user's session keeps showing in the Activity / Now Playing section for a long time (screenshot: Dragon Ball test item still 'BadDong · Prexu' after stop). Suggests Prexu isn't sending a prompt 'stopped' timeline report to Plex on stop, so the Plex session only expires on its own idle timeout. Investigate the stop/timeline-report path (player::timeline fire_stopped_report) — confirm a stopped state=stopped report is POSTed to /:/timeline immediately on stop/teardown, not just on natural EOF. Unrelated to composition hosting.

description: Tab in/out of the webview, click-to-focus, and focus restoration after Alt+Tab / minimize-restore. wry routes parent WM_SETFOCUS -> controller.MoveFocus(PROGRAMMATIC); verify it holds under composition where there is no Chrome child HWND. Blocked on C4a.

Only 3a (Tab OUT of webview to native chrome) untested; app has no native chrome to tab to, so low value. Closing as parity-met.

NOT yet exercised on a >100% monitor — log showed no scale-factor change during window moves (all test monitors same DPI). VERIFY-BLOCKED: needs a 150% monitor to confirm crisp text + scale event.

comments.author: Nick Williams

HONEST NOTE: log shows NO ScaleFactorChanged / manual 'rasterization scale ->' event fired during the test; the install-time SetShouldDetectMonitorScaleChanges(true) drove WebView2's own auto-rescale. Our set_scale_factor->set_rasterization_scale manual re-apply path is an unexercised backup (fires if Tauri emits ScaleFactorChanged). Closing on visual pass.

If candidate still mislocated, the fix shape needs revisiting.

comments.text: Impl landed (NotifyParentWindowPositionChanged on move). NOT tested — needs a CJK IME installed. VERIFY-BLOCKED until runtime IME test.

Gates C4b-C4f: only the FAILs get fixed (avoid speculative reimplementation). Checklist: (1) keyboard — type in search box, arrow-key nav, player shortcuts; (2) IME — type CJK via Microsoft IME, candidate window position; (3) focus — Tab in/out of webview, click-to-focus after Alt+Tab, focus after minimize/restore; (4) DPI — run on 150%/200% monitor + drag window across monitors of differing scale, check crispness + hit-testing; (5) drag/drop — drag a file onto the window; (6) native context menu — right-click editable field; (7) browser accelerator keys (F5/Ctrl+R/Ctrl+P) behave as configured. Grounded in code: wry creates the composition controller against the WRY_WEBVIEW container hwnd (vendor/wry/src/webview2/mod.rs:164); container WM_SETFOCUS does SetFocus(GW_CHILD) which has no Chrome child HWND under composition (mod.rs:221); mouse+cursor already forwarded (composition_host.rs:138).

comments.author: Nick Williams
comments.text: C3e VERIFIED + DONE. Runtime-verified on real GPU (PREXU_COMPOSITION_HOST=1): window resize, maximize/restore, fullscreen toggle, and mini player in ALL FOUR corners + mini resize — video tracks correctly, no see-through gaps. Architecture: in composition mode the video DComp visual is a child of the main window (moves with it free); only swapchain pixel SIZE tracks window resize (render thread, ResizeBuffers + re-import FBO via re-runnable AngleGl) and the visual OFFSET tracks the mini inset corner (composition_host::set_video_offset, main-thread DComp op). sync_geometry/sync_geometry_now/apply_host_geometry route size->render thread + offset->visual when host is None. resync_host dispatches apply_host_geometry to UI thread in composition mode (legacy path unchanged). Popout resizes the MAIN window (not a separate one) so it uses the same verified path. Commits: 13f986a(resize) ddf253c(mini offset). Bug found+fixed in verify: initial version forwarded only size, leaving visual pinned at origin -> mini showed wrong corner.

title: C3d: replace mpv --wid embedding with libmpv2 RenderContext (DComp video visual)
description: Drop the 'wid' property (lifecycle.rs:135) + force-window; switch vo gpu-next->libmpv; create a libmpv2::render RenderContext (OpenGL/ANGLE) drawing into a shared D3D11 texture presented on the video DComp visual. Render loop integrated with the app event pump. PRESERVE every existing mpv property (hwdec, keep-open, osd, cache tuning), audio filter chains (af presets), and libass subtitle styling — all unchanged (they are property sets, not render-path). Depends on C3b.
notes:
C3d IN PROGRESS. Reference: spike/dcomp-hwdec (C3b) = working prototype of the whole pipeline.
PIPELINE (per frame): render.update() -> mpv renders into ANGLE FBO (=shared D3D11 texture) -> CopyResource into composition swapchain backbuffer -> swapchain.Present -> DComp video visual updates. Setup: shared BGRA texture + CreateSwapChainForComposition (video visual content) + ANGLE EGL (libEGL/libGLESv2) + mpv vo=libmpv + RenderContext::new(OpenGLInitParams{get_proc_address}). Tree: root->[video(bottom), webview(top)].
INCREMENT 1 DONE (commit 14c0244): secure ANGLE loader (abs-path LoadLibraryEx SEARCH_SYSTEM32|DLL_LOAD_DIR; SHA-256 pin + WinVerifyTrust Authenticode; SetDefaultDllDirectories hardening). DLLs are Microsoft-signed, bundled + copied next to exe. 198 tests pass.
REMAINING:
- Inc2: video_render module — lift egl_angle.rs (D3D11 shared texture, ANGLE EGL ctx, FBO import) via the secure loader; add video DComp visual to composition_host tree (below webview); create swapchain. Compile green.
- Inc3: mpv vo=libmpv + RenderContext (composition mode only; legacy wid path when flag off); dedicated render thread owning the GL context, driven by mpv render update callback; per-frame render->copy->Present. PRESERVE all mpv props (hwdec/keep-open/osd/cache/af/subs).
- Inc4: wire to playback lifecycle (ensure_init/destroy), size the video visual to the window, teardown. Runtime-verify on GPU (PREXU_COMPOSITION_HOST=1): video plays composited under React UI, hwdec engaged (read hwdec-current).
THREADING: D3D11 device free-threaded; swapchain Present off-main-thread OK; DComp tree built once on main thread (visual content=swapchain), Present updates visual without per-frame Commit. ANGLE GL ctx thread-affine -> render thread owns it.
Needs interactive GPU runtime verification (like C3c).
close_reason: Closed
