title: Path C: DComp visual hosting (fork wry + mpv render API)
description:
The 'proper' single-surface fix if Path A fails. Convert the webview to ICoreWebView2CompositionController so mpv's swapchain visual and the webview visual live in one DirectComposition tree on the main window with true z-order + transparency. 

SCOPE (large): (1) fork wry or build a custom WebView2 host using ICoreWebView2CompositionController + RootVisualTarget; re-implement input forwarding, focus, IME, and DPI/rasterization-scale handling that windowed mode does for free. (2) Replace mpv --wid embedding with the libmpv render API: OpenGL via ANGLE + WGL_NV_DX_interop2 to share the GL color texture into a D3D11 texture, presented on a DComp visual (keeps hwdec). libmpv2 4.1.0 RenderContext covers the OpenGL path; software path would need raw libmpv2_sys FFI and defeats hwdec.

No Tauri/wry Windows precedent (demonstrated only on macOS). Highest risk/effort. Only pursue if Path A's sibling-airspace proves unworkable.
design:
# Path C plan — single-surface DComp (verified 2026-06-21 vs stack: tauri 2.10, wry 0.54.2, tao 0.34.5, webview2-com 0.38.2, windows 0.61, libmpv2 v4+sys)

## Premise (CONFIRMED)
DWM composites a top-level window's DirectComposition visual tree into its per-window surface; Alt+Tab thumbnails + Windows.Graphics.Capture(CreateForWindow) include it. So putting BOTH the WebView2 UI and the mpv video on ONE DComp tree on the main HWND gives video-in-Alt+Tab + true alpha overlay. (MS DComp/WGC docs.)

## Corrections to Phase 0
- Interop = EGL share-handle (ANGLE): D3D11 texture w/ D3D11_RESOURCE_MISC_SHARED -> IDXGIResource::GetSharedHandle -> eglCreatePbufferFromClientBuffer(EGL_D3D_TEXTURE_2D_SHARE_HANDLE_ANGLE). NOT WGL_NV_DX_interop2 (only native desktop-GL / ANGLE non-default GL backend, DComp-hostile). media-kit precedent uses EGL path.
- libmpv2 4.x safe render API = libmpv2::render {RenderContext, RenderParam, RenderParamApiType::OpenGl, OpenGLInitParams}; OpenGL ONLY. sw backend reachable only via libmpv2-sys raw FFI (defeats hwdec). render ctx vs --wid mutually exclusive (1 render ctx per core; created before VO).
- WebView2 visual hosting: ICoreWebView2Environment3::CreateCoreWebView2CompositionController(HWND,handler) -> ICoreWebView2CompositionController (QI to ICoreWebView2Controller). Host must: SetRootVisualTarget(IDCompositionVisual) (Rust binding name; C++ put_RootVisualTarget) + Commit own DComp device + set Bounds; forward input SendMouseInput/SendPointerInput(+CreateCoreWebView2PointerInfo); cursor via get_Cursor/SystemCursorId/CursorChanged in WM_SETCURSOR; drag/drop via CompositionController3 + IDropTarget; focus via Controller side; IME UNVERIFIED. All bound in webview2-com-sys 0.38.2. Classic IDCompositionVisual / Windows.UI.Composition only — NOT WinAppSDK Microsoft.UI.Composition.
- wry 0.54.2 = windowed controller only (CreateCoreWebView2ControllerWithOptions); no composition path/feature. Tauri PlatformWebview exposes windowed controller()+environment() only. => fork wry OR bypass wry.

## Staged plan (each phase is a GATE; throwaway harness before touching the app)
### C0 — Capture feasibility prototype (standalone Rust bin, NOT the app) — DECISIVE, cheapest kill-gate
mpv OpenGL render via ANGLE -> shared D3D11 texture (EGL share-handle) -> IDCompositionVisual -> CreateTargetForHwnd on a plain Win32 window. Then Alt+Tab + WGC capture the window. PASS = video visible AND appears in the thumbnail. Test windowed AND fullscreen/borderless (independent-flip edge: iflip can escape WGC -> black). KILL: if a DComp video visual can't be captured, Path C is dead.

### C1 — WebView2 visual-hosting spike (standalone)
CreateCoreWebView2CompositionController against a bare HWND; RootVisualTarget into a DComp tree; forward mouse/pointer/cursor/focus; load the real React build; confirm clickable + transparent over a solid visual. Proves the airspace killer + the input plumbing cost.

### C2 — Combine (standalone): one DComp tree = video visual (z below) + webview visual (transparent, z above)
Confirm overlay + capture TOGETHER outside Tauri. This proves the whole goal before any app surgery.

### C3 — Land in app (fork wry OR bypass)
Fork wry to create the composition controller + expose RootVisualTarget; OR bypass wry (own DispatcherQueueController + CreateCoreWebView2CompositionController against Tauri main HWND, drop wry webview). Replace mpv --wid with render context. Rewire geometry/fullscreen/popout/minimize (all assume a top-level host HWND today) to DComp visual transforms. Delete WS_POPUP host_window.rs + taskbar_preview.rs iconic workaround.

### C4 — Re-implement windowed-mode freebies lost in visual hosting
input edge cases, IME, DPI/rasterization scale, drag/drop, native context menus, focus cycling, multi-monitor DPI.

## Top risks / unknowns
1. Must fork/bypass wry — re-implement WebView2 input/focus/cursor/IME (large, no Rust precedent).
2. shared-texture -> IDCompositionVisual leg: no found end-to-end precedent (media-kit stops at Flutter texture registry). UNVERIFIED — C0 settles it.
3. IME under visual hosting UNVERIFIED.
4. independent-flip fullscreen edge could drop video from capture — C0 tests it.
5. bypass loses Tauri webview integration; fork = maintenance burden. Decide at C3 from C1 learnings.
6. libmpv2 sw fallback needs sys FFI + defeats hwdec.

## Precedent
No wry/tao/Tauri project does single-shared-DComp WebView2+video (Stremio uses stacked transparent child HWNDs = airspace). C++/C# samples exist for the primitives (WebView2SampleWinComp; pabloko WebView2->D3D11+DComp+GraphicsCaptureItem::CreateFromVisual; WPF WebView2CompositionControl). Would be first known wry/tao/Tauri impl.

title: Native player: ~1s host-window lag during maximize/restore in playback
description: On maximize/restore during playback the mpv host window visibly lags ~1s behind the Tauri chrome (dual top-level window desync; host can't ride DWM maximize animation, repositions only on the animation-delayed WM_SIZE). Pre-existing, not caused by Wave B refactor. Options: proactive monitor-work-area apply on state transition, immediate throttle-bypass on maximize/restore, or disable maximize animation. Needs ms-precision log capture to confirm magnitude.
close_reason: Closed

title: Minor structure/security cleanups batch
description: (1) QueueContext.tsx:48-56 validate JSON.parse shape, fall back to emptyQueue. (2) PlayerControls.tsx 26 props pass-through -> group into pip/minimize/queue/subtitleSearch objects. (3) getItemMediaBadges duplicated Dashboard.tsx:45-51 + LibraryView.tsx:68-74 -> src/utils/media-badges.ts. (4) watch-sync.ts tag WatchSync -> ws; pass Error not String(err). (5) downloads.rs:81-82 reject path separators/.. in rating_key+file_name. (6) build.rs:33-37 require MPV_SOURCE in release builds (C:/libmpv DLL-planting). (9) useSkipSegments.ts:135-171 render-phase queueMicrotask -> useEffect or pure derived state. (10) useAuth.ts:272-286 useMemo useAuthState return value.

(3) CollectionDetail.tsx:75-90 sequential independent awaits -> Promise.all. (4) QueuePanel.tsx:108 index-in-key reorderable list -> ratingKey. (5) Player.tsx:706-720 inline posterUrl lambdas + per-render derivePostPlayDetailProps/deriveUpNextSlice -> useCallback/useMemo.

title: Auto re-discover server connection when stored URL unreachable
description:
## Why
User changed router; Plex server got new DHCP IP. App restores stored server URL blindly at boot (useAuth.ts:70 getServer) with no reachability check. All API calls fail silently, home page shows skeletons forever, relay WS (derived from same stale URI in storage/server.ts deriveRelayUrl) also fails. Only manual fix is avatar menu -> Change Server, which is not discoverable when everything just looks broken.

## What
On boot (and on sustained fetch failures), ping the stored server connection (e.g. /identity with short timeout). If unreachable, call discoverServers(token) and try to match the stored server by clientIdentifier; if found with different connection URI, transparently update saved server + relay derivation and retry. If discovery fails or no match, surface a clear 'server unreachable' state with a Change Server CTA instead of skeletons.

## Acceptance
- Boot with stale server IP -> app self-heals within seconds when server is discoverable under new IP
- If not discoverable: visible error state with Change Server button, no infinite skeletons
- Relay URL re-derived after server URI update
- Tests for the re-resolve path (mock discoverServers)
close_reason: Closed

title: Popout: snap window to nearest corner on drag-release
description:
In the old (pre-native-player) popout, the floating window snapped to the nearest screen corner. Current native popout is a free-floating window — dragging moves it freely with no snap (nearest_corner only runs at exit to persist the corner, it does NOT reposition the live window). 

Goal: on drag-release of the popout window, compute nearest_corner (already exists in popout.rs) from the live GetWindowRect + work area, then SetWindowPos the window to that corner's corner_origin. 

Notes:
- Needs a window-moved / drag-end signal. Options: tao WindowEvent::Moved (debounced to detect drag-end) on the main window while in popout mode, or a frontend pointerup on a drag handle.
- Reuse existing nearest_corner + corner_origin + work-area resolution in popout.rs.
- Interacts with prexu-6qz (borderless): a borderless popout needs its own drag region anyway; corner-snap should hook the same drag-end.
- Keep it gated to popout mode only (popout=true); do not affect normal windowed playback.
close_reason: wontfix: conflated with old minimize-snap; free-floating drag delivered via 6qz, no corner-snap wanted

title: Borderless popout: drop OS title bar on enter, restore on exit
description: Popout currently keeps the main window's OS title bar (Prexu + min/max/close), so it looks like a shrunk app, not a clean floating mini-player. Add set_decorations(false) on player_enter_popout and set_decorations(true) on player_exit_popout. Needs core:window:allow-set-decorations capability. Native-player single-window design (mpv host is child of main), so this restyles the main window in-place.
notes:
ATTEMPT 1 (set_decorations toggle) REVERTED (commit 72be5c449d) — two regressions confirmed via hands-on:
1. No drag: borderless window has no OS title bar -> no draggable region. Needs an explicit data-tauri-drag-region handle in the popout chrome.
2. ~5s black-but-audio on exit: set_decorations(true) on exit triggers Win32 SWP_FRAMECHANGED non-client recalc that stalls the WebView2 transparent re-arm ([player:transparent] applied fired 3-5s late vs same-second pre-borderless). 
PROPER FIX needs: (a) a drag region, (b) a borderless mechanism that avoids the frame-change repaint (e.g. decorations:false set once at window creation, or a dedicated borderless popout window), NOT a per-popout decorations toggle. Also still wants corner-snap (separate). Scope as its own follow-up, out of wave-3.
close_reason: Closed

title: Validate + harden headless_mpv Windows CI job (fetch mpv dev kit)
description: prexu-fbk.2 shipped the headless libmpv integration test (tests/headless_mpv.rs, #[ignore]+#[cfg(windows)], passes locally — lavfi synthetic source, asserts init->FileLoaded->duration->eof). The .github/workflows/ci.yml 'headless-mpv' job that runs it is currently continue-on-error:true because it is UNVALIDATED on GitHub windows-latest and likely incomplete: it hand-generates mpv.lib via dumpbin/lib.exe but does NOT supply the libmpv C headers (MPV_SOURCE/64/include/mpv) that libmpv2-sys needs at build time. Fix: fetch the official mpv dev kit (ships mpv.lib + headers) into MPV_SOURCE in CI instead of hand-building the import lib; confirm the job goes green on a real runner; then remove continue-on-error and make it a required check. Skills: github-actions, rust, prexu.
notes: DONE + verified green on real windows-latest runner (run 26677017552): ci job 4m53s (tsc/unit/cargo/e2e) + headless-mpv 4m24s (fetch+sha256 verify, build, test pass). Approach: pinned+checksummed DLL fetch (zhongfly mpv-winbuild 2026-05-29-13a3e3ad0e, sha 2c4883..ce5d) + vendored MSVC mpv.lib/headers. Also fixed 2 latent CI blockers surfaced by first real run: perf.test.tsx wall-clock benches (skipIf CI) and webkit e2e (chromium-only, WebView2 is the real target).
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike + build: headless libmpv integration test (vo=null)
description: The only way to exercise the real libmpv FFI path (init/warmup prexu-204, load -> FileLoaded -> duration -> eof event emission) without a window/display. SPIKE FIRST: confirm the bundled libmpv-2.dll has the lavfi input (so av://lavfi:testsrc=duration=1 can synthesize a 1s clip with no fixture file); if not, fall back to a tiny committed fixture media file. THEN: a Rust tests/ integration test that creates mpv with vo=null, loads the test source, pumps events briefly, and asserts the init->FileLoaded->duration->eof signal sequence. Gate behind #[ignore] locally + a dedicated CI job that has libmpv-2.dll present. Acceptance: spike documents lavfi availability; if viable, an integration test that passes in CI with the dll.
notes: SPIKE: lavfi AVAILABLE in bundled libmpv-2.dll (libmpv2 4.1.0) - av://lavfi:testsrc loads headless, no fixture needed. BUILT tests/headless_mpv.rs (#[ignore]+#[cfg(windows)], vo=null/ao=null, hard timeout 20s/2000 iters); passes locally, all 4 signals (init->FileLoaded->duration->eof) in 10 iters/0.05s. CI windows-latest job added but continue-on-error (UNVALIDATED on real runner + missing mpv include headers) - hardening tracked in prexu-r8o.
close_reason: Closed

title: events.rs: extract + unit-test mpv-event mapping logic
description: src-tauri/src/player/events.rs has ZERO tests but owns the mpv-event->app-event mapping that prexu-0cs/ta9/mto/anp depend on: EOF detection (eof-reached property -> player://eof), the first-frame one-shot (first PlaybackRestart per file -> player://host-window-ready, gated by hwdec_logged flag), time-pos/buffered throttle gating, and the EndFile reason mapping (0=EOF/2=STOP/3=QUIT/4=ERROR/5=REDIRECT). Extract the DECISION logic into pure fns (one-shot first-frame gate, should-emit throttle, reason->label, property->event-name routing) — mirror how geometry logic was already extracted — leaving mpv.get_property()/app.emit() as the thin untestable shell. Add #[cfg(test)] unit tests. Acceptance: cargo test green; the first-frame one-shot + EOF + throttle decisions covered.
close_reason: Closed

title: Native player Rust-side testability
description: Increase automated coverage of the native mpv/player Rust code. Current state (verified 2026-05-29): pure logic is already extracted + unit-tested — nearest_corner (8), compute_minimize_inset+DPI (4), initial_host_geometry (3), geometry-throttle state machine (3), popout-state, MinimizeCorner serde. Remaining wins are bounded; the residual (Win32 syscalls, libmpv rendering, transparency compositing, real DPI/monitor geometry, the rendered frame) is inherently visual/OS-integration and stays MANUAL — WebDriver is NO-GO (prexu-g8a.5) and Playwright drives the web build with invoke() stubbed. Team: sonnet for all three tasks; load /core:anti-fabrication,/core:git + rust:testing,tauri:testing,prexu:prexu on start; use claude task-list. DEFERRED — to be worked next session per user.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: CI: wire E2E (playwright install + test:e2e) into mise/CI
description: Add a CI step / mise task that runs npx playwright install then npm run test:e2e (chromium at minimum) so the refreshed suite runs on PRs. Ensure it slots into the project ci flow alongside vitest/tsc/cargo. Acceptance: e2e runs in CI and is required before merge.
notes: Created mise.toml (tasks: tsc/test/cargo-check/e2e; ci depends on all 4; node 22) and .github/workflows/ci.yml (ubuntu, PR->main + push main/feature/**; npm ci, tsc --noEmit, vitest run, cargo check, playwright install chromium, test:e2e). playwright.config.ts webServer auto-starts vite so no manual dev server. Making CI 'required before merge' = GitHub branch-protection setting, user-owned.
close_reason: Closed

title: Spike: tauri-driver feasibility for native player behaviors
description: Timeboxed spike. Investigate whether tauri-driver / WebDriver can drive the real Tauri app on Windows (WebView2) to assert native player STATE (isMinimized, popout, fullscreen flags, geometry persisted) — note it cannot see the mpv surface itself. Report: setup viability on Windows, what's assertable, ROI vs keeping §1-3 manual. Do NOT build a full suite; produce a go/no-go recommendation + minimal proof if viable.
notes: Spike result 2026-05-29: NO-GO (conditional). Tauri v2 WebDriver is supported on Windows (msedgedriver vs WebView2) per v2.tauri.app, but: of 8 manually-tested native behaviors, only 3 surface in the DOM and are WebDriver-assertable — body.player-transparent class (useTransparentWindow.ts), MinimizedPlayer DOM subtree vs full Player (Player.tsx:436), and mini-player.rect in localStorage (mini-rect.ts). All 3 are ALREADY covered by vitest unit tests. The painful behaviors (mpv Win32 surface, window geometry, transparency compositing/desktop-bleed, fullscreen state, DPI) have NO DOM footprint and are fundamentally unassertable by WebDriver. Fragility: msedgedriver must pin to the Evergreen WebView2 runtime or the suite hangs; no headless on Windows; needs a debug --no-bundle build + WebdriverIO harness. ROI: low. Narrow exception: a single IPC-roundtrip smoke test (invoke -> Rust -> event -> DOM reflects) is the only thing tauri-driver would uniquely prove vs vitest's mocked invoke. RECOMMENDATION: keep native player behaviors manual; do not build a tauri-driver suite.
close_reason: Spike complete: NO-GO. Native player behaviors are not WebDriver-assertable (mpv is a Win32 HWND, no DOM); the 3 DOM-assertable signals are already unit-tested; msedgedriver/WebView2 pinning is fragile. Keep §1-3 manual. Full memo in notes.

title: Verify web-mode player chrome renders; add scrub/pill DOM coverage if so
description: Determine whether the web build (IS_NATIVE_PLAYER=false path, HLS.js fallback) renders the player chrome (controls, seek bar, skip buttons) in-browser. If yes, add DOM-level Playwright coverage for the scrub-bar interaction (acc) and skip-pill (6mi) WITHOUT native mpv. If the web path doesn't render player chrome, document that and close as not-applicable. Acceptance: clear yes/no + coverage added or rationale recorded.
notes: VERDICT: web build does NOT render player chrome in E2E. IS_NATIVE_PLAYER=false path renders HTML5 video + PlayerControls/SkipSegmentButton, BUT they are gated behind !player.isLoading (Player.tsx ~573); with no real HLS stream in the web/E2E build, isLoading never clears, so chrome never mounts. Scrub-bar (acc) + skip-pill (6mi) DOM coverage NOT achievable via Playwright. Authoritative coverage = Vitest unit tests (MiniChrome.test.tsx). Closed as not-applicable for E2E.
close_reason: Closed

title: Episode next/prev nav falls back to non-existent /player/:key route (bounces to home)
description: useEpisodeNavigation.ts:57/60 build handleNextEpisode/handlePrevEpisode as navigate('/player/${ratingKey}'), but App.tsx defines only /play/:ratingKey (PlayBridge) — there is no /player/:key route, so the catch-all (App.tsx:224) redirects to '/' (home). Player.tsx uses episodeNav.handleNextEpisode() as the fallback when playNext() returns null (Player.tsx:230). Result: when the queue has no next item, next/prev episode bounces the user to the dashboard instead of playing. prexu-0cs's cross-season queue fix sidesteps this at season boundaries but the broken fallback remains. Fix: navigate('/play/${ratingKey}') or call playerSession.replaceRatingKey(ratingKey). Found during prexu-0cs/ta9 wave-2 work 2026-05-29.
close_reason: Fixed: next/prev fallback navigates to /play/:key (PlayBridge) instead of the non-existent /player/:key. 3 vitest cases + tsc green.

title: Media cards stay blurry — poster never resolves to full image
description: Some media-card posters stay at the blurred placeholder and never load the full thumb, even after scrolling the row into view and back. Observed in detail-page 'More with <actor>' rows (2026-05-29: 'More with Samara Weaving' entire row blurry). Network shows many thumb transcode requests served (disk cache 200) but these cards never swap to sharp. Likely IntersectionObserver lazy-load not firing for these rows, or blurhash/placeholder not replaced on image onload. Fix: audit poster lazy-load + onload swap; ensure off-screen rows resolve when scrolled into view.
close_reason: Fixed: useLazyImage checks img.complete after render to resolve the cache-hit race that skipped the blur->sharp swap. vitest green. Code+unit verified; live cache-timing wants hands-on confirm.

title: Mini scrub bar: grab area too small + erratic seek mapping
description: In mini mode the scrub/seek bar is hard to grab (hit area too small) and dragging produces wildly erratic seeks. Logs 2026-05-29 08:53:19-08:54:01 show '[player:minimize] mini-seek' jumping non-monotonically (e.g. 4.8 -> 1281.8 -> 1336 -> 1390 -> 1337 -> 1297 -> 1271 -> 1395 -> 1416). Pointer-to-time mapping appears to read absolute position over too-wide a region and/or the track hit target is only a few px tall. Fix: widen pointer hit area (padded invisible hitbox), map seek from the track rect only, clamp during drag. Respect data-mini-no-drag so it doesn't fight anchor-drag.
close_reason: Fixed: replaced 4px native range input with rect-based div scrub (padded hit area, clamped seek) in MiniChrome. tsc+vitest green. Visual/interaction fix — hands-on re-test of mini scrub recommended.

title: Dashboard: lag between window resize and content reflow filling new size
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-27. When user resizes the main Tauri window while on the dashboard, the dashboard content (sidebar, shelves, cards) lags behind the new window size by ~1-2 seconds. Visible as a gap between the new window edge and where the dashboard content actually ends.

Same root cause family as prexu-0p3 (player chrome reflow lag). WebView2 with transparent: true does not aggressively repaint CSS reflow on window resize; React needs to be nudged to re-render, OR CSS needs GPU compositing hints to force fresh paint.

Candidate fixes:
- ResizeObserver on document.documentElement triggers a setRenderTick / cheap re-render
- window.addEventListener('resize', forceRender) at AppLayout level
- Emit Tauri 'window://resized' event from Rust WindowEvent::Resized, React subscribes and dispatches a state tick
- transform: translateZ(0) + will-change: transform on AppLayout root to force the content onto its own compositor layer that re-flows independently
- Tauri 'window::on_resize' callback inside Rust to forceful WebView2 invalidate

Start with the simplest: window resize listener on the React side that triggers a state update. If insufficient, escalate to ResizeObserver or compositor layer hints.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Implemented: AppLayout adds rAF-throttled window resize listener that triggers setResizeTick state update. Forces React commit per frame during resize, which forces WebView2 repaint. Verify drag-resize on dashboard now snaps content to new size without 1-2s lag.
close_reason: Dashboard reflow lag fixed per user 2026-05-27 ('much better'). Layout read + scrollLeft paint commit on rAF tick. Minor residual lag on aggressive quick-resize accepted as good enough.

title: mini-player: drop resize-handle IPC throttle 50ms -> 33ms (30Hz)
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-27 during the prexu-my6 revert post-mortem. The mini-player resize handle (src/components/player/MiniChrome.tsx) currently throttles onUpdateMiniRect IPC to 50ms (20Hz) during the resize-handle drag (RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS constant). The 50ms floor was inherited from the same '60Hz hard-freezes mpv' rationale as the window-drag sync_geometry throttle in src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs.

The mini-resize path DOES rebuild the mpv vo D3D11 swapchain on every set_geometry, so we can't go to 60Hz. But 30Hz (33ms) is untested and likely fine - the comment specifically called out 60Hz as the freeze threshold.

Acceptance:
- RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS dropped to 33ms (or measure-confirm a safe value)
- Manual smoke: resize the mini player aggressively, video tracks corner growth/shrink smoothly without mpv stalls
- If 33ms still freezes, file follow-up with measured upper bound

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: RESIZE_IPC_THROTTLE_MS 50 -> 33ms in MiniChrome.tsx. Matches sync_geometry floor proven safe in window-drag path. Smoke test on next tauri dev session.

title: [enhancement] mini-player: show current time / remaining time under scrub bar to match full player (prexu-a6z.4)
description:
**Observed during A6Z.6 testing:**
Mini-player chrome has scrub bar, ±10s skip, play/pause, restore, close buttons all functional. But NO timestamps anywhere — user reasonably expects at least current playback time visible, ideally matching the full-player convention of:
- Current time on the left of the scrub bar
- Remaining time (or end time) on the right

**Context:**
- prexu-a6z.4 added the scrub bar + ±10s skip to mini chrome but the spec did not include time labels
- prexu-b956ef08a4 commit added these affordances; this enhancement extends them
- Full player (PlayerControls.tsx) shows current/remaining time formatted as MM:SS or HH:MM:SS
- Mini chrome (MiniChrome.tsx) does not — bar is bare

**Suggested implementation:**
- Reuse the same time-formatting helper used by full PlayerControls (likely in utils/time.ts or similar)
- Add two small text spans flanking the scrub bar:
  - Left: current time (formatted)
  - Right: remaining time with leading '-' (or end time, pick one matching full player)
- Use the same color + font-size tokens as the full controls so they scale down naturally in the smaller mini frame
- Keep them out of the drag region so clicks don't toggle play/pause

**UX edge cases:**
- Very small mini sizes (360x200 default) — may need to hide the timestamps if width < threshold to avoid wrapping
- Live streams / no-duration content — fall back to current time only, hide remaining

**Out of scope:**
- Hover-over-scrub-bar tooltip preview (separate enhancement if wanted)
- Buffered-region visualization (different feature)

**Suggested Skills:**
- react: MiniChrome component changes
- testing: small snapshot test for time-label presence

**Files likely touched:**
- src/components/player/MiniChrome.tsx (or wherever mini chrome lives post-ps6 split)
- existing time-format utility (reused, no duplication)

**Priority rationale:**
P3 — polish/consistency gap, not a bug. Doesn't block any A6Z test from passing structurally; the user can still scrub by visual position. But it's a clear feel-difference from the full player and likely a 30-minute fix.
notes: Fixed in 29afc3ae. MiniChrome scrubWrap now flex-row with current/remaining time spans. Hidden below 300px width. Live (Infinity duration) hides remaining. 5 vitest cases cover render + threshold + clamp + live.
close_reason: Time labels added per spec.

title: [bug] Pop-out → Minimize first-transition flash exposes desktop under transparent WebView (prexu-a6z.5)
description:
**Reproduction (intermittent, NOT consistently reproducible):**
1. Cold-ish state: app warm but minimize mode not yet exercised this session
2. Enter Pop-out (popout window restores to last persisted corner + size)
3. While in Pop-out, click the Minimize button
4. **Observed (first time only)**: pop-out exits to full main window, minimize takes effect putting video in last-known mini corner (upper-left in this case) — but the rest of the WebView is transparent/clear for a few seconds, exposing the desktop behind it (user saw Discord stream through)
5. **After the dashboard paints**, the transparent area fills with the route UI as expected
6. **Subsequent transitions** in the same session: clean, no flash

**Distinguished from prexu-mto:**
- prexu-mto: clear flash on Resume-from-Beginning when starting playback (initial player mount)
- this: clear flash on a popout-to-minimize transition (mode swap, player already mounted)

Different code path but same underlying class of bug: body.player-transparent is on, AND the WebView's other route content has not yet painted into the non-mini region. With the WebView transparent + nothing painting in the non-mini region, the Win32 mpv host window's pixels show in the mini region only, and the desktop shows through the surrounding empty area.

**Likely cause:**
On pop-out exit, the underlying route (dashboard, item-detail) was offscreen for the duration of pop-out, possibly unmounted or its compositor layer dropped. When minimize takes over, useTransparentWindow keeps body transparent (mini mode uses transparent body too so video shows through the masked region), but the OTHER region needs route content to paint. That paint is slow because:
- React reconciler must rebuild the dashboard tree
- Vite dev-mode HMR overhead
- Could be cold-cached after the popout window swap reset something

**Likely fix candidates:**
A. Keep the underlying route mounted at all times while player is active (already supposed to be true via PlayerOverlay sitting outside route tree — verify it actually is during popout)
B. Don't toggle body.player-transparent until the underlying route has had at least one paint frame; defer the class flip by 1-2 RAF cycles on mode transitions
C. Paint a navy background under the WebView (the existing index.html body navy is the right idea but may not be applying during the transition — verify the body class state during the gap)

**Repro environment:**
Windows, dev build (npm run tauri dev), warm session. User reports only seen ONCE, could not reproduce in subsequent attempts in same session. May be timing-sensitive on:
- Whether Discord (or any topmost partly-transparent window) is layered behind
- Cache state of dashboard data
- Specific corner targeted by mini mode

**Test plan:**
- Try cold app start, enter playback, enter pop-out, then minimize, observe
- Repeat in DevTools network-throttled mode to widen the window
- Add console logging at body.player-transparent toggle vs route mount/paint to confirm sequence

**Suggested Skills:**
- react: useTransparentWindow + PlayerOverlay lifecycle
- tauri: Win32 window-show timing under mode transitions
- testing: this may benefit from a Playwright timing test once reproducible
notes: Approach for full fix (C in 2026-05-27 perf trio): emit player://host-window-ready from end of player_exit_popout and player_enter_minimize commands AFTER their SetWindowPos completes. Update useTransparentWindow to either (a) re-defer on a new player://host-window-busy event fired BEFORE the transition, or (b) the popout/minimize commands explicitly remove the player-transparent body class via emit, then re-add after host-window-ready. Choose simpler path on implementation. Pre-condition: prexu-aqd's drag-fix landed (so we are on the known-working WS_POPUP architecture).
close_reason: Rust commands player_exit_popout + player_enter_minimize now emit busy/ready around their geometry work. useTransparentWindow listens for both: busy drops body.player-transparent so navy body bg fills WebView while dashboard paints, ready re-arms via 2x rAF + sync layout read (uzk paint-commit trick). 12 hook tests + 1427 suite green.

title: [bug] Pop-out forgets which monitor user dragged it to across exit/re-enter (prexu-a6z.3)
description:
**Reproduction:**
1. Main window on monitor 1, enter popout (snaps to monitor 1 corner)
2. Drag popout to a corner of monitor 2 (secondary display)
3. Exit popout (main window restores to its pre-popout position on monitor 1)
4. Re-enter popout
5. **Expected:** popout opens on monitor 2 (where user last had it)
6. **Actual:** popout opens on monitor 1 (where main window currently lives)

**Root cause:**
src-tauri/src/player/commands/popout.rs:40 `current_work_area` uses MonitorFromWindow on the MAIN window's HWND. After exit, main has been restored to its pre-popout geometry (which is on monitor 1), so re-entry's MonitorFromWindow returns monitor 1's work area. The drag-to-monitor-2 gesture is lost.

The recently-shipped prexu-dhg fix persists the CORNER but not the MONITOR.

**Design considerations:**

This is more nuanced than the corner fix. Three sub-decisions to make:

1. **Monitor identifier stability.** HMONITOR handle values change across reboots and monitor disconnect/reconnect. Need a persistent identifier — likely a tuple of (work_area_rect, MONITORINFOEX::szDevice) recorded at exit, then matched at enter against current EnumDisplayMonitors output. If no match found (monitor disconnected), fall back to main window's current monitor.

2. **Conflict with main-window-restore on exit.** Currently exit_popout restores main to its stashed pre-popout geometry — so main snaps back to monitor 1 even if popout is on monitor 2. Two options:
   - **(A) Decouple:** popout has its own remembered monitor + corner; main ALWAYS restores to original. Re-entry: target popout's remembered monitor regardless of where main is.
   - **(B) Follow main:** don't restore main to its pre-popout position if popout has been dragged to a different monitor; leave main wherever the popout landed. Then re-entry's MonitorFromWindow(main) naturally returns the right monitor.
   - (A) is cleaner architecturally but adds a new persistent field. (B) avoids new state but breaks the contract that exit_popout = full restore.

3. **First-run / no-history fallback.** On first popout entry after a fresh install, no persisted monitor exists — use MonitorFromWindow(main) as today.

**Suggested implementation (option A):**
- Add POPOUT_KEY_MONITOR to popout-player.json storing { device_name, work_area: [x, y, w, h] }
- player_exit_popout: enumerate monitors, find the one MonitorFromWindow(main_now_on_M2) maps to, capture its device_name + work_area, persist
- player_enter_popout: load persisted device_name; EnumDisplayMonitors to find a monitor with matching name; if found use ITS work area, else fall back to MonitorFromWindow(main)
- Keep main-window-restore behavior identical to today (it's a contract worth preserving for the maximize+restore loop)

**Suggested Skills:**
- rust: windows crate EnumDisplayMonitors/GetMonitorInfoExW
- tauri: tauri-plugin-store JSON schema
- testing: unit tests for monitor-match logic + fallback path

**Test plan:**
- Manual: drag popout to monitor 2, exit, re-enter — verify monitor 2
- Manual: drag popout to monitor 2, exit, disconnect monitor 2, re-enter — verify graceful fallback to monitor 1
- Manual: same-DPI multi-monitor (currently confirmed working same-DPI from this session)
- Unit: monitor matcher returns correct monitor by device_name, returns None when name not found
- Coordinate with prexu-mhn (different-DPI risk) — same code path

**Out of scope for this issue:**
- DPI-different multi-monitor (separate bug prexu-mhn)
- Per-monitor remembered corner+size (just monitor here; corner + size are global per dhg fix)
notes:
**Confirmed via live logs 2026-05-24 10:54:** drag traces show user moved popout from secondary monitor work area (1602,-2160,3840x2160) into primary monitor (-2238,-2160,3840x2160), ending at (684,-637). Exit_popout's current_work_area correctly reported primary monitor's work area at exit (post-fix from prexu-dhg now detects corner BottomRight of primary correctly). Re-entry then queried MonitorFromWindow on main_hwnd which had been restored back to secondary monitor, so opened BottomRight of secondary. Logs prove the diagnosis: corner persists, monitor does not.

Recommended fix is option A from original design notes (decouple popout monitor from main window position): add POPOUT_KEY_MONITOR persistence using device name from MONITORINFOEX, match on enter via EnumDisplayMonitors with fallback to MonitorFromWindow(main).
close_reason: Closed

title: [risk] Pop-out: untested behavior across monitors with different DPI scaling (prexu-a6z)
description:
**Status:** RISK / untested — not a confirmed regression yet.

**Context:** A6Z.3 multi-monitor was validated for SAME-DPI monitors (popout works correctly when dragged/extended across two 100%-scale displays). DIFFERENT-DPI scenarios were not tested in this session. Historically (pre-native-player) Prexu has had bugs in this area.

**Specific scenarios to test:**
1. Primary at 100%, secondary at 150% → enter popout on primary, drag onto secondary. Does popout resize to maintain physical size? Or stay at logical pixels?
2. Same setup reversed (enter on 150% display, drag to 100%)
3. Cross-DPI corner-snap on exit: popout dragged from 100% display into a corner of the 150% display — does nearest_corner compute correct work-area coordinates?
4. Multi-monitor with vertical layout (one above the other) at different DPIs

**Relevant code:**
- src-tauri/src/player/commands/popout.rs::current_work_area uses MonitorFromWindow + GetMonitorInfoW which return PHYSICAL pixels. The new nearest_corner helper uses these directly. If the popout window's GetWindowRect (also physical) is being compared correctly within the same monitor's coordinate space, the math should hold across DPIs.
- mpv host window positioning (src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs) uses set_geometry with what Tauri reports — needs to verify Tauri's inner_position/inner_size are normalized vs raw.

**Acceptance:**
- Enter+exit+re-enter popout on each monitor in a heterogeneous-DPI setup, all four scenarios above
- Resize popout while it's on each monitor — verify no growth/shrinkage glitches
- nothing crashes, video stays visible, geometry round-trips cleanly

Filed as backlog because: no concrete repro yet and user does not have a different-DPI setup readily available for testing in this session.
notes: DPI doc-comments merged via ajn commit 4035381ab4. Still OPEN pending USER cross-DPI hands-on: 100/150% primary<->secondary both directions, cross-DPI corner snap, monitor-disconnect fallback.
close_reason: Hands-on tested across mixed-DPI monitors — popout behaves correctly

title: [bug] Brief clear-window flash on Resume-from-Beginning when starting playback (prexu-a6z.1)
description:
**Reproduction:**
1. Open Prexu warm (app already running, dashboard visible)
2. Click a poster (e.g. Alice in Borderland) → ResumePopover opens
3. Click 'Resume from Beginning'
4. **Observed**: brief (<1s) flash of clear/transparent window before video starts
5. **Expected**: solid navy or instant video, no transparent flash

**Distinguished from prexu-vs5 (closed):**
vs5 covered the COLD-START path (window first becoming visible after Tauri shell launch) — the app_ready handshake + 3s safety net + body-only opaque navy in index.html addressed that. This new flash happens WARM, in-session, on the playback-start transition. Different code path.

**Likely cause:**
useTransparentWindow toggles `body.player-transparent` ON the moment Player.tsx mounts — before the mpv host Win32 sibling window has been created/positioned behind the WebView. Sequence:
1. ResumePopover.tsx → playerContext.start() → /play/<rk> navigation
2. Player.tsx mounts → useTransparentWindow effect adds `player-transparent` class
3. body becomes alpha=0, WebView reveals what's behind it
4. mpv HostWindow creation runs async (player_init Tauri command)
5. For the ~one frame between #3 and #4, desktop shows through

**Suggested fix:**
Defer the `player-transparent` body class until AFTER the player_init / first-frame event fires, not at component mount. Either:
- Have useTransparentWindow listen for a 'player:host-window-ready' event from Rust and only flip the class then
- OR keep body opaque during a small init window (~250ms) and only flip if not init by then
- OR have the player_init Tauri command paint the mpv host with a navy clear-color BEFORE returning, so even if the WebView is transparent and the host is there, the user sees navy not desktop

**Suggested Skills:**
- react: useTransparentWindow hook lifecycle
- tauri: player_init command sequencing
- rust: mpv host window creation order

**Repro environment:**
Windows, dev build (npm run tauri dev), warm start, after main window already painted dashboard. Logged sub-1s but visually noticeable.
notes: Fixed in 8060d554. useTransparentWindow now defers body.player-transparent class until player://host-window-ready (emitted from PlaybackRestart) or 250ms safety timeout. Rust event flows from events.rs PlaybackRestart branch. 8 vitest cases cover defer + event + timeout + cleanup paths.
close_reason: Resume-from-Beginning flash fixed by deferred transparency.

title: [cleanup] Strip iteration-history + beads-ticket comments from player code (CLAUDE.md compliance sweep)
description:
CLAUDE.md / core comment-discipline rule: 'Don't reference the current task, fix, or callers ("used by X", "added for the Y flow", "handles the case from issue #123"), since those belong in the PR description and rot as the codebase evolves.'

The native-player branch accumulated a lot of inline beads-ticket references and 'previously this did X' narrative as the developer iterated through hard Win32 transparency bugs. The current state is correct but the comments document HOW we got here, not WHY the current invariant holds.

Examples (non-exhaustive):
- Player.tsx L116 — references ensure_init + pb.subtitleStyle + the load case + prexu-id
- Player.tsx L131-141 — five-issue archaeology of mask-based hiding (prexu-s0f / 4ml / ya6 etc.)
- Player.tsx L226-229 — '7il.4: the native path NOW has a SECOND button…'
- Player.tsx L432-447 — handleExitRef rationale referencing prexu-jk0
- AppLayout.tsx L120-128 — 5-step history of hiding mechanism (kfa → ya6 → cay → 9bk → quq)
- src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs — extensive (prexu-buw), (prexu-hhx), (prexu-7il.2 / 7il.7), (prexu-ifj), (prexu-bm0) annotations
- src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs — same density

A reader six months from now does not care which ticket introduced a behavior — git blame answers that. They care about the structural invariant the code is enforcing NOW.

Proposed:
- Sweep all of src/pages/Player.tsx, src/components/AppLayout.tsx, src/components/player/MiniChrome.tsx, src/contexts/PlayerContext.tsx, src-tauri/src/player/*.rs, src/hooks/player/useNativePlayer.ts
- Delete: all (prexu-NNN) parentheticals, all 'previously…' / 'this was changed because…' narrative, all 'NOW does X' temporal language.
- Keep: structural invariants ('Win32 windows are thread-affine, so SetWindowPos from a non-owner thread blocks indefinitely'), non-obvious WHYs ('flushSync because the Dashboard commit takes ~1s and would leak transparent pixels').
- Target ≥ 200 LOC of comment deletion with zero loss of structural understanding.

Acceptance:
- 0 'prexu-' references in src/ and src-tauri/ comments
- 0 'NOW' / 'previously' / 'used to' / 'we now' in comments
- Spot-check: any retained comment, removed in isolation, would leave a future reader confused about an invariant

This is a single mechanical sweep; do it AFTER the structural extractions (prexu-ps6, prexu-ve9, prexu-m1g) land so we're not stripping comments out of code that's about to move anyway.

## Required Skills
react, rust, prexu, anti-fabrication, documentation
close_reason: Swept 20 player-module files; net -75 LOC of beads-ticket prefixes, iteration-history paragraphs, and 'now does X' temporal language. 5 surviving prexu-NNN references are legitimate: 1 DOM element id ('prexu-subtitle-style'), 1 open-followup pointer (prexu-ii3 in flushSync docblock), 3 inside Rust #[cfg(test)] blocks (test comments excluded from sweep scope). Structural invariants preserved verbatim: Win32 thread-affinity, mpv keep-open EOF behavior, flushSync Dashboard-cascade reason, WebView2 transparent-frame quirk. 1387 tests pass, tsc + cargo check clean.

title: [cleanup] Unify MiniCorner type definitions across TS / Rust / IPC boundaries
description:
The four-corner anchor concept has FOUR independent definitions:

1. TypeScript: MiniCorner union in src/utils/mini-rect.ts
2. Rust enum: MinimizeCorner in src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs
3. Rust string-match: parse_minimize_corner in src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs
4. Re-aliased: PopOutCorner = (same 4 strings) in src/services/player.ts (misnamed — popout uses same set as minimize)

Any future fifth corner OR rename edits four files. A typo in parse_minimize_corner that no test catches silently falls back to bottom-right.

Proposed:
- TS side: keep MiniCorner as the single source of truth, drop the PopOutCorner alias in services/player.ts (re-export MiniCorner instead).
- Add VALID_CORNERS literal tuple already exists in mini-rect.ts — export it for any iteration use.
- Rust side: in mod.rs MinimizeCorner, derive serde::Deserialize with #[serde(rename_all = 'kebab-case')] so the parser is automatic, deleting parse_minimize_corner entirely. Add a unit test that an unknown string fails to deserialize (so we get a hard error at the IPC boundary instead of silent fallback).
- Same enum reused on the popout commands so corner parsing happens once.

Acceptance:
- 1 type definition in TS (MiniCorner), 1 in Rust (MinimizeCorner, serde-derived).
- parse_minimize_corner function deleted.
- services/player.ts PopOutCorner alias deleted; callers import MiniCorner from utils/mini-rect.
- cargo test + npx vitest run clean
- Unknown corner string from frontend → hard 400-style error, not a silent fallback to bottom-right

Small. ~50 LOC removed.

## Required Skills
rust, react, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: MiniCorner is now single-sourced in src/utils/mini-rect.ts (TS) and src-tauri/src/player/mod.rs (Rust, serde-derived kebab-case). Deleted PopOutCorner alias and parse_minimize_corner helper. Popout's persistence layer reads/writes the enum via serde_json (on-disk format unchanged). Unknown corner strings now hard-error at the IPC boundary instead of silently falling back to bottom-right. 10 Rust tests pass (+1 new serde round-trip), 1323 TS tests pass, tsc + cargo check clean.

title: [cleanup] Move saveMiniRect + playerEnterMinimize side-effect out of setMiniRect reducer into useEffect
close_reason: Pure reducer + two useEffects (persist + IPC). All 14 PlayerContext tests + full 1318-test suite pass. StrictMode double-invocation no longer fires duplicate IPC. Side bonus: 30-LOC ticket-archaeology comment block on minimize() compressed to 9 LOC focused on the structural Dashboard-cascade reason (long-term fix lives in prexu-ii3). Commit 7829f5f16.

title: Cold-start: warm libmpv handle at app launch to eliminate 12s hwdec probe
description:
Concrete trace captured 2026-05-23 02:09:12 (fresh-install run after cargo build). prexu-ps1's diagnostic logs landed at info, so we now have the per-stage timing:

- 02:09:12.x: load_url → mpv init → loadfile sent
- 02:09:34: FileLoaded → player://ready (+22s — cold plex.direct TLS handshake + demuxer parses moov)
- 02:09:46: playback-restart, hwdec-current=d3d11va, time-pos starts moving (+12s — hardware decoder probe + first frame decode)
- Total cold→first-frame: 34s

Dominant: network/demuxer (22s). Secondary: hwdec probe (12s).

The 12s gap is what the user sees as 'stuck at 00:00:00' after the loading screen dismisses — FileLoaded is what dismisses the loading screen, but actual playback waits on hwdec.

Proposed first attempt (from prexu-ps1 closure): warm-init. In the Tauri setup hook, spawn a hidden libmpv handle, set hwdec=auto-safe + vo=gpu-next, run a no-op probe (load a tiny built-in test pattern or ), then keep the handle alive. When user clicks Play, the existing ensure_init reuses the warmed handle; hwdec is already chosen and the D3D11 device is initialised. Expected gain: most of the 12s gap.

Network dominator (22s) is harder — TLS handshake + first byte-range from Plex direct cannot be pre-warmed without knowing the URL. Two paths: (a) DNS pre-resolve to plex.direct on app launch (gets you ~100ms of the 22s), (b) keep an idle keep-alive HTTPS connection to the user's PMS so the TLS session is resumable. Both are speculative — instrument first to confirm where in the 22s the time actually goes (TLS handshake vs byte-range latency vs moov parse).

Acceptance: measured cold→first-frame drops below 15s on a fresh-install run, with the timing logs landed in prexu-ps1 used as the canonical attribution method.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
notes: Fixed in 9e6aea71. Background thread spawned from app_ready calls PlayerState.ensure_init - creates host window + libmpv handle with hwdec=auto-safe + vo=gpu-next so the user's first Play skips the ~12s probe cost. Idempotent via existing ensure_init mutex check. No frontend changes.
close_reason: Warmup spawned from app_ready - first Play near-instant after handshake.

title: [task] mini-player: add Skip Intro/Credits + scrub bar (deferred from prexu-jk0)
description:
Deferred scope from prexu-jk0 — autoplay handoff was fixed (see followup-of dep) but the original bug also flagged that MiniChrome lacks visible UI for: (1) Skip Intro button when an intro marker is reachable, (2) Skip Credits / Next Episode button at credits marker, (3) timeline / scrub bar.

  - Add a compact scrub bar along the bottom of the mini region.

  - Hover/activity-visibility chrome shows a small 'Skip Intro' badge when activeSegment is an intro and player is minimized.
  - Similar badge for 'Skip Credits' / 'Next Episode' when activeSegment is credits.
  - Bottom-edge slim scrub bar (no preview thumbnails) that the user can drag to seek.

title: [bug] mini-player DPI scale wrong when moving Tauri window between monitors of different DPI
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-22 by user testing (test 33, image #3). Same DPI: PASS. Different DPI: FAIL. After moving the main window to a monitor with a different DPI scale (e.g., 100% → 125% or vice versa), the mini player ends up at the wrong size or position relative to its anchor corner.

prexu-7a2 (already closed) addressed an earlier DPI alignment fix during initial minimize; this is a separate runtime DPI-change scenario — Win32 fires WM_DPICHANGED when the window moves to a different-DPI monitor and the mpv host's set_geometry needs to recompute against the new scale.

Investigation hints:
- Check if there's a WM_DPICHANGED handler in src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs or main.rs that re-runs sync_geometry with the new scale factor.
- Check if the React side (Player.tsx miniContainer) reads window.devicePixelRatio at the right time. Logical px in miniRect vs physical px in Rust set_geometry — the bridge must rescale on DPI change.
- Tauri's WindowEvent::ScaleFactorChanged is the cross-platform hook for this.

## Required Skills
rust, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
close_reason: Fixed in 21fb85b4b — WM_DPICHANGED handler updates cached scale_factor before sync_geometry; MinimizeState refactored to store logical px; compute_minimize_inset rescales on every placement. 4 Rust unit tests cover scale=1.0, scale=1.25 rescale, left-anchored corners, and clamping.

title: [bug] popout: mpv host stuck out of window after drag, stuck on single frame for several seconds
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 via image #41 / image #42 during pop-out drag testing. After dragging the pop-out window, mpv ended up partially OUTSIDE the popout window's right edge and below it. Video froze on a single frame ('Crap!' subtitle visible) for several seconds, then resumed.

Likely cause: sync_geometry's 50ms throttle dropped a critical move/resize event, leaving the mpv host at a stale screen position relative to the popout. The pending_geometry mechanism might also have a race.

Will be largely fixed by the mpv-as-child-window architectural change (separate bd issue). In the meantime, two quicker mitigations: 1) reduce the throttle from 50ms to ~16ms during active drag, 2) ensure pending_geometry is always applied after each drag completes (not dropped on next event). Either patch is a stopgap until the parenting refactor lands.

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: User confirmed mpv drag + resize is good enough (some minor ghosting acceptable). Commits: f71ee2f08d (move/resize split + SWP_NOSIZE), 8e7bc08be3 (SWP_NOCOPYBITS), 960e02b101 (50→33ms throttle). Close ready.
close_reason: Drag + resize feel good per user verification 2026-05-27. Minor remaining ghosting deferred.

title: [cleanup] make mpv host a child of Tauri main window so drag is free; current sync_geometry throttle visibly lags
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 from pop-out drag testing (image #41). Today the mpv host is a sibling Win32 window of the WebView, repositioned on each Tauri WindowEvent::Resized/Moved via sync_geometry (50ms throttle). During an active drag Windows fires hundreds of move events but most are dropped by the throttle; mpv visibly lags behind the popout window during drag and sometimes ends up partially outside the new window position.

Image #42 shows the symptom: mpv stuck on a single frame for several seconds because a critical sync_geometry call was dropped.

Architectural fix: make mpv host a CHILD of the Tauri main window (not a sibling). Win32 child windows automatically follow their parent's translation — no event-driven sync needed for pure drag. Resize still needs sync_geometry but only fires on actual resize, not on every move.

Transparency compositing should still work: mpv as a child behind the WebView2 (z-order child position), WebView2 still has transparent pixels in the mpv area, OS composites through.

Bigger architectural change than the context split. Needs careful testing on hwnd parenting, mpv's d3d11 swapchain behaviour, fullscreen transitions (prexu-dsh already noted dont resize mpv from host wndproc).

## Required Skills
rust, prexu, tauri, anti-fabrication
notes: Landed in worktree-agent-a659f37a1ae981337 commit 64c3d838. HostWindow now WS_CHILD of main HWND. Coordinates passed parent-client-relative (0,0,w,h). WindowEvent::Moved arm dropped - Win32 moves child for free. apply_host_topmost becomes no-op (children inherit parent topmost). cargo check + cargo test 29/29 + tsc + vitest 1401 all pass. GUI smoke NOT done by agent. Parent will smoke-test in next session.
close_reason: Refactor merged to feature/native-player.

title: [cleanup] split PlayerContext so isMinimized/popout changes do not cascade through Dashboard
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 as the architectural cleanup behind prexu-pa4. Today PlayerContext holds session + play + stop + isMinimized + minimize + restoreFromMinimize + isPopOut in a single memoized value. When any one changes, all consumers re-render including Dashboard's deeply-nested tree which makes the React commit take ~1s on the Home route.

Split into focused contexts:
- SessionContext: session, play, stop, replaceRatingKey, updateSession
- MinimizeContext: isMinimized, minimize, restoreFromMinimize
- (maybe) PopOutContext: pop-out state if needed

After the split, isMinimized changes only invalidate AppLayout + Player.tsx (~5 components vs ~50 today). React commit drops to sub-frame; flushSync becomes unnecessary; transitions feel instant.

~200 line refactor. Best done in its own session.

## Required Skills
react, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Landed inline on feature/native-player as commit d8bd1f9. PlayerSessionContext + PlayerMinimizeContext now distinct - Dashboard consumers (only reading {play}) no longer re-render on minimize toggle. 1401 vitest pass + tsc clean. Identity-stability test added.
close_reason: Refactor merged to feature/native-player.

title: [bug] player chrome layout lags WebView resize after popout exit until auto-hide cycle
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-21 during prexu-70y/prexu-wow testing. After exiting pop-out (or any abrupt window resize), the React chrome (PlayerControls top + bottom bars) stays positioned at the previous-smaller window size for up to 3 seconds even though the Tauri main window and mpv host have both already expanded. The chrome auto-hide timer fires → opacity transition forces a re-paint → mouse-over re-shows the chrome at the correct full size.

Not a state bug — React.isMinimized=false correctly, mpv host is at full WebView client area correctly. The chrome's CSS (position: fixed; inset: 0) simply hasn't re-flowed against the new WebView viewport yet. WebView2 + tao window resize → CSS reflow is lazy on the WebView side.

Cosmetic, briefly-visible, self-correcting. Filed as P3.

Candidate fixes:
- WindowEvent::Resized on Rust emits a player://resize event; Player.tsx subscribes + setState to force re-render.
- ResizeObserver on document.documentElement in Player.tsx triggers a setRenderTick to nudge layout.
- transform: translateZ(0) on the chrome container so it sits on its own compositor layer that re-flows independently.

## Required Skills
react, tauri, prexu, anti-fabrication
notes: Re-verified 2026-05-29 STILL OPEN. 2.3.4 popout-exit chrome still lags until auto-hide. NEW repro: fullscreen-ENTER also shows chrome sized to prior (~mini 959x720) viewport — controls cluster mid-screen instead of spanning full width/height. Same WebView2 lazy-reflow family across all viewport-change triggers (popout-exit, fullscreen-enter). Fix should force chrome reflow on EVERY host geometry change, not only the auto-hide opacity cycle.
close_reason: Closed

title: Dashboard/library: per-item incremental rendering instead of whole-shelf skeletons
description:
Better UX inspired by the official Plex client. Currently App.tsx's pre-fetch in AppRoutes() awaits getRecentlyAddedBySection for each section as a batch via Promise.all-style and only writes to the dashboard cache when the whole batch resolves; the dashboard shelf shows a unified row-level skeleton until all items arrive. The official Plex client renders items incrementally — each tile is its own loading state and pops in as it streams.

Surfaced 2026-05-21 by user during prexu-7il testing. Per-item rendering would noticeably improve perceived speed on first load and slow networks (e.g., remote Plex).

Refactor sketch:
- Each dashboard shelf component owns its own items array (state) and renders tiles individually with per-tile skeletons.
- Backing fetch produces items as they resolve (Promise.allSettled per item OR streaming-style array growth) rather than blocking on the whole batch.
- Consider migrating shelf data to React 19 use() + Suspense boundaries per row for cleaner UX integration.
- Apply same approach to /library/:sectionId LibraryView for scroll-down-before-loaded responsiveness.

Scope: dashboard shelves + LibraryView. Out of scope: detail pages, search.

## Required Skills
react, prexu
notes: Fixed in add9d191. Chose option (a) CSS staggered fade. PosterCard accepts optional index prop and applies animation-delay = min(index, 8) * 30ms inline. Dashboard's Continue Watching / Recent Movies / Recent TV shelves pass index via .map((item, index)). Tiles populate progressively instead of all blinking in at once. LibraryView left for a followup if requested - virtualized grid path is different. 5 new vitest cases on stagger behavior.
close_reason: CSS stagger shipped - data-layer refactor deferred.

title: [bug] popout window can extend past monitor edge on multi-monitor setups
description:
Reported during prexu-j18 testing (2026-05-20). Pop-out on a multi-monitor setup with non-primary monitors at negative coords can land with the bottom edge below the visible work area, putting controls off-screen.

Likely current_work_area + corner_origin clamping doesn't account for the work-area origin being negative, or the user-saved size exceeds the current monitor's work area when they pop out from a different monitor than they last popped out on. Need to clamp the final (x,y,w,h) to be fully inside the current monitor's work area, not just clamp width/height.

Deferred from prexu-7il.1 since it's a separate edge-case, not a blocker.

## Required Skills

title: [bug] mini-player snaps to primary monitor instead of current

Root cause: src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs::primary_work_area() used SystemParametersInfoW(SPI_GETWORKAREA) which always returns the PRIMARY monitor's work area. Fix: replaced with current_work_area(&main) using MonitorFromWindow(hwnd, MONITOR_DEFAULTTONEAREST) + GetMonitorInfoW so the mini window snaps to the work area of whichever monitor the main Tauri window currently lives on.
