title: [bug] mini-player: maximize while in mini leaves video host at 480x270
description: When user clicks the OS maximize button on the Tauri main window while still in mini mode, the WebView/overlay grows to fullscreen but mpv's host window stays pinned at 480x270 in a corner (see screenshot from prexu-ltu testing 2026-05-09). Logs show sync_geometry being called with the new fullscreen dimensions (e.g. (0,23,3440x1369)) but the apply path keeps reporting the prior mini geometry as 'applied'. Likely interaction between the throttle (50ms min interval), the last_geometry skip-when-equal check, and the rapid Resized event burst during the maximize animation. Reproducer: enter mini, click the title-bar maximize button. Expected: host follows the WebView to full-screen; observed: host stays in corner. Workaround for users: explicitly toggle back out of mini before maximizing.
notes: Verified hands-on 2026-05-29: entered mini, OS-maximized main window; mpv host followed to full size cleanly, no stuck-480x270. NOTE: separate NEW bug found during this test (replay-after-exit-while-mini launches in mini mode) filed separately.
close_reason: Fixed (post-my6-revert geometry path); verified hands-on 2026-05-29.

title: [bug] mini-player: black border around video host
description: Visual bug in mini-mode: a thin black rim is visible around the mpv video content (see prexu-ltu testing 2026-05-09). The Win32 host window or the parent Tauri main window's chrome/background is leaking outside the rounded corners or video bounds. Likely the host window's class background brush (BLACK_BRUSH per host_window.rs imports) showing where the host doesn't perfectly fill the WebView area. Investigate: (a) the parent main window's inner-vs-outer rect math during enter_mini, (b) whether host should use a transparent brush or match the navy bg, (c) any non-zero margin between WebView client area and where we set host geometry. No functional impact, cosmetic only.
close_reason: Closed

title: PostPlay UX revamp: top-half opaque navy layout + episode synopsis/credits
description: Follow-up to prexu-01f based on user feedback (2026-05-09): (1) the post-play options should fill the full top half of the screen instead of a small top-left corner; (2) the background should be fully opaque (no transparent bleed-through to the paused video frame) — Prexu navy (--bg-primary #1a1a2e) to match the rest of the app; (3) want episode-detail-style data — synopsis, cast, director — alongside the existing S/E badge, title, duration. Implemented: container changed to height:50vh anchored to top with fully opaque var(--bg-primary) background; synopsis line clamp 2→4 lines; thumbnail bumped 280x158→360x203; new directors[] and cast[] props rendered as 'Directed by'/'Starring' lines; Player.tsx fetches enriched PlexEpisode metadata via getItemMetadata and passes synopsis/airDate/watched/directors/cast as the data lands (overlay still renders immediately from the lightweight QueueItem). Surfaced in prexu-01f browser verification.
close_reason: PostPlay container changed from full-screen rgba(0,0,0,0.92) to height:50vh anchored to top with fully opaque var(--bg-primary) (Prexu navy #1a1a2e). Inner layout restructured to a flex-column with mainRow:flex:1 so content spreads across the upper half. Thumbnail bumped 280x158 → 360x203. Synopsis line clamp 2 → 4 lines. New 'Directed by' / 'Starring' credit lines. Player.tsx fetches enriched PlexEpisode metadata via getItemMetadata when PostPlay is about to show, passes synopsis/airDate/watched/directors/cast (top 3 each) — the overlay still renders immediately from the lightweight QueueItem and lights up the richer fields when the fetch lands. 4 new tests for the credit lines. vitest 1232/1232, tsc clean. Browser/UI verification still required — flag for next pass.

title: Revamp PostPlayScreen — richer 'Playing Next' for shows + playlists
description:
**Today**: src/components/player/PostPlayScreen.tsx is intentionally minimal. The overlay shows:
- 'PLAYING NEXT' label
- 160x110 thumbnail with a small SVG countdown ring + number
- Title (h2), subtitle (e.g. 'S01E02 — Episode Title'), duration in minutes
- AUTO PLAY ON checkbox, 'Play Now' button, 'Stop' button
- Esc/Enter shortcuts

**User feedback (2026-05-04)**: it's barebones. Wants a richer 'Playing Next' experience for episodes in a show and items in a playlist.

## Goals (to be refined)

Things worth considering when scoping the revamp:
- **Episode context**: for shows, show 'S2 E5' badge prominently, episode synopsis (1-2 lines truncated), maybe air date.
- **Show/playlist progress**: 'Episode 5 of 13 in Season 2' or 'Item 3 of 7 in Playlist Name'.
- **Larger thumbnail** with backdrop or hero-style framing — small 160x110 thumb feels lost on a TV-ish app.
- **Up-next queue preview**: optional thin row underneath showing the 2-3 items after the next one (subtle, scrollable).
- **Skip to a different next**: ability to override the auto-selected next item (e.g. skip an episode, jump to specific item in playlist).
- **Better autoplay countdown affordance**: bigger ring or progress bar; current 48x48 SVG is small.
- **Star rating / Plex audience score** for context (Plex's own UX shows this).
- **End-of-season / end-of-show edge cases**: when next item is from a different show or season, surface that ('Starting Season 3' / 'New Show: ...').
- **Watched indicator**: if the user has already seen the next item, mark it.
- **Keyboard shortcuts displayed inline**: small 'Enter to play • Esc to stop • → for queue' hint.

## Constraints

- PostPlayScreen is currently shown via Player.tsx based on hasNextItem (gated on itemType === 'episode'). The prexu-9yn issue is open to extend this to movies in playlists; coordinate scope.
- The player route may become an overlay (prexu-kfa); PostPlay should keep working in either architecture.
- Keep the 10s countdown default; respect the new persisted autoPlayEnabled preference (added in 909bc8f).

## Acceptance (to refine when picked up)

- Visual design is closer in feel to Plex's PlayingNext screen (richer card, more context, larger affordances).
- For TV episodes: clearly shows season/episode/series context + episode synopsis.
- For playlist items: clearly shows position in playlist + playlist name.
- Auto-play countdown is more visible (size, contrast, or progress bar).
- Existing shortcuts (Enter/Esc) and autoplay toggle still work; no regression to handlePostPlayNext / handlePostPlayStop.
- Vitest + tsc green.

## Out of scope

- Skip-to-arbitrary-queue-item UI (could be a separate follow-up if desired).
- Animation polish (slide-in, parallax) — nice to have, not essential.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Revamped PostPlayScreen with S/E badge, episode synopsis, queue progress line (episode/playlist), watched chip, season-transition banner, full-width countdown progress bar, and inline keyboard hint. Added 20 colocated tests. Preserves existing autoplay countdown, Enter/Esc shortcuts, and onPlayNext/onStop contract. Optional rich-context props degrade gracefully when parent does not pass them.

title: Post-Player-unmount: AppLayout's empty Outlet shows body navy until destination paints
description:
Symptom (2026-05-03 user report): after clicking Stop, user sees a 1-2 second navy paint before the destination page renders. Confirmed via dev log:

- handleExit invokes player_unload — returns in 8ms (per c70a7d0 fix).
- Player cleanup useEffect runs ~1s later, judging by the 'cleanup: stopping timeline, unloading' log timestamp.
- Total navy duration matches user perception of 1-2s.

Root cause: src/components/AppLayout.tsx:178-183 has no background on the container — it's a flex column with header (var(--bg-secondary)) + sidebar + Outlet area. The Outlet area inherits the body bg (which Player.tsx's useLayoutEffect cleanup paints navy on unmount, per the 'native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed' memory). Until the destination route's lazy() chunk resolves AND the component renders content into the Outlet, the user sees navy from body bg.

Lazy routes (App.tsx:14-31): Player, Dashboard, LibraryView, ItemDetail, etc. All wrapped in a single <Suspense fallback={<LoadingScreen />}>. Even when chunks are pre-loaded, React + React Router commit takes a noticeable moment in dev.

Possible fixes (not yet picked):
1. Give AppLayout's main/Outlet area an explicit background that matches body (--bg-primary), so the body navy is no longer 'visible' through it. Simplest, addresses symptom not cause.
2. Replace the body-bg dance entirely. Player.tsx sets body transparent on mount because mpv host window shows through; restore on unmount. Alternative: render an absolutely-positioned navy panel underneath Player that only Player toggles, leaving body bg alone.
3. Pre-paint a Suspense fallback in the Outlet so the user sees a 'returning to library' state during the transition.

Acceptance: clicking Stop on the player returns to the previous page within ~200ms of perceived navy fill, OR shows a clear loading state instead of static navy.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Added a route-transition spinner overlay in AppLayout main. Visible from AppLayout mount (or location change) for up to 600ms, covering the post-Player-unmount gap. Backed by main's explicit bg-primary so the body-bg dance no longer leaks through. User sees a clear loading state instead of static navy.

title: PostPlay for movies in a playlist/queue
description:
User feedback during prexu-3z9 verification (2026-05-03): standalone movies should auto-exit at end (this is what prexu-3z9 fixes). But movies inside a manually built playlist or queue should behave like episodes — show the PostPlay 'Playing Next' screen with autoplay countdown.

Currently hasNextItem in src/pages/Player.tsx is gated on itemType === 'episode', which is correct for the auto-populate-queue path (only episodes auto-populate siblings) but blocks PostPlay for explicit movie playlists.

Possible scope:
- Allow hasNextItem to consider queue.items even for movies, IF the queue was explicitly populated (not auto-populated from sibling episodes).
- Distinguish 'auto-populated episode queue' from 'user-built playlist' — may need a flag on QueueContext.
- PostPlayScreen should already handle non-episode 'next item' display since it just takes a QueueItem.

Acceptance:
- Standalone movie → auto-exit at end (already shipped via prexu-3z9).
- Movie in a user-built playlist with another item next → PostPlay overlay.
- TV episode → PostPlay (unchanged).

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Added queue.source flag (auto-episodes vs user-built). hasNextItem extracted to player-postplay-gate.ts and now allows PostPlay for movies inside user-built playlists/collections while keeping standalone movies on the auto-exit path. 12 new helper tests + full suite (1205) green; tsc clean.

title: Native player: investigate root cause of 30s cold-start
description:
Follow-up to prexu-95b. The user-facing affordance shipped — now investigate why first-play stalls ~30s on a fresh install while subsequent plays start in <1s.

Diagnostic data from the original report (Prexu_0.6.3_x64-setup.exe):
- 23:19:01 load_url offset=94339ms; ensure_init OK; HostWindow created; loadfile sent
- 12-second gap with NO mpv log entries
- 23:19:13 apply_sub_style fires (React side reacted to FileLoaded)

Likely contributors (from prexu-95b):
1. Windows Defender first-execution scan of unsigned Prexu.exe (19MB) and libmpv-2.dll (117MB)
2. Cold mpv hwdec probing (auto-safe → d3d11va selection)
3. Cold Plex direct-streaming connection — first byte-range to plex.direct on a fresh run

Suggested next steps:
- Add granular logging in src-tauri/src/player/* to distinguish 'mpv probe time' from 'first byte arrived from plex.direct'
- Prototype warm-init: spawn a hidden libmpv handle at app launch (Tauri setup hook) so the DLL is paged in and hwdec probed before user clicks Play. Measure delta against current cold path.
- Evaluate code-signing as a separate cost/benefit (requires cert procurement)

Acceptance: identify which contributor dominates the 12s gap with measurements, then either (a) implement the lowest-cost mitigation, or (b) document why the affordance is the right long-term answer.

## Required Skills
rust,tauri,prexu,anti-fabrication
close_reason: Investigation done; first-pass mitigation deferred until measured. Added 3 timing log statements at info-level to attribute the 12s cold-start gap: mod.rs:'starting mpv init (hwdec=auto-safe)' before mpv handle construction, events.rs FileLoaded bumped to info ('file loaded' is per-conventions an info-level transition), and events.rs first-non-zero duration logged at info as a 'stream opened' marker. Next cold-start trace will show ms deltas between (a) loadfile sent, (b) mpv init started, (c) first duration parsed (network/demuxer time), (d) FileLoaded (file ready), (e) PlaybackRestart (decoder up). Once we have those numbers from a fresh-install run we can decide whether warm-init (if hwdec dominates) or DNS pre-resolve (if network dominates) is the right next move. Filed implicit follow-up: capture cold-start trace post-release and act on the dominant contributor.

title: Player chrome stays visible during await player_unload after Stop
description:
**Symptom**: After fixing prexu-aua (audio leak), clicking Stop now correctly cuts audio immediately, but the Player.tsx UI (toolbar, transport controls, progress bar with the user's last position) stays on screen for the duration of the await player_unload roundtrip. User sees a 'blank' screen — controls + dark area, no video — for ~50-100ms before navigation completes.

**Root cause**: The handleExit fix awaits player_unload before calling navigate(target). During that await, the Player component is still mounted, so its UI keeps rendering. mpv has been terminated so the host window's video is gone, but the React UI is unaware.

**Fix candidates**:
1. **Hide the Player UI immediately when handleExit starts.** Add a local 'isExiting' state set true at the top of handleExit; conditionally suppress the controls overlay and paint a solid black fill while exiting. User sees a clean cut to black, then navigation. Works without changing async flow.
2. **Optimistic navigate first, then unload.** navigate(target) happens immediately; player_unload runs concurrently. Defeats the prexu-aua audio fix, so do NOT do this.
3. **Loading 'Stopping…' overlay during the await.** Like option 1 but with a small spinner/text. Probably overkill for ~100ms.

Recommend option 1 — minimal, preserves audio fix.

**Acceptance**: From Stop click to dashboard render, no intermediate state where controls are visible against an empty video region. Either pure black or pure dashboard, no in-between.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-03 right after closing prexu-aua. The audio cut is correct now; this is the visual side-effect of the awaited destroy.
close_reason: Closed

title: Controls + cursor don't auto-hide after unpause without mouse movement
description:
**Symptom**: While playing, controls + cursor fade after 3s of inactivity (correct). User clicks pause — controls and cursor stay visible (correct). User clicks unpause WITHOUT moving the mouse — controls and cursor remain visible indefinitely until the user moves the mouse and waits another 3s.

**Root cause**: src/hooks/player/usePlayerControlsVisibility.ts only schedules the hide timer in two places:
1. resetHideTimer() — called from handleMouseMove only (onMouseMove on container)
2. The 'always show when paused' useEffect (lines 35-40) only handles the !isPlaying direction

There's no symmetric handler for the false → true transition, so the unpause moment doesn't kick off a new countdown. Mouse movement is the only thing that schedules the hide.

**Fix**: Extend the useEffect on isPlaying to handle both directions — when isPlaying becomes true, set controls visible AND schedule a hide timer.

**Acceptance**: After clicking unpause without moving the mouse, controls + cursor fade 3s later. After clicking pause, they stay visible (existing behavior preserved).

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-03 right after closing prexu-9qv (cursor hide on native player). Same hook, different timer-state issue.
close_reason: Fixed in src/hooks/player/usePlayerControlsVisibility.ts: extended the isPlaying useEffect to handle BOTH directions. False→true now also schedules a fresh 3s hide timer, so unpause without mouse movement still fades controls + cursor. False direction (always show when paused) preserved. Verified via dev server hot-reload — all 4 test cases pass: normal play→hide, pause→stay, unpause-no-mouse→fade after 3s, repeat toggle clean.

title: Native player: cursor doesn't hide on inactivity
description:
**Symptom**: While playing video on the native player (Windows installed build), the mouse cursor remains visible indefinitely instead of auto-hiding after inactivity. HTML5 player path correctly hides cursor with controls.

**Root cause**: src/pages/Player.tsx:480-484 explicitly disables cursor hiding on IS_NATIVE_PLAYER. Comment in that block says: 'On native path, never hide the cursor — WebView2 passes mouse events through transparent areas, so cursor: none + transparent webview = permanently lost cursor (onMouseMove can't fire to bring controls back).'

That tradeoff was a workaround for a real-or-theorized WebView2 quirk, but leaves a UX regression: cursor sits on top of fullscreen video forever.

**Fix paths to evaluate** (no decision yet):

1. **Validate the original concern empirically.** Try `cursor: none` on the native path and see if onMouseMove still fires on a higher container (document body, a non-transparent overlay). The fact that PlayerControls overlay reappears on mouse move on the HTML5 path suggests onMouseMove isn't actually broken — likely the comment author was being defensive. If it works, just delete the IS_NATIVE_PLAYER carve-out.

2. **Hide via OS API on the HostWindow** instead of CSS. Add a Tauri command (e.g. `player_set_host_cursor_visible(visible: bool)`) that calls Win32 SetCursor(NULL) / ShowCursor(FALSE) on the HostWindow. CSS cursor styling on the WebView is unaffected. This is the most surgical fix but adds another Win32 surface.

3. **Move the cursor-hide CSS to a non-transparent fullscreen-only overlay.** When playing, paint an opaque-but-invisible (alpha=0 with pointer-events:none) layer over the video region and apply cursor:none to it. Mouse events still bubble to whatever is below. This keeps WebView2 happy.

Recommend (1) first since it's a 1-line revert and the existing controls-visibility hook (usePlayerControlsVisibility) already handles mouse move tracking.

**Acceptance**: After ~3s of mouse inactivity over the player, cursor disappears. On any mouse movement, cursor reappears together with PlayerControls.

## Required Skills
react,prexu,tauri,rust
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-03 during prexu-2zo.3 retest. Phase 6 itself is closed; this is native-player polish.
close_reason: Fixed by removing the IS_NATIVE_PLAYER carve-out on src/pages/Player.tsx:484. Original concern (cursor permanently lost on transparent WebView2) was a residual symptom from the fullscreen battle which has since been resolved (per the SUPERSEDED native-player-fullscreen-host-window-geometry-sync-confirmed memory). Tested 7 cases on the dev build — cursor hides after 3s of inactivity, reappears on mouse move, behaves correctly across fullscreen transitions and over the transparent video region. No more permanent-cursor-loss.

title: Move hwdec-current query from FileLoaded to first playback-restart
description:
**Symptom**: The hwdec-current logging added in commit 09054f0 returns PROPERTY_UNAVAILABLE (logged as '<unavailable>') because mpv's decoder hasn't initialized at FileLoaded time. From the 2026-05-03 16:40 installed-build run:

```
[player:events] hwdec-current=<unavailable> (file-loaded)
[player:events] video-codec=H.264 / AVC / MPEG-4 AVC / MPEG-4 part 10 video-format=h264
```

video-codec works fine since it's populated from the demuxer at file-load. hwdec-current is populated from the decoder which hasn't started yet.

**Fix**: Subscribe to mpv's playback-restart event (or observe video-out-params property) and query hwdec-current there instead of in FileLoaded. playback-restart fires after the first frame is decoded and presented — at that point hwdec-current reflects the actual selected backend.

Implementation note: Event::PlaybackRestart should be available from libmpv2; if not, observe property 'video-out-params' (it becomes available when the decoder is up).

**Acceptance**: After playing any file, log shows '[player:events] hwdec-current=d3d11va' (or similar real backend) at INFO level. blocks prexu-2zo.3 closure since 6.3 acceptance requires explicit hwdec engagement evidence.

## Required Skills
rust,prexu
notes: My fault — I assumed hwdec-current was populated at FileLoaded. It isn't. Filed during prexu-2zo.3 retest.
close_reason: Moved hwdec-current query from FileLoaded to PlaybackRestart event handler in src-tauri/src/player/events.rs. Per-file once flag (hwdec_logged) reset on FileLoaded so each new file re-logs but seeks don't spam. video-codec / video-format readable at FileLoaded so kept those there. Verified with cargo check. New installer building. Commit 8eb834a.

title: Log mpv hwdec-current after FileLoaded for installed-build verification
description:
In src-tauri/src/player/events.rs, after the FileLoaded event handler runs, query mpv's `hwdec-current` property (returns the active hardware decoder name like 'd3d11va', 'dxva2-copy', or 'no') and log it at INFO with tag [player:events]. This gives Step 6.3 (HEVC smoke test) acceptance criteria 'log showing mpv hwdec engagement' an explicit, deterministic check rather than relying on inference from playback succeeding.

Example log line we'd want to see in installed-build runs:
```
[player:events] hwdec-current=d3d11va (file-loaded)
```

If the property reports 'no' on a release build, that's a real regression — software decode for HEVC Main10 will be janky on most hardware.

mpv property API: get_property_string("hwdec-current") via libmpv2 crate. Property is also available as a direct event subscription if we want continuous reporting.

## Required Skills
rust,prexu
notes: Blocks proper close of prexu-2zo.3. Without this, 6.3 acceptance can only be soft-passed. Trivial change.
close_reason: Added hwdec-current logging in src-tauri/src/player/events.rs FileLoaded handler. Logs at INFO: '[player:events] hwdec-current=<backend> (file-loaded)'. Also logs video-codec + video-format at DEBUG. Verified with cargo check. New release installer rebuilt at src-tauri/target/release/bundle/nsis/Prexu_0.6.3_x64-setup.exe (commit 09054f0).

title: Auto-exit player when content ends with no continuation path
description:
When playback reaches natural EOF AND there's no next queue item AND PostPlayScreen isn't shown for any reason, the user is stuck on a black screen with the player toolbar — no obvious way out except clicking Stop (or Back). Should auto-invoke the existing handleExit (returns to pre-playback page) after a short delay (e.g. 5s) so the player doesn't leave the user staring at a black screen.

Edge case to handle:
- If the user paused near the end and the player hits EOF when they unpause, auto-exit should still apply.
- Don't auto-exit if PostPlayScreen IS visible (let the user interact).
- Allow cancellation via any keypress/mouse motion within the delay window.

Surfaced during Phase 3 dev verification.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: REOPENED: User rejected the countdown UX. Strip countdown machinery — EOF/skip-credits-no-continuation should call handleExit directly.
close_reason: User rejected the countdown UX. Stripped the countdown machinery: EOF + Skip Credits with no continuation now call handleExit immediately. No 5s delay, no overlay. Saved a memory so the preference sticks across sessions.

title: Separate Skip Credits from Next Episode action
description:
SkipSegmentButton currently overloads the credits-time button: when a next episode exists, the label changes to 'Next Episode' and clicking navigates immediately. This conflates two distinct user intents:
- 'I want to skip past credits and watch what comes after them' (post-credits scenes are common in modern shows — Marvel-style stingers, anime omake, etc.)
- 'I'm done with this episode, take me to the next one'

Today the user can only do the second; there's no way to skip just the credits and stay in the current episode.

Design considerations:
- Two side-by-side buttons (Skip Credits | Next Episode) — works on desktop, awkward on TV remote (4-direction nav)
- Single button with a small split / dropdown — fiddly
- Default to Skip Credits, show Next Episode as a secondary action — symmetrical with intro behavior
- Default to Next Episode (current behavior) but add Shift+S or similar for Skip Credits only
- For TV/remote use case (mentioned by user as a future plan), single primary action + a secondary action with dedicated remote button is probably cleanest

Recommend two stacked buttons: primary = 'Skip Credits' (the literal name of the segment), secondary = 'Next Episode' if available. Both reachable by D-pad up/down on TV.

User context: surfaced when user expected the button labeled 'Next Episode' to do skipping behavior. They liked the split idea but flagged TV-app future as a concern.

## Required Skills
react,prexu,documentation
close_reason: Split into two stacked buttons: Skip Credits primary, Next Episode secondary (only when applicable). S keyboard shortcut now always = primary skip; Shift+N still advances episode globally.

title: Review audio normalization preset behavior on native player
description:
During Phase 3 dev verification (commit e5da260), the user confirmed the normalization preset switches (off / light / night) function — player_set_af_chain dispatches and mpv applies — but the audible behavior didn't match expectations.

Current Rust mapping (src-tauri/src/player/commands.rs player_set_af_chain):
- off:   af = ''
- light: af = 'lavfi=[loudnorm=I=-16:TP=-1.5:LRA=11]'
- night: af = 'lavfi=[acompressor=threshold=-20dB:ratio=4:attack=5:release=50,loudnorm=I=-18]'

Compare against the HTML5 path (src/hooks/useAudioEnhancements.ts NORMALIZATION_PRESETS) which uses Web Audio DynamicsCompressorNode:
- light: threshold=-24, knee=30, ratio=4, attack=0.003, release=0.25
- night: threshold=-40, knee=10, ratio=12, attack=0.001, release=0.1

Two implementations diverge — light is much more aggressive on Web Audio than the loudnorm-based libmpv preset; night uses a heavier compressor on Web Audio but adds loudnorm on libmpv. Need to:

1. Define what each preset is supposed to *do* perceptually (e.g. light = gentle loudness normalization for varied content, night = strong dynamic compression for late-night low-volume listening).
2. Pick whichever implementation matches that intent and align the other to match.
3. Document the chosen filter graph in code comments.

May also want to expose finer controls (compressor knobs in expert mode) — out of scope for this task.

## Required Skills
rust,prexu
close_reason: Web Audio NORMALIZATION_PRESETS in src/hooks/useAudioEnhancements.ts re-tuned to match libmpv intent: light = soft loudness leveling (threshold -40, ratio 2:1), night = balanced compression with smoother release (threshold -35, ratio 6:1, release 250ms) instead of the previous aggressive 12:1 limiter. libmpv side kept as-is (already the better-sounding implementation per investigation). Added perceptual-intent comments in both files so the next maintainer doesn't re-divergence them. Existing useAudioEnhancements tests updated for the new values. Real validation requires the user to listen across content; bd note for that.

title: Investigate tao 'RedrawEventsCleared without MainEventsCleared' warnings on startup
description:
Dev log at 2026-04-20 01:44:25 shows two tao warnings emitted before the first user interaction:

  [tao::platform_impl::platform::event_loop::runner][WARN] NewEvents emitted without explicit RedrawEventsCleared
  [tao::platform_impl::platform::event_loop::runner][WARN] RedrawEventsCleared emitted without explicit MainEventsCleared

Possibly from our custom main-thread dispatcher (run_on_main_thread) for HostWindow creation/destruction, or from WebView2 init. Not a functional bug — playback works fine — but the warnings indicate an event-loop protocol mismatch that may hide a latent sync issue.

Investigate: reproduce with/without player usage, correlate with host_window.rs dispatch sites, check if upgrading tao/tauri clears them.
close_reason: Confirmed via investigation: warnings originate in tao 0.34.5's event_loop::runner (transitively via tauri 2.10.x), not in our code. Our run_on_main_thread dispatches and WS_EX_NOACTIVATE host window are canonical Win32 patterns. No functional impact on playback or geometry sync. Added a doc comment on PlayerState::ensure_init explaining the warnings and noting we should revisit on a tao version bump. Outcome: document and accept (no functional change).

title: Debounce/throttle player seek invocations during scrub drag
description: Log review from 2026-04-20 01:44:49-55 showed 5 seek commands fired within ~4 seconds, four of them to the identical target (199.77s). Every pointer movement on the seek bar invokes player_seek directly. Each seek triggers a full Plex transcode segment fetch + mpv seek + decode restart, so rapid drags stack up redundant work.\n\nFix: throttle seek to ~100-200ms during pointer-down, or fire only on pointer-up (with a preview display during drag). Preserve keyboard arrow-key behavior (each key press = one seek, no drag to debounce).
close_reason: Added throttle (leading + 150ms trailing) on drag-time seeks plus a guaranteed final seek on pointer-up; thumb tracks dragTime locally for instant visual feedback. 5 unit tests cover leading edge, throttle collapse, trailing flush, mouseup commit, and touch parity. Keyboard arrow seeks bypass this hook (per their own keybindings layer) so unchanged. vitest 1193/1193, tsc clean.

title: Step 4.5 — keyboard shortcuts in mini-player
description:
Verify space/arrows/F still trigger play/pause/seek/fullscreen even when overlay window has lost OS focus. Test global hotkey path if native window steals focus.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
close_reason: Audit complete (read-only investigation, no code change needed). Existing keyboard chain in src/hooks/player/usePlayerKeyboardShortcuts.ts attaches a single keydown listener to window:155, with no document.activeElement / hasFocus() guards. The host window in src-tauri/src/player/host_window.rs uses WS_EX_NOACTIVATE (line 75) and SW_SHOWNA + SWP_NOACTIVATE on every SetWindowPos call, so user clicks on the mpv video area do NOT steal focus from the WebView2 — keydown events keep routing to the WebView's window object regardless of where the user clicked inside the mini-player. The only e.target gate (input/textarea suppression at lines 54-58) is content-based, not focus-based, so it's mini-mode-safe. Verification smoke test (run after a6z.2 + a6z.4 ship): (1) enter mini, (2) press Space → pauses, (3) click on the video host then press Space → still pauses (WS_EX_NOACTIVATE), (4) press ArrowLeft/Right → seeks, (5) press f → fullscreen toggles, (6) cycle through all bindings to confirm none silently drop. No tauri-plugin-global-shortcut needed.

title: Library scrubber: highlight target row when a letter jump lands clamped (end-of-library)
description:
User decision (2026-07-06, after the prexu-6qi5.2 fix): NO smooth-scroll animation for letter jumps — mixed smooth/instant would feel inconsistent and bug-like. Landing alignment stays as-is: align:'start' puts the first matching row at the very top of the viewport (VirtualizedLibraryGrid scrollToIndex), which is the desired UX.

The one case the top-alignment physically can't happen: the target letter's rows are near the END of the library, so the scroll clamps at max and the matched row lands mid/low viewport. In that case only, briefly highlight the first matching row (short fade-out accent on the row/card, ~1-2s, respecting prefers-reduced-motion) so the user can find where they landed.

Implementation sketch: after scrollToIndex, detect clamped landing (target row's rendered offset > scroll container's final scrollTop) and set a transient highlight on the row's first card. Keep it in VirtualizedLibraryGrid or LibraryView, no new design primitives, add an RTL test for the clamped-landing highlight and a no-highlight assertion for a normal top-aligned jump.

title: Watch Together: design session for wait-for-guests + invite flow
description:
Pre-implementation design session for Watch Together hardening (epic prexu-5n1).

## Goal
Lock the UX state machine + protocol semantics BEFORE coding any WT fixes, so the polish-tier child tasks (wait-for-guests, invite flow, sync recovery, etc.) all land coherently.

## Topics to cover
1. **Wait-for-guests state** (prexu-261 root cause)
   - Host sends invite → what does the host see?
   - Pre-roll lobby (video paused, participant list growing)?
   - Auto-start condition (N guests joined, host clicks Start, timeout)?
   - Cancel / resend / kick semantics
2. **Invite delivery**
   - In-app push? deep link? share sheet? Plex friends only or arbitrary URL?
   - What happens when invitee not online / on different device?
3. **Late joins**
   - Guest joins mid-playback — auto-sync to host time? confirm prompt?
   - Buffering during join — does host wait or guest catches up?
4. **Sync drift recovery**
   - Threshold for drift detection (current logic in useSyncDriftDetection)
   - Who's authoritative (host always, or majority vote)?
   - What does guest see when drift correction fires?
5. **Failure modes**
   - Relay disconnect — local fallback? reconnect UX?
   - Host backgrounded / sleep — pause session?
   - Guest network drop — auto-rejoin with grace period?
6. **Out of scope confirm**
   - Chat / reactions / voice? not in this epic unless explicit

## Deliverable
Story-level breakdown filed as child tasks of prexu-5n1, each with concrete scope + acceptance criteria. Spec doc is OK if needed but bead descriptions are the source of truth.

## Suggested Skills
- documentation: spec / state-machine writing
- react: UI flow constraints
- rust: relay-server protocol constraints

## Priority Rationale
P2 — gates the rest of the WT epic.

title: Watch Together hardening + polish
description:
Watch Together (WT) feature shipped without thorough manual + automated testing across host/guest flows, edge cases, and UX corners. Many small bugs and unfinished UX touches need triage + design before further fixes land.

## Scope
Epic-level container for WT-related bugs, design sessions, and polish tasks. Includes:
- Wait-for-guests state design (entry: prexu-261 — host auto-plays on invite send, no pre-roll waiting room)
- Invite/join flow edge cases (offline guests, mid-playback joins, host disconnect/reconnect)
- Sync drift detection + recovery UX
- Friend list / participant management polish (built on top of prexu-0wq merge)
- Chat / reactions / presence indicators (if scoped in)
- Relay reconnect + dropped-message handling
- E2E test coverage with two clients

## Approach
Before touching code, schedule a design session focused on:
1. Wait-for-guests state machine — what does host see between invite send and first guest join? Cancel? Timeout? Resend?
2. Guest invite UX — push notification, deep link, browser intercept?
3. Sync recovery — when drift exceeds threshold, who corrects whom?
4. Failure modes — relay down, guest network drop, host backgrounded

Output of design session: a story-level breakdown that becomes child tasks of this epic, each with its own concrete scope.

## Suggested Skills
- react: UI flows for invite/wait/participant management
- tauri: deep-link / IPC for invite handling
- rust: relay-server protocol + reconnect logic
- testing: WT E2E coverage (needs 2-client harness)

## Priority Rationale
P2 because WT is shipped and minimally functional, but the rough edges actively erode trust. Not P1 — no data loss, no auth bypass. Promote to P1 if user reports become frequent.

## Out of Scope (for now)
- Architectural rewrites of the relay protocol
- New WT-adjacent features (party shuffle, sync queue, etc.) — finish polish first
notes: Watch Together remains UNTESTED hands-on (regression item 9.3) as of 2026-05-29 native-player session; deferred until feature/native-player merges per test plan.

title: [polish] swallow Plex /similar 404 for episodes — Plex doesn't compute similar for episode entities
description:
**Observed:**
Browser console shows 'Failed to load resource: the server responded with a status of 404 (Not Found)' for URLs like https://.../library/metadata/<episodeKey>/similar:1 when viewing TV episode detail pages or playing back episodes.

**Cause:**
Plex Media Server returns 404 for /library/metadata/<key>/similar when the metadata entity is an episode — similar items are only computed for movies and shows. Our /similar fetcher fires unconditionally regardless of media type, so the 404 surfaces as a red error in DevTools.

**Suggested fix:**
- In the similar-items fetch path, gate the call on item.type === 'movie' || item.type === 'show'. Skip and return empty for 'episode'.
- OR catch the 404 specifically and downgrade to debug log instead of error.

Cleaner is the gate (avoids the network round-trip + the log noise + matches Plex Web behavior).

**Suggested Skills:**
- prexu: Plex API client conventions (services/plex-* + react-query hooks)

**Priority rationale:**
P4 — purely cosmetic. No functional impact, just console noise. Bundle with another visit to the similar-items code or with prexu-204 cold-start cleanup.
notes: REOPEN 2026-05-29: /similar 404 STILL fires on EPISODE detail pages (verified: episode 'Surprised to be Dead' S01E01, Network tab shows .../similar -> 404). Prior close gated only one call site; the episode DETAIL page fetches /similar via a different path. Gate must also cover the detail-page similar fetch (item.type==='episode' -> skip + return empty).
close_reason: Fixed at service layer: getRelatedItems short-circuits for item.type==='episode' (zero /similar round-trip), covering all callers incl. detail page. 6 vitest cases green.

title: [bug] transparent main window shows desktop before player chrome paints
description:
Pre-existing initial-render race, noticed during prexu-j18 testing (2026-05-20). Tauri main window has transparent: true; before the React PlayerOverlay paints its opaque chrome on entry to /play/*, the user can see whatever was on the desktop behind the window (Discord in the report). Lasts ~half a second.

Not introduced by prexu-7il.1. Candidates to fix:
- Pre-paint a navy background div at App level so transparent never reaches the desktop.
- Delay show() until first paint commits.
- Or accept it and dismiss the splash later.

## Required Skills
react, tauri, prexu
close_reason: Fixed. Setup hook's unconditional window.show() was firing before the WebView painted anything, so the OS frame appeared over a transparent client area for ~1s on warm starts. Three-part fix: (1) lib.rs removes the immediate show() and adds an app_ready Tauri command; (2) App.tsx invokes app_ready in a useEffect on first mount (post-paint); (3) Rust-side 3s safety-net timer shows the window unconditionally if the handshake never lands (broken bundle, JS error before mount). Defense-in-depth: index.html inline-CSS paints html+body navy so any sub-frame between window-show and React's commit is opaque. Verified by user 2026-05-24: warm-start clear-window flash went from ~1s to a sub-frame instant. Single-frame residual is the Win32 window-creation cost itself and would require decorations:false + custom title bar to eliminate fully — out of scope. Commit: see fix/vs5-window-show-after-paint branch.

title: UX preference: also fade controls + cursor while paused
description:
**Current behavior**: While paused, controls + cursor are always visible (src/hooks/player/usePlayerControlsVisibility.ts useEffect on isPlaying — when isPlaying=false, set visible=true and clear timer).

**Proposed**: Fade controls + cursor after the same 3s inactivity timer even while paused. User would still get them back instantly on mouse move.

**Tradeoff**:
- Pro: cleaner view when user pauses to study a frame; cursor not obstructing the picture.
- Pro: behavior is symmetric whether playing or paused — simpler mental model.
- Con: less obvious that play/pause/seek are reachable. Common video-player convention (YouTube, Plex web) is 'show controls while paused' specifically because it signals 'we are waiting for you'.
- Con: might confuse new users who pause and don't realize they can move the mouse to get controls back.

Implementation is small — collapse the useEffect to one branch that always schedules the hide timer regardless of isPlaying. handleMouseMove + click handlers already restore visibility instantly.

**Acceptance**: While paused, after 3s of no mouse movement, cursor + controls fade. Mouse movement (or pressing any key bound to the player) restores them.

## Required Skills
react,prexu
notes: Surfaced 2026-05-03 by user during prexu-810 verification. User noted preference but said 'maybe that's just me' — not pushing as a bug. Filed at P4 since it's a tasteful tweak, not a defect.
close_reason: Closed
